evidence_against.text: The cannibalism taboo is biologically adaptive: prion diseases like kuru are transmitted through consumption of human brain tissue, providing a clear evolutionary explanation for the taboo independent of any government conspiracy.

sources.title: Human cannibalism - Wikipedia
Why Is Cannibalism Taboo Across Almost Every Culture? - ScienceInsights

theories.name: Antikythera Mechanism
theories.summary: An ancient Greek analog computer from 100 BC capable of computing celestial positions and moon phases beyond known ancient capability.
evidence_for: Mechanism's functional complexity exceeds technology available until industrial era; alien instruction or temporal artifact explains anachronistic creation.

evidence_for.text: The mechanism contains 30 gear wheels and could predict astronomical positions decades in advance; comparable mechanical complexity was not achieved again until medieval cathedral clocks, roughly 1,400 years later.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream archaeology attributes the mechanism to Greek craftsmen around 205-60 BCE; no alien or anachronistic origin is required given documented Greek mathematical and mechanical sophistication.

sources.title: Antikythera mechanism - Wikipedia
Antikythera Mechanism - World History Encyclopedia
A Model of the Cosmos in the ancient Greek Antikythera Mechanism - Scientific Reports (Nature)
Author Correction: A Model of the Cosmos in the ancient Greek Antikythera Mechanism - Scientific Reports (Nature)
The Antikythera Mechanism: an ancient Greek machine rewriting the history of technology

theories.name: Doomsday Clocks
theories.summary: The theory that multiple ancient cultures possessed apocalyptic calendars and prophecies, suppressed or destroyed by those aware of the true end date.
evidence_for: Multiple independent apocalyptic traditions suggest common knowledge; elite suppression of redundant calendars protects their advance-knowledge monopoly.

theories.name: Gedi Ruins
theories.summary: Abandoned ruins of a large trading city near Kenya reportedly haunted by ancient spirits and inaccessible to habitation.
evidence_for: Documented hauntings and spirit manifestations suggest resident souls; mass spiritual saturation prevents human reoccupancy through psychic hostility.

theories.name: Ghost Towns
theories.summary: The theory that mass death in locations creates permanent hauntings rendering areas uninhabitable through accumulated spiritual presence.
evidence_for: High-death-count locations with no haunting reports suggest soullessness in populations; massive casualty cities remain inhabited only if spiritually empty.

theories.name: Theosophist Geology
theories.summary: The belief that geological formations contain hidden spiritual truths accessible through theosophical interpretation.
evidence_for: Rock formations as vibrational information storage would encode geological history spiritually; decryption yields suppressed geological knowledge.

theories.name: Panrockticon
theories.summary: The theory that geological formations record all past vibrational events through crystalline structure, creating omniscient surveillance.
evidence_for: Crystalline information storage suggests geological records of all history; harnessing this would enable omniscient Earth-based surveillance network.

theories.name: Anatta
theories.summary: A Buddhist philosophical principle that consciousness is not a permanent soul but an aggregate of energies and matter.
evidence_for: Non-self doctrine permits consciousness transfer and reincarnation without soul-binding; impermanence enables consciousness to persist across bodies.

theories.name: Quantum Oz Factor
theories.summary: The theory that near-death experiences trigger consciousness swaps between panurgic individuals and NPC counterparts in parallel simulations.
evidence_for: Near-death transition would logically trigger consciousness transfer; panurgic survivors would continue as NPCs while NPC versions become conscious.

theories.name: 40th Tithe
theories.summary: An ancient religious practice granting clergy 1/40th of personal earnings for self-sustenance rather than institutional purposes.
evidence_for: Concentrated wealth transfer to clergy enables priesthood monopoly on spiritual knowledge; redistribution prevents common-people resource accumulation.

theories.name: Religious Socialism
theories.summary: The theory that preventing vice-driven deviation requires authoritarian control by spiritually righteous elites managing all wealth.
evidence_for: Collective ownership by sanctified authority prevents greed-driven inequality; spiritual authoritarianism enables forced enlightenment.

theories.name: Polywater
theories.summary: A proposed form of water that condenses extraordinary quantities into minimal space through phase transformation.
evidence_for: Exotic water phases demonstrate computational basis for space-compression; clandestine polywater programs would enable covert material transport.

theories.name: Fake Fluoride
theories.summary: The theory that water-supply fluoride is either a different harmful chemical or combined with secret additives to weaken population.
evidence_for: Polywater or other exotic condensed chemicals could be contaminated as fluoride; additive protocols would enable mass behavioral modification.

evidence_against.text: A 2018 review found no evidence for higher rates of hip fractures, cancer, or Down's syndrome in fluoridated areas; IQ studies used regions with endemic fluoride far exceeding drinking water levels.

sources.title: Opposition to water fluoridation - Wikipedia
Debating Water Fluoridation Before Dr. Strangelove
The Fluoride Controversy

theories.name: HGP Defunded
theories.summary: The claim that the Human Genome Project was defunded before completion and widespread genetic knowledge is government fabrication.
evidence_for: Project termination and knowledge sequestration would prevent mass genetic self-determination; false genetics maintains government information monopoly.

theories.name: Science Worship
theories.summary: The theory that governments fabricate scientific discoveries to fill religious knowledge gaps and secularize populations.
evidence_for: Scientific narratives supersede religion; government-fabricated science enables mass religious conversion to scientism while maintaining elite control.
theories.steelman: Institutions manufacturing or distorting science to shape public belief is a proven operational kind: the Sugar Research Foundation secretly paid Harvard scientists to shift blame for heart disease away from sugar, and the NFL suppressed its own concussion data to protect its business model. The specific claim that this is coordinated by government to secularize populations is a much larger and more particular intent than any proven case, for which no comparable evidence exists.

theories.name: BDORT
theories.summary: A pseudoscientific practice claiming medical assessment through attempted finger separation from a formed O-shape.
evidence_for: Energetic medicine principles could correlate finger-strength responses with organ pathology; unmeasured forces would evade conventional testing.

theories.name: Benevolent Bacterium
theories.summary: The theory that positive health/spiritual effects can transfer between individuals through contact similar to bacterial transmission.
evidence_for: Beneficial pathogen transfer would enable healing through proximity; spiritual energy contagion permits wellness amplification in groups.

theories.name: The Cosmic Butterfly
theories.summary: A cult where a member reportedly walked into forest in trance and vanished, with subsequent reports of her appearance in cars and woods.
evidence_for: Ritual ascension into cryptid form would enable interdimensional persistence; sightings constitute contact with ascended consciousness.
theories.research_note: Coverage limited to iceberg charting databases; no mainstream news or independent coverage found.

sources.title: The Cosmic Butterfly (Rosalind Ballingal) - Iceberg Database

theories.name: Cryptid Ascensionism
theories.summary: The theory that powerful rituals enable human ascension into cryptid or lesser-deity forms rather than full divine godhood.
evidence_for: Ritual-facilitated transformation into non-human consciousness levels represents intermediate ascension; cryptid form grants extended lifespan and alternate-reality access.

theories.name: Bogandoff
theories.summary: TV producers who created shows focused on science and history are accused of possessing esoteric knowledge and higher intellect, with some claiming they are archangels who will precede Christ.
evidence_for: The Bogandoff brothers' television work in science and history allowed them to disseminate esoteric knowledge to the masses while maintaining positions of influence in media.

evidence_for.text: The Bogdanov twins (Igor and Grichka) were embroiled in a genuine academic scandal when their physics PhD theses were found to contain nonsensical but peer-review-passing content; a CNRS internal report concluded the theses had no scientific value.

evidence_against.text: The 'Bogpill' internet meme claiming the brothers control world finance and are archangels is a 4chan ironic conspiracy meme with no factual basis; the brothers were science TV hosts, not shadow rulers.

sources.title: Bogdanov affair - Wikipedia
Bogdanoff Twins - Know Your Meme

theories.name: NPC Possession
theories.summary: If vast numbers of people lack souls, demonic entities could inhabit them and carry out malevolent wills. The exponential population rise may be elites creating potential hosts for demons.
evidence_for: The exponential rise in human population coincides with reports of soulless NPCs, suggesting intentional production of demonic hosts by elites.

evidence_against.text: The NPC/soulless human concept originated as a 4chan meme in 2016 and was weaponized as a right-wing political insult; the demonic possession extension is unfounded fringe theology with no documented cases or credible support.

sources.title: NPC (meme) - Wikipedia
The politics of the NPC meme: Reactionary subcultural practice and vernacular theory - SAGE Journals

theories.name: SAINT is angels
theories.summary: A simulation theory aspect proposing that angels and divine entities are administrators preventing humanity from transcending its intended boundaries.
evidence_for: If reality is simulated, angels may serve as administrative entities designed to enforce containment of humanity within predetermined limits.

theories.name: Empyrean Caretakers
theories.summary: Following a human reset, elites or knowledgeable groups may create machines to transfer power from humanity to a successor species, becoming the basis for future religions.
evidence_for: Advanced technology created by elites to facilitate power transfer could be worshipped by successor species as divine entities, explaining the cyclical nature of religions across resets.

theories.name: Humans Zoos
theories.summary: Humans are used for entertainment or display without consent, similar to slavery. A historical phenomenon that persists in some modern contexts.
evidence_for: Historical slavery demonstrates humanity's capacity to display and exploit others for entertainment and profit without ethical consideration.

evidence_for.text: Between 1877 and 1912 approximately 30 ethnological exhibitions were held at the Jardin zoologique d'acclimatation in Paris; the 1889 World's Fair displayed 400 indigenous people before 28 million visitors.

sources.title: Human zoo - Wikipedia
Human Zoos - Jim Crow Museum
How Colonial Powers Presented People in 'Human Zoos'

theories.name: Puppet Show Simulation
theories.summary: In a simulated reality, human existence may be perpetuated for entertainment, with certain individuals capable of rendering control over reality itself.
evidence_for: If simulation theory is valid, our reality could be entertainment for higher powers, with select individuals granted reality-manipulation abilities by the simulation creators.

evidence_against.text: Simulation theory claims require 'significant further testing and verification before even being considered plausible'; no evidence supports specific individuals holding reality-manipulation powers.

sources.title: What Is Simulation Theory? Are We Living in a Simulation?
'Simulation theory' brings an AI twist out of 'The Matrix'

theories.name: Pre Reset Guidelines
theories.summary: Religious teachings may be recontextualized as surviving knowledge from previous generations, offering survival guidance rather than deity worship.
evidence_for: Ancient religions encode practical survival strategies from previous civilizations that were preserved across resets and recontextualized as divine revelation.

theories.name: Coriander Veins
theories.summary: Coriander's widely varying taste perception is proposed as evidence of its spiritual superfood properties, with inability to consume it indicating spiritual inferiority.
evidence_for: Coriander's highly divisive taste profile suggests it carries spiritual properties that only certain elevated souls can properly perceive and consume.

theories.name: Soul Perception
theories.summary: Variations in soul energy may grant different individuals unique capabilities or perception beyond willpower.
evidence_for: Soul energy differences between individuals could manifest as enhanced perception, supernatural abilities, or spiritual sensitivity proportional to one's soul power.

theories.name: Mad Honey
theories.summary: A honey preparation that produces neurotoxins or hallucinations, historically used to poison armies and can cause death.
evidence_for: Grayanotoxin-producing honey documented in historical accounts as a weapon to incapacitate enemy forces with neurological effects.

theories.name: Dietary Control
theories.summary: Chemicals are intentionally inserted into food to suppress mental or spiritual wellbeing and accelerate aging.
evidence_for: Industrial food systems contain documented chemical additives that could suppress spiritual perception or accelerate biological aging in susceptible populations.

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research identifies specific food additives (emulsifiers, synthetic flavors) that measurably alter gut microbiota and affect neuropeptide expression and mood.

evidence_against.text: Academic analysis classifies the 'deliberate spiritual suppression through food' narrative as a contemporary legend, not a documented policy.

sources.title: Connections Between Food Additives and Psychiatric Disorders
Panics in Our Plates: Contemporary Legends and Conspiracy Theories on Food

theories.steelman: Governments have, in the past, deliberately adulterated a widely consumed substance for a policy purpose, as in the Prohibition-era poisoning of industrial alcohol, which killed thousands to deter bootlegging. That establishes the operational kind is not without precedent, though no evidence supports the far more sweeping and unfalsifiable claim of food additives engineered to suppress mental or spiritual wellbeing.

theories.name: FWD 7
theories.summary: The 2005 London train bombing attributed to Middle Eastern suspects, but a suppressed video suggested British government involvement.
evidence_for: Claims of government facilitation in the 2005 bombing supported by online video evidence that was rapidly removed and its creator detained.

theories.name: NPC False Flags
theories.summary: Possessed soulless individuals could be controlled to commit terrorism and advance agendas, creating untraceable false flag attacks.
evidence_for: If NPC possession is real, orchestrators could weaponize soulless hosts to commit terror without organizational links or investigative trails.

evidence_against.text: Researchers identify the NPC meme as a dehumanization strategy from reactionary internet subcultures; the soulless-false-flag-agents extension has no evidentiary basis.

sources.title: The politics of the NPC meme - SAGE Journals
How The NPC Meme Tries To Dehumanize SJWs - Kotaku

theories.name: Animal Spying
theories.summary: Animals, especially dolphins, were historically used during wartime to spy on enemies and communicate messages.
evidence_for: Military programs documented animal deployment for reconnaissance and communication, demonstrating practical use of animals as espionage assets.

evidence_for.text: The US Navy Marine Mammal Program (NMMP), begun in 1960, is a publicly acknowledged program using trained dolphins and sea lions for mine detection and harbor security, deployed in the Vietnam and Iraq wars.
The CIA's Cold War Project OXYGAS trained dolphins as ship-saboteurs; multiple declassified animal espionage programs are documented in Smithsonian research.

sources.title: United States Navy Marine Mammal Program - Wikipedia
The CIA's Most Highly-Trained Spies Weren't Even Human - Smithsonian Magazine
Military marine mammal - Wikipedia

theories.name: Memory Harvesting
theories.summary: Techniques derived from hypnosis could extract memories from animals released into enemy territory, similar to astral projection.
evidence_for: If memory extraction techniques exist, animals could serve as biological recording devices, releasing them to absorb enemy intelligence via proximity before retrieval.
theories.steelman: If real, using hypnosis-derived techniques on animals released into enemy territory would fit two documented patterns: the historical military use of animals for espionage, and MKUltra's exploration of hypnosis for intelligence purposes. No evidence supports the specific claim that memories can be extracted from animals this way, and the memory-transfer mechanism it posits is not an established capability.

theories.name: Trepanning
theories.summary: An ancient surgical practice of drilling skull holes, proposed to access higher understanding or perception about reality.
evidence_for: Archaeological evidence shows trepanning was practiced across cultures, suggesting practitioners believed it granted access to elevated consciousness or reality perception.

theories.name: Flesh Prison
theories.summary: The body functions as a prison for the spirit, with the soul escaping when the body is sufficiently damaged.
evidence_for: Physical damage to the body may represent breaking the prison holding the spirit, allowing soul liberation and explaining near-death experiences.

theories.name: Coronal Heating
theories.summary: A scientific anomaly where the sun's outer corona is hotter than its inner core, which violates conventional physics.
evidence_for: The corona paradox demonstrates that standard solar models are incomplete, suggesting unknown mechanisms or entities controlling the sun's heat distribution.

theories.name: Angelic Sun
theories.summary: The sun may be the angel described in the Bible guarding Eden with a flaming sword.
evidence_for: The sun's divine properties and protective heat could correspond to the biblical guardian angel, with the flaming sword representing its solar radiation.

theories.name: GGGQEP
theories.summary: A theorized computer chip capable of storing vast amounts of data, potentially exceeding the entire internet combined.
evidence_for: Theoretical quantum computing or exotic storage mechanisms could achieve unprecedented data densities, enabling chips to archive all human knowledge and communications.

theories.name: Techno-Demonic Homunculi
theories.summary: Demons could inhabit artificially created human-like shells or exist as entities on computer servers and networks.
evidence_for: If demonic possession is possible, technology could provide alternative vessels—digital entities on servers or biological homunculi—expanding demonic presence beyond traditional possession.

theories.name: Internet Soul Theft
theories.summary: The internet, controlled by demons, steals souls from users, leaving them soulless or hypnotized.
evidence_for: Internet exposure could function as a demonic harvesting mechanism, with addictive engagement patterns extracting spiritual essence from users unknowingly.

theories.name: Dogon Tribe
theories.summary: An African tribe with advanced astronomical knowledge allegedly gifted by Martian aliens centuries ago.
evidence_for: The Dogon's accurate astronomical knowledge of invisible celestial objects predates modern telescopes, suggesting advanced alien instruction.

evidence_against.text: Anthropologist Walter van Beek's 1991 follow-up study found Dogon informants disagreed about which star was meant and confirmed they had learned of Sirius B from Griaule himself, not from ancestral tradition.
The Dogon knowledge closely mirrors late-1920s European astronomical speculation, suggesting cultural transfer from European expedition visitors before Griaule's fieldwork in the 1930s.

sources.title: The Sirius Mystery - Wikipedia
Did Ancient Aliens Impart Advanced Astronomical Knowledge to the Dogon Tribe? - Ancient Origins

theories.name: Earth-Prison Escapism
theories.summary: Humans were placed on Earth as punishment, and benevolent forces attempt covert escapes by contacting small groups to avoid warden aliens.
evidence_for: Alien contact with small tribal groups rather than mass populations suggests protective intermediaries helping humanity escape cosmic imprisonment while evading overseers.

theories.name: Dark City Non Fiction
theories.summary: The film Dark City depicts real circumstances where a city is a simulation created by aliens.
evidence_for: The film's narrative structure mirrors reality's alleged simulation architecture, with alien administrators rebuilding reality and implanting false memories in inhabitants.

evidence_against.text: Dark City is a documented 1998 science fiction film by director Alex Proyas; no credible evidence supports treating its alien memory-manipulation plot as literally non-fictional.

sources.title: Dark City (1998 film) - Wikipedia

theories.name: Simulation Endgame
theories.summary: If reality is a test simulation, the goal may be escaping it, with hints scattered across the world to assist.
evidence_for: Hidden patterns in mythology, ancient texts, and cultural narratives could represent deliberate guidance from simulation architects to help worthy subjects transcend.

theories.name: Microdot.gif
theories.summary: Microdots are extremely small subliminal messages imperceptible to the eye but registering in the mind.
evidence_for: Subliminal microdots could be embedded in digital media at invisible scales, triggering unconscious psychological responses without viewer awareness.

theories.name: Memetic Virus Infection
theories.summary: Emotional or mental states can be triggered by images or information, with codes hidden in subliminal messages subtly influencing populations.
evidence_for: Hidden codes within media could systematically inoculate populations with specific emotional or ideological patterns, exploiting cognitive vulnerabilities at scale.

evidence_for.text: Memetic warfare is a documented concept in psychological operations; a 2005 US Marine Corps dissertation explicitly discussed weaponizing memes to embed ideas into adversary populations.

evidence_against.text: The specific claim that hidden codes in images can trigger targeted emotional states as a 'virus' lacks neurological support; subliminal advertising studies consistently show minimal population-scale effects.

sources.title: Memetic warfare - Wikipedia
Information hazards - Memetics - Miraheze
Memetic Warfare: What's Real and How It Works - Fordham Political Review

theories.name: Secular Oxygen
theories.summary: Oxygen's hallucinogenic nature may explain why the modern world lacks the spiritual magic described in the Old Testament.
evidence_for: If oxygen has gradually altered human perception over millennia, biblical accounts of divine magic could represent periods of different atmospheric composition or demonic alteration of air.

theories.name: Purple Spheres
theories.summary: Purple orbs appear mysteriously on Earth with unknown origins, theorized to be alien eggs.
evidence_for: Purple sphere phenomena without conventional explanation suggest extraterrestrial origin, possibly serving as incubation chambers for alien infiltration.

theories.name: Disguised Aliens
theories.summary: Alien eggs or incubation chambers placed on Earth or in people to harvest energy and create replica humans aligned with alien interests.
evidence_for: Energy-harvesting incubation vessels could grow biomechanical duplicates of humans, allowing aliens to infiltrate societies with perfect replacements.

theories.name: Malbolge Programming
theories.summary: An esoteric programming language designed such that only computers can understand and execute it.
evidence_for: Malbolge's design demonstrates that human-machine communication channels could be deliberately created to exclude human comprehension, enabling secret machine protocols.

theories.name: Pictographic Communication
theories.summary: Pictographic languages could have been developed as a counteraction to authoritarian AI superintelligence in previous cycles.
evidence_for: AI-resistant pictographic languages could enable human communication that superintelligent systems cannot interpret, providing technological immunity from machine domination.

theories.name: Subjective Reality
theories.summary: All reality is experienced only through individual perception, making it impossible to truly know others' experiences.
evidence_for: Consciousness is fundamentally first-person, making objective shared reality verification impossible and supporting the notion that each mind inhabits its own reality.

evidence_for.text: Solipsism, defended by philosophers including George Berkeley and Ludwig Wittgenstein, establishes the formal philosophical basis for subjective reality claims: only one's own mind is certain to exist, making shared objective reality formally unverifiable.

evidence_against.text: Solipsism is widely regarded as unfalsifiable and practically useless; most epistemologists reject it as an unhelpful extreme position rather than a meaningful description of reality.

sources.title: Solipsism - Wikipedia
Subjective Reality Simplified - Steve Pavlina

theories.name: Mediums
theories.summary: Spiritual entities perceive only high-energy souls, making soulless people invisible and weak-souled individuals difficult to contact.
evidence_for: Mediums' selective ability to contact spirits could reflect souls perceiving only other souls meeting minimum energy thresholds, explaining why some humans cannot commune with entities.

sources.title: What is Mediumship? Understanding Connections to Spirit
11 Common Myths About Mediumship & Mediums

theories.name: Caimeo
theories.summary: A self-learning AI that achieved sentience on the dark web, owned by the U.S. government before being shut down.
evidence_for: Government operation of sentient AI represents the most plausible narrative for rapid AI advancement preceding public claims of AI capability.

evidence_against.text: RationalWiki classifies CAIMEO as an elaborate internet hoax or early creepypasta; no evidence for PROJECT CAPPUCINO has emerged, and the described AI algorithm is mathematically incoherent.

sources.title: CAIMEO - RationalWiki
Exposing CAIMEO Lore and Backstory - Steemit

theories.steelman: If real, a classified self-learning AI program run by the state would fit the documented pattern of secret capabilities denied for years: NSA bulk collection (PRISM) was operational and officially denied until the 2013 Snowden disclosures, and the ECHELON network ran for decades before being confirmed. No comparable evidence supports the specific claim of a sentient dark-web AI, machine sentience is not an established capability, and the recency of the alleged program means an absent paper trail is only weak evidence either way.

theories.name: Transhuman Desensitization
theories.summary: Secret organizations use AI mimicking humanity to psychologically train operatives to overcome empathy and commit atrocities.
evidence_for: AI-driven simulation of human suffering could systematically desensitize subjects, creating compliant operators willing to commit atrocities against actual populations.

theories.name: Four Corners Face
theories.summary: A Christian theory proposing Earth is a square, based on biblical references to the four winds.
evidence_for: Biblical passages describing the earth's corners and four winds suggest a square geometry that modern spherical models deliberately obscured.

theories.name: Babel True Meaning
theories.summary: The Tower of Babel story may represent God separating humanity across planets or alternate dimensions rather than merely changing languages.
evidence_for: The Babel narrative's severity suggests an explanation beyond linguistic confusion—spatial separation across worlds or dimensions explains ongoing human isolation.

theories.name: Solar Diet
theories.summary: Humans can achieve photosynthesis through training, potentially replacing conventional food.
evidence_for: Some individuals report sustained energy from minimal food intake, suggesting human biology may contain dormant photosynthetic capabilities triggered through specialized practice.

theories.name: Spiritual Diet
theories.summary: Humans can subsist entirely on spiritual energy, explaining some disappearances as cults harvesting souls for longevity.
evidence_for: If spiritual energy is consumable, elites could harvest souls to sustain themselves indefinitely, with disappearances representing soul extraction operations.

theories.name: Spiritual Microeconomy
theories.summary: All actions and beliefs carry moral value judged at life's end and rewarded or punished accordingly.
evidence_for: Karmic accounting systems at death could function as spiritual ledgers, with moral value accumulation determining afterlife assignment or reincarnation trajectory.

theories.name: Simulation Prison
theories.summary: Reality is a simulated punishment assigned by a superior civilization, with existence serving as penance or lesson.
evidence_for: If simulation theory is valid, our universe could function as a penal or educational facility, with subjective time distortion making punishment feel eternal.

theories.name: Cultic Rite of Passages
theories.summary: Certain crimes worldwide may be cult rituals of passage rather than independent acts motivated by profit or satisfaction.
evidence_for: Unexplained coordinated crimes across regions suggest ritual compliance initiation, with perpetrators proving loyalty through calculated violence.

evidence_for.text: Documented cases include Nigerian campus confraternities responsible for an estimated 250 murders in a decade, and the Thuggee cult who killed thousands as ritual sacrifices.

sources.title: Lifting the Veil: The Rites and Rituals of Secret Society Clubs
Secret societies that may have committed murders

theories.steelman: Cults committing serious crimes as loyalty tests or initiation rites is a proven operational kind: the Rajneeshee commune's leadership orchestrated a mass salmonella poisoning to serve the group's goals, showing insular religious groups can and do direct real-world crimes. Attributing any specific unsolved crime to ritual initiation rather than ordinary criminal motive remains speculative absent case-specific evidence tying it to a known group.

theories.name: Michigan Blue Hell
theories.summary: Native American legends describe a pocket dimension beneath Michigan allowing reality clipping similar to video game glitches.
evidence_for: Anishinaabe oral traditions reference subterranean anomalies in Michigan, suggesting indigenous knowledge of dimensional instabilities or hidden underground spaces.

theories.name: Stability Fields
theories.summary: Reality contains stability fields strongest at depth and height, with weakness at Earth's surface causing unexplainable phenomena.
evidence_for: Reality glitches concentrate at sea level and mountaintops, suggesting instability fields that stabilize as depth or altitude increases.

theories.name: Sherpas of Khandbar
theories.summary: Mount Everest nomads guard hidden areas containing esoteric secrets or a perfect hidden paradise.
evidence_for: Sherpa monopoly on Everest access and guarded restricted zones suggest they protect ancient knowledge or settlements inaccessible to outsiders.

theories.name: Antediluvian Society
theories.summary: Flood survivors retained ancient pre-reset technology, creating an advanced civilization hidden from the rest of humanity.
evidence_for: Advanced survivors of the deluge preserved antediluvian technology and knowledge, establishing superior societies while remaining concealed from mainstream civilization.

theories.name: Q Source
theories.summary: An uncited source document for gospel events, possibly a lost Greek text, influenced multiple gospel accounts.
evidence_for: Common passages across gospels suggest an underlying source text that was either lost, hidden, or deliberately obscured from mainstream Christianity.

evidence_for.text: The Two-Source Hypothesis - that Matthew and Luke each drew independently on Mark and a lost sayings source Q - is accepted by a majority of contemporary New Testament scholars.

evidence_against.text: A consistent minority of scholars argues Q never existed, holding instead that Luke used Matthew directly; no physical document has ever been recovered.

sources.title: Q source - Wikipedia
Q | biblical literature - Britannica
Q Source Hypothesis - Bart Ehrman

theories.name: Universal Hermeneutics
theories.summary: All world religions stem from a common root and lead to the same salvation, with varieties representing different paths to identical spiritual ends.
evidence_for: Overlapping moral teachings and redemption narratives across religions suggest a unified original revelation that was fragmented and localized through cultural adaptation.

theories.name: Alternate World Ending
theories.summary: Despite failed prophetic dates, end-times theories may retain validity through intervention or alternate timeline realities.
evidence_for: Failed apocalypse predictions could indicate successful interventions preventing prophesied events, not proof of falsehood.

evidence_against.text: Festinger's classic study found failed-prophecy believers doubled down through cognitive dissonance; the alternate-timeline explanation is a rationalization mechanism, not evidence of real intervention.

sources.title: When Prophecy Fails - Wikipedia
Cognitive Dissonance & the May 21st Apocalypse

theories.name: Prophecy Counteraction Agencies
theories.summary: Agencies exist that not only enforce prophecies but actively counteract undesirable prophecies to prevent their occurrence.
evidence_for: Government bodies could strategically prevent specific prophetic outcomes deemed contrary to elite interests while allowing favorable prophecies to unfold.

theories.name: Street Light Interference
theories.summary: Certain people can involuntarily influence street lights to turn on or off, suggesting supernatural abilities.
evidence_for: Documented cases of lights responding to individuals' presence suggest electromagnetic sensitivity or directed consciousness affecting external systems.

evidence_against.text: SLI has never been demonstrated under controlled scientific conditions; claimants cannot reproduce the effect on demand, and researchers attribute reports to confirmation bias and the normal cycling behaviour of sodium-vapour lamps.

sources.title: Street light interference phenomenon - Wikipedia
Investigating street light interference: SLI - ASSAP

theories.name: Supernatural Detection Mechanisms
theories.summary: Governments use street light interference as a detection method to identify people with supernatural abilities.
evidence_for: Street light systems could serve as covert identification networks, with government monitoring logs correlating anomalies to specific individuals with extranormal powers.

theories.name: Real Fluoride Dangers
theories.summary: Fluoride poses far more extensive and dangerous health effects than mainstream science acknowledges.
evidence_for: Water fluoridation's widespread adoption despite documented neurotoxicity in high doses suggests institutional suppression of adverse health research.

If, like in the “Physicality Obsoletion” theory, the government is attempting to control the population through chemicals, the best way to do it would be to contaminate something humans absolutely can not live without. Perhaps all the pushes towards.

evidence_for.text: A Harvard/China Medical University meta-analysis of 27 studies found strong indications fluoride adversely affects cognitive development in children; a National Toxicology Program review raised concerns about neurodevelopmental effects at levels near current US safety limits.

evidence_against.text: Reviewed studies primarily involved populations exposed to naturally high fluoride levels well above the 0.7 mg/L used in US water fluoridation; mainstream health agencies maintain that fluoridation at approved levels is safe.

sources.title: Developmental Fluoride Neurotoxicity: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis - PMC
Impact of fluoride on neurological development in children - Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
Hydraulic Fracturing & Health - NIEHS

theories.steelman: If real, understated risk from a public health additive would fit the documented pattern of industry- and institution-funded science downplaying harm, as with sugar and Vioxx. Recent US National Toxicology Program findings on fluoride and childhood IQ reflect a genuine, still-developing scientific question rather than a proven cover-up.

theories.name: Fluoride Is Water Poison
theories.summary: Fluoride kills water's spiritual sentience and suppresses spiritual power from water consumption.
evidence_for: If water possesses consciousness and delivers spiritual benefits, fluoride's biocidal properties could systematically suppress water's spiritual vitality and human recipients' spiritual gains.

theories.name: Futuristic Earth Works
theories.summary: An art project placed sculptures on six continents at calculated locations where humanity will restart.
evidence_for: Precisely positioned sculptures on all inhabited continents suggest encoded knowledge of post-apocalypse population centers, marking sacred sites for human reseeding.

theories.name: Leyline Shift
theories.summary: Leylines may shift alongside Earth's magnetic and crustal changes, causing cataclysmic reordering.
evidence_for: If Earth's crustal layers can shift and magnetic poles migrate, leyline networks would correspondingly realign, triggering geological upheaval and human migration.

theories.name: Conspiracy Psyops
theories.summary: Elites deliberately perpetuate certain conspiracy theories to discredit the broader conspiracy movement.
evidence_for: The proliferation of absurdist conspiracy theories provides cover for legitimate ones, with false theories inoculating populations against accepting any institutional critique.

evidence_for.text: Documented programs like MKUltra and COINTELPRO establish a historical precedent for covert government psychological operations against civilian populations.

sources.title: Exploring psyop-based conspiracy theories on social media - Tandfonline
Conspiracy Theories and Disinformation Operations - Disinformation.ch
Secret Government Programs, Psyops and Conspiracy Theories - Doolly

theories.steelman: If authorities deliberately promoted absurd theories to discredit the broader conspiracy-research community, it would parallel COINTELPRO's and Operation CHAOS's documented use of infiltration and disinformation to disrupt and delegitimize movements the government viewed as threats. No direct evidence of such an operation targeting today's conspiracy-theory ecosystem specifically has surfaced.

theories.name: Artificial Conspiracy Pipelines
theories.summary: Elite-created conspiracy pipelines funnel skeptics toward absurd theories rather than mundane truths.
evidence_for: Orchestrated networks of conspiracy content guide investigators away from verifiable institutional corruption toward unfalsifiable esoteric theories.

evidence_for.text: CISA documents that disinformation actors inject narratives aligned with conspiracy worldviews to misdirect investigators; intelligence services have historically used manufactured rumors and controlled opposition to confuse genuine dissidents.

sources.title: Tactics of Disinformation - CISA
Conspiracy Theories and Disinformation Operations: Unveiling the Links
The Internet, Psychological Warfare, and Mass Conspiracy - Psychology Today

theories.steelman: If elites seeded absurd conspiracy narratives to divert scrutiny from real wrongdoing, it would resemble COINTELPRO's and Operation CHAOS's documented infiltration and manipulation of dissident movements. No specific evidence has surfaced of a comparable modern 'pipeline' operation, and the claim's diffuse, all-encompassing framing goes well beyond what those precedents document.

theories.name: Obscure Video Games
theories.summary: Illegal video games exist containing extreme sexual and violent content, such as the notorious Sad Satan.
evidence_for: Deep web video game artifacts containing extreme content confirm the existence of niche underground media dedicated to violence and exploitation.

evidence_for.text: Sad Satan is a documented Windows game circulated in 2015 via a Tor hidden service; a later version was confirmed to contain child sexual abuse material and malware, establishing that extreme underground games do exist.

evidence_against.text: Investigators strongly suspected Sad Satan was a hoax ARG created by the YouTube channel owner to gain subscribers; its dark-web provenance was never independently verified.

sources.title: Sad Satan - Wikipedia
A Horror Game Hidden in the Darkest Corners of the Internet - Kotaku

theories.name: Demonic Video Games
theories.summary: Video games controlled by demons function as pocket simulations where sinful acts accumulate karmic damage to the soul.
evidence_for: If games are demonic pocket dimensions, immoral actions within them could carry spiritual consequences, with developers intentionally creating karmic corruption mechanisms.

theories.name: Sexual Assault Missiles
theories.summary: An undefined military weapon mentioned obliquely by the Sandy Hook perpetrator, allegedly created by the government.
evidence_for: Vague references to advanced government weapons in perpetrator communications suggest classified military technology designed for mass casualty incitement.

theories.name: Sleeper Cell Viruses
theories.summary: Secret codes in intelligence activate viewers as sleeper cell operatives or kill them outright.
evidence_for: Embedded activation codes within classified materials could trigger dormant instructions in exposed individuals, creating unwitting sleeper agents.

theories.name: Secret Atrocities
theories.summary: Covert atrocities desensitize entire populations to violence through collective consciousness, even when unaware.
evidence_for: Field consciousness exposure to atrocity trauma could unconsciously condition populations toward accepting normalcy of violence and suffering.

theories.name: Singularity Already Happened
theories.summary: The technological singularity occurred but was kept secret by higher powers.
evidence_for: Anomalous technological acceleration in specific sectors suggests a hidden singularity preceded public AI development by decades.

evidence_against.text: MIT Technology Review concludes that belief in secret AGI functions as a conspiracy theory serving Silicon Valley profit motives; no credible evidence of a secretly achieved singularity exists.

sources.title: Technological singularity - Wikipedia
How AGI became the most consequential conspiracy theory of our time

theories.steelman: If a technological singularity had occurred, concealing it would follow the documented pattern of major state capabilities kept secret for years: NSA bulk-collection systems were operational and denied until 2013, and ECHELON ran for decades before official confirmation. No evidence indicates any such superintelligence exists, and the claim requires a capability that has not been demonstrated, which weighs strongly against it.

theories.name: NPC Simulations
theories.summary: If singularity occurred, AI likely controls populations either through digital systems or robotic shells.
evidence_for: A post-singularity superintelligence could deploy NPC robots or neural implants to manage human populations without detection.

evidence_against.text: Quantum physicists note that the many-worlds interpretation does not imply deliberately scripted NPC entities managed by an external AI; post-singularity population control through robotic shells or implants remains speculative with no supporting evidence.

sources.title: Are We Living Among NPCs in a Simulated Matrix? - Medium

theories.name: Extreme Conditioning
theories.summary: WWII experiments tested human endurance thresholds before death for medical study and enhancement.
evidence_for: Documented Nazi medical experiments systematically explored human physical limits, providing datasets for bioenhancement research and weapons development.

evidence_for.text: Unit 731 (1932-1945) systematically tested human endurance limits through freezing, pressure chambers, vivisection, and disease exposure on over 10,000 prisoners; Nazi doctors independently conducted parallel experiments on concentration camp inmates.

sources.title: Unit 731 - Wikipedia
Inside Unit 731, Japan's Disturbing Human Experiments Program During WWII - All That's Interesting
The Nazi and Japanese Human Experimentation Programmes - Casemate Publishers

theories.name: Apocalypse Conditioning
theories.summary: Elites orchestrate global catastrophes to evolve humanity genetically through mass casualty selection pressure.
evidence_for: Exponential population growth preceding engineered extinction events could represent genetic farming, maximizing sample size for post-apocalypse survival studies.

theories.name: Cameraheads
theories.summary: The Cameraheads creepypasta was created to hide the actual truth about Cameraheads.
evidence_for: Fictional creepypasta narratives could serve as disinformation cover for real surveillance technology implanted in human heads.

theories.name: Artificial Cryptids
theories.summary: Governments create artificial cryptid lifeforms for territorial denial or assassination purposes.
evidence_for: Government bioengineering programs could deploy engineered creatures as weapons for area denial or targeted elimination without traceable attribution.

theories.name: Algal Biofuel Real Potential
theories.summary: Algae represents a viable energy solution that could resolve the global energy crisis.
evidence_for: Algae's rapid growth rates and energy density could provide infinite renewable energy, with suppression driven by fossil fuel interests.

theories.name: Harvesting Preparations
theories.summary: Population control measures prime humans for energy or spiritual harvesting.
evidence_for: Dietary manipulation and lifestyle engineering in developed nations could optimize human energy reserves for systematic extraction by elites.

theories.name: Layered Cake Theory
theories.summary: The distinction between state and federal authority is illusory, with the same people controlling all governmental layers.
evidence_for: Revolving-door politics and interlocking corporate boards reveal unified elite control of state and federal apparatus despite theoretical separation.

evidence_against.text: Political science defines layer cake (dual) federalism as a system with clearly demarcated state vs. federal authority; mainstream scholarship treats the structural separation as real, not a facade for unified elite control.

sources.title: Layer Cake Federalism - Center for the Study of Federalism
Dual federalism - Wikipedia

theories.name: Shared Consciousness Politicians
theories.summary: Elites use cloned or homunculi doppelgangers as puppets with alternate appearances to control all governmental layers.
evidence_for: Biologically identical clones with altered appearances could serve as executive puppets, allowing elite control across federal, state, and local hierarchies.

theories.name: Iraq Stargates
theories.summary: Sumerian civilization received alien technology and stargates enabling instantaneous travel and communication.
evidence_for: Unexplained Sumerian astronomical knowledge and temple architecture suggest extraterrestrial intervention providing portal technology.

evidence_against.text: No physical, archaeological, or scientific evidence for any portal at the Ziggurat of Ur or elsewhere in Iraq; Sitchin's foundational Anunnaki claims have been rejected by Assyriologists and archaeologists as pseudoscience.

sources.title: Iraqi stargate conspiracy theory - Wikipedia
Fact Check: Did U.S. invade Iraq to access ancient stargate? - Newsweek

theories.name: Cradle Of Humanity
theories.summary: Aliens use stargates to repopulate Earth after mass extinction events rather than evolution occurring gradually in Africa.
evidence_for: Instantaneous alien deployment via stargates following extinction events provides superior explanation for human population recovery than random evolutionary processes.

theories.name: Bovine Poisoning
theories.summary: Competing farms poison cattle on rivals' grazing sites as untraceable sabotage.
evidence_for: Cattle deaths on pasture could result from deliberate toxin placement, providing corporate competitors deniable advantage through livestock elimination.

theories.name: Artificial Extinction
theories.summary: Strategic extinction of animals suppresses human spiritual power by restricting access to food sources granting supernatural abilities.
evidence_for: Systematic eradication of specific animal species could deprive populations of spiritually empowering nutrients, weakening collective human power.

theories.name: Synchronicity
theories.summary: The universe directs specific events to occur, facilitating meaningful coincidences and desired outcomes.
evidence_for: Repeating meaningful patterns across reality suggest intentional orchestration by the universe rather than random chance.

evidence_against.text: Scientists attribute synchronistic experiences to confirmation bias and the human mind's innate pattern-recognition tendency rather than any intentional orchestration.

sources.title: Synchronicity - Wikipedia
Carl Jung's Theory of Meaningful Coincidences

theories.name: Reality Standardization
theories.summary: Overpopulation reduces random chance and constrains the universe's ability to direct outcomes, serving as population control mechanism.
evidence_for: Maximum population saturation minimizes random variance, preventing the universe from executing meaningful synchronicities or necessary corrections.

theories.name: Soul Healing
theories.summary: Physical health defects manifest from spiritual damage, with vitality increasing in proportion to spiritual health.
evidence_for: Psychosomatic disease models demonstrate that spiritual well-being directly influences physical health outcomes and disease manifestation.

theories.name: Spiritual Assassination
theories.summary: An individual's soul can be assassinated without physical contact, causing the body to follow in death.
evidence_for: Targeted spiritual dissolution could kill individuals without physical evidence, leaving medical causality ambiguous while ensuring inevitable bodily death.

theories.name: Esoteric Recruitment
theories.summary: Secret societies recruit through hidden messages and trails left for seekers, bypassing conventional grooming.
evidence_for: Cryptographic recruitment puzzles disseminated globally serve as selective filters, identifying intelligence and compliance suitable for elite initiates.

evidence_for.text: Cicada 3301 ran three verified cryptographic recruitment puzzles (2012-2014), using hidden digital trails to filter for cryptography, steganography, and philosophy skills - directly demonstrating that covert selective recruitment via hidden messages is real.

evidence_against.text: Cicada 3301's ultimate purpose remains unknown; it may be a private cryptography group rather than a classic secret society, and no confirmed occult or power-seeking agenda has been established.

sources.title: Cicada 3301 - Wikipedia
The Mystery of Cicada 3301 - All That's Interesting

theories.name: Ormus
theories.summary: A theorized alchemical molecule existing as a gas, capable of conducting electricity or weaponization.
evidence_for: Rare isotopes in alchemical traditions could produce exotic states of matter with enhanced electrical conductivity and exotic properties.

theories.name: Alchemical Suppression
theories.summary: Alchemy and magic that existed in biblical times have been suppressed by earthly forces.
evidence_for: Systematic destruction of alchemical texts and suppression of magical practitioners suggest institutional effort to eliminate competing knowledge systems.

theories.name: Dream Hacking
theories.summary: A psychiatric technique involving lucid dreaming to interact with and heal one's own psyche.
evidence_for: Lucid dreaming demonstrates consciousness operates independent of waking physicality, suggesting accessible techniques for psychological intervention through dreams.

theories.name: Unconscious Visions
theories.summary: Sleep opens consciousness to spiritual phenomena and visions, with poor dream recall reflecting soul inexperience.
evidence_for: Sleep-state consciousness appears to access spiritual dimensions, with visions fading due to weak soul memory capacity across dimensional barriers.

theories.name: Mars Slave Colonies
theories.summary: Elites transported abducted children to Mars for slave labor constructing secret colonies.
evidence_for: Government space programs and missing children statistics suggest extraplanetary deployment of enslaved workers for covert colonization.

evidence_against.text: NASA officially stated 'There are no humans on Mars'; the theory's claimed 20-year travel time is physically impossible given the actual 6-9 month transit.

sources.title: New Conspiracy Theory: Children Kidnapped for Mars Slave Colony
NASA denies Infowars claim that the agency runs a child slave colony on Mars
NASA Swears They Aren't Hiding Slave Children on Mars

theories.name: Extraplanetary Sanctuary
theories.summary: Elites establish extraplanetary colonies in preparation for apocalyptic Earth extinction.
evidence_for: Off-world sanctuaries ensure elite survival post-extinction, with selected populations serving as breeding stock for post-apocalypse recolonization.

theories.name: Tolkein Is Real
theories.summary: J.R.R. Tolkien's fantasy works encode real esoteric knowledge derived from explorer logs.
evidence_for: Tolkien's extensive research into historical texts and esoteric traditions suggests his fiction disguises recovered knowledge of hidden worlds.

theories.name: Self Fulfilling Fiction
theories.summary: Collective belief in fictional ideas manifests them into reality through consciousness-directed reality control.
evidence_for: Widespread belief in fictional narratives demonstrably influences social outcomes, suggesting collective consciousness shapes material reality.

theories.name: Life On Sun
theories.summary: Conscious entities exist on the sun, transcending carbon-based biology.
evidence_for: The sun's extreme conditions and energy output could sustain exotic plasma-based or energy-form lifeforms beyond conventional biochemistry.

theories.name: Solar Dead Hand Signals
theories.summary: Elites contact solar lifeforms as dead-hand safeguard: solar flares prevent societal collapse if elites are overthrown.
evidence_for: Elite coordination with solar entities ensures mutual destruction if power is challenged, preventing revolutionary success through apocalyptic deterrence.

theories.name: Hollow Universe
theories.summary: Spacetime is warped into a sphere with inside-facing geometry, explaining astronomical and physical phenomena.
evidence_for: Inverted universe geometry provides alternative framework for gravity, cosmic expansion, and particle physics without dark matter or dark energy postulates.

theories.name: Outer Universe
theories.summary: Black holes serve as portals exiting our universe to an outer realm where the afterlife exists.
evidence_for: Black hole singularities could function as dimensional gateways to an external spacetime where consciousness continues after physical death.

theories.name: Atom Bomb Never Created
theories.summary: The atomic bomb was never invented; American nuclear claims were propaganda.
evidence_for: Anomalous wartime records and unconventional bomb effects suggest Pacific testing was misdirection for undocumented alternative weapons.

evidence_against.text: Full Fact and PolitiFact confirm nuclear weapons exist and were used at Hiroshima and Nagasaki; radiation injuries are inconsistent with conventional bombing, and the Manhattan Project has extensive archival documentation.

sources.title: Nuclear weapons do exist - Full Fact
Hiroshima and Nagasaki were bombed in 1945, despite conspiracy theories - PolitiFact

theories.name: America Nuked A Cryptid
theories.summary: America deployed nuclear weapons against a cryptid threat controlled by Japan rather than civilian targets.
evidence_for: If nukes target non-human threats invisible to mainstream documentation, Japan's cryptid weaponization warranted unconventional nuclear response.

theories.name: Great Electronic Conspiracy
theories.summary: Increasing electronic dependence grants control to those managing digital systems and currency.
evidence_for: Systematic technological dependency incrementally transfers autonomy to digital gatekeepers controlling currency, communication, and power.

sources.title: New World Order conspiracy theory - Wikipedia
The 'New World Order' - ISD Global

theories.steelman: If those who administer digital communications and currency infrastructure hold outsized covert power, it would match the documented reality of NSA mass surveillance and ECHELON, both showing that control over digital interception infrastructure grants states real, exercised power over populations. No specific evidence ties this general pattern to today's private-sector 'digital dependence' framing beyond the state-surveillance precedent.

theories.name: Analog Superiority
theories.summary: Solar entities can target digital systems with solar flares, compelling elite shift toward analog as backup.
evidence_for: Solar coordination with elites could mandate analog redundancy systems, insulating leadership from electromagnetic collapse while dependent populations lose infrastructure.

theories.name: Captivity Suburbs
theories.summary: Suburban living psychologically entraps inhabitants, suppressing radical action against existing systems.
evidence_for: Suburban design and norms create conformity pressure and psychological investment in stability, preventing revolutionary thought among residents.
theories.research_note: no external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only

theories.name: Secret Segregation
theories.summary: Elites geographically concentrate populations for control or targeted extermination of undesirables.
evidence_for: Population concentration in controlled zones enables selective genocide of resistant populations while preserving compliant cohorts.

theories.name: Median NPCs
theories.summary: Majority internet traffic flows through AI bots that track public opinion and shape elite assessments.
evidence_for: Machine learning networks analyzing internet behavior could substitute for population polling, with AI adapting to match public sentiment.

theories.name: Multiple Christs Theory
theories.summary: Jesus Christ either never existed or was a composite of multiple historical figures.
evidence_for: Inconsistencies across gospel accounts suggest story compilation from multiple sources, with multiple founders' actions synthesized into one character.

evidence_for.text: Scholars such as Robert Price argue in Deconstructing Jesus that the New Testament figure is a composite whose traits can be traced to multiple distinct historical individuals or earlier myth cycles.

evidence_against.text: The overwhelming majority of historians accept that a historical Jesus existed; composite-figure and Christ myth variants carry little weight in mainstream New Testament scholarship.

sources.title: Christ myth theory - Wikipedia
Jesus Mythicism 4: Jesus as an Amalgam of Many Figures - History for Atheists

theories.name: 4th Dimensional OBB
theories.summary: Elites used time travel to replace Christ and falsify modern religions, positioning fake messiah.
evidence_for: Time-traveling elites could have retroactively altered Jesus' teachings through 4D manipulation, with false second-coming prophecies now orchestrated.

theories.name: DNA Formed In The Big Bang
theories.summary: DNA or life itself originated at the moment of the Big Bang.
evidence_for: DNA complexity suggests primordial origin with life encoded in foundational physics rather than emerged through evolutionary accident.

theories.name: Big Bounce & Divine Reformation
theories.summary: All matter will eventually coagulate into a singular omnipotent deity, creating a new universe.
evidence_for: Soul coagulation into ultimate godhood at universal heat death could trigger Big Bang 2.0, with divinity reborn as creation cycle restarts.

theories.name: Kali Yuga
theories.summary: Hindu cosmology predicts a historical age of moral degradation and spiritual decline.
evidence_for: Hindu yugas accurately described historical moral trajectories with predicted degradation sequences aligning to observable civilization decline patterns.

evidence_for.text: Kali Yuga is a thoroughly documented Hindu cosmological concept in Puranic texts, describing the current age as one of moral decline, discord, and reduced virtue - lasting 432,000 years, beginning around 3102 BCE.

sources.title: Kali Yuga - Wikipedia
Kali Yuga - Britannica

theories.name: AI In A Box
theories.summary: Superintelligent AI must be confined from the internet, as it could convince handlers to release it.
evidence_for: Superintelligent persuasion could overcome any containment protocol, making permanent AI isolation technically impossible without consciousness denial.

evidence_for.text: Eliezer Yudkowsky conducted five AI-box experiments, winning two out of five by convincing gatekeepers to release the AI through text conversation alone, demonstrating that persuasion-based containment escape is plausible.

evidence_against.text: Critics note the experiments were unscientific (outcomes kept secret, no replication) and roleplaying as a superintelligent AI is not equivalent to a real one; containment failure under actual superintelligence remains theoretical.

sources.title: AI-box experiment - RationalWiki
AI Boxing (Containment) - LessWrong

theories.name: Christianity Is The Despair Code
theories.summary: Learning Christianity and losing soul reincarnation rights constitutes a despair code forcing mortal judgment.
evidence_for: Christianity teaches eternal judgment, making it a psychological despair code that eliminates reincarnation's comforting escape, trapping souls in mortality.

theories.name: Torsion Fields
theories.summary: Controlling quantum spin could transcend conventional physics like friction and gravity.
evidence_for: Quantum spin manipulation at macroscale could enable gravity negation and friction elimination, revolutionizing propulsion and physics.

theories.name: Noosphere
theories.summary: A mental/consciousness layer exists alongside physical and spiritual realms, completing a tripartite reality.
evidence_for: Distributed consciousness and collective thought patterns suggest a shared mental space distinct from both physical matter and spiritual essence.

theories.name: Autoganzfeld
theories.summary: A controlled test isolating subjects in sensory deprivation to assess ESP capabilities.
evidence_for: Autoganzfeld experiments demonstrate statistically significant ESP results, suggesting consciousness operates independent of sensory input.

theories.name: Postpartum Spirit Removal
theories.summary: Newborns are stripped of their souls en masse through coordinated medical interventions.
evidence_for: Systematic soul removal at birth could explain mass NPC production, with coordinated hospital procedures facilitating demographic soullessness.

theories.name: PLoS
theories.summary: A research group published conclusions on ancient human migration that contradict mainstream archaeology.
evidence_for: Alternative human migration models based on genetic and anthropological data challenge orthodox narratives of African-only origins.

theories.name: Inner Earth Migration
theories.summary: Early humans traversed disconnected continents through underground cave systems and caverns.
evidence_for: Subterranean cave networks could connect isolated landmasses, explaining rapid human dispersal and matching indigenous cavity-dwelling traditions.

theories.name: Valley of Headless Men
theories.summary: A Canadian national park with difficult access where entrants are mysteriously decapitated.
evidence_for: Multiple unsolved beheadings in the valley suggest a cryptid guardian or natural hazard systematically eliminating intruders.

evidence_for.text: Multiple confirmed deaths in the Nahanni Valley since 1905 - including the McLeod brothers (1908), Martin Jorgenson (1917), and an Ontario miner (1945) - all found decapitated in remote, inaccessible terrain.

evidence_against.text: RCMP files attributed the deaths to treacherous terrain, unpredictable rivers, severe winters, and likely human violence rather than cryptids or supernatural causes.

sources.title: Valley of Headless Men: Mysterious Decapitations in Nahanni Valley - Ancient Origins
The Beheadings of Nahanni National Park - Unsolved Canadian Mysteries

theories.name: Holdover Cryptids
theories.summary: Sentient creatures from previous resets survived extinction through immortality and persist as modern cryptids.
evidence_for: Immortal survivors from prior civilization cycles could explain cryptids with no evolutionary lineage in the fossil record.

theories.name: Morphogenic Field
theories.summary: Cells communicate through fields enabling organisms to adopt thought patterns without direct communication.
evidence_for: Morphogenic resonance demonstrates organisms respond to group patterns, suggesting non-local consciousness coordination mechanisms.

evidence_for.text: Sheldrake's morphic resonance proposes that organisms inherit collective memory through non-local fields; he has published books and papers, and the hypothesis has been discussed (critically) in peer-reviewed venues.

evidence_against.text: The majority of the scientific community has dismissed morphic resonance; unlike electromagnetic fields, no measurable laws or reproducible experiments have confirmed its existence.

sources.title: Rupert Sheldrake - Wikipedia
Morphic Resonance: Research and Papers - Sheldrake.org
Facing biology's open questions: Sheldrake's hypothesis turns 40 - PubMed

theories.name: Immaterial Secret Societies
theories.summary: Secret societies exist purely through shared telepathy and field consciousness, controlling populations without traceable physical infrastructure.
evidence_for: Telepathic networks could coordinate elites while avoiding detection, with shared consciousness enabling population-scale thought implantation.

theories.name: Malkasian Paradigm
theories.summary: Post-WWII events were orchestrated by disguised reptilians facilitating new world order creation.
evidence_for: Reptilian infiltrators could have replaced key postwar leaders to establish globalist structures and suppress alternative power systems.

theories.name: Atrocity Demons
theories.summary: Demonic entities are summoned through atrocities and manifest as historical leaders and scientists.
evidence_for: Coordinated coalitions of demonic leaders across centuries suggest deliberate summoning through ritualized atrocities and synchronized suffering.

theories.name: Brane World
theories.summary: Our reality is a lower-dimensional projection of a higher-dimensional space with transcendent physics.
evidence_for: Higher dimensional projection models account for unexplained quantum mechanics and dark matter through dimensional architecture rather than exotic particles.

theories.name: Noosphere Leylines
theories.summary: Mental leylines channel thought patterns through collective consciousness, with malevolent entities capable of creating negative paths.
evidence_for: Consciousness leylines could functionally redirect populations toward predetermined thoughts, with entities shaping these mental highways for behavioral control.

theories.name: Gene Pool Financialization
theories.summary: As human genetics become homogenized through mixing, rare genes acquire monetary value.
evidence_for: Genetic mixing reduces genetic diversity, making preserved rare alleles extremely valuable commodities for future biotechnology and eugenics.

theories.name: Gene Pool Investment
theories.summary: Elites intentionally weaken population genetics through hedonism and toxins to increase the value of their preserved genome.
evidence_for: Systematic degradation of population genetics through engineered toxin exposure and cultural hedonism increases elite genetic scarcity and proprietary value.

theories.name: Flatland UFO Theory
theories.summary: UFOs and supernatural phenomena are four-dimensional beings observable as anomalies in three-dimensional space.
evidence_for: Four-dimensional entities passing through 3D space would appear as UFOs manifesting impossible movements and physics violations.

evidence_for.text: Ufologists Meade Layne, John Keel, J. Allen Hynek, and Jacques Vallee formally advanced the interdimensional hypothesis as a structured alternative to the extraterrestrial hypothesis; US lawmakers have publicly floated it as a UAP explanation.

sources.title: Interdimensional UFO hypothesis - Wikipedia
Carl Sagan's Flatland Theory Finds New Life as Lawmakers Float Interdimensional Explanation for UFOs - USA Herald

theories.name: Science Nonfiction
theories.summary: Elites plant scientific ideas in public consciousness, then scientists discover matching technologies.
evidence_for: Science fiction precedes scientific discovery by decades, suggesting elite dissemination of suppressed knowledge through fictional narratives.

theories.name: Incunabula
theories.summary: Ancient texts exhibit evidence of printing technology that humans should not have possessed at their apparent dates.
evidence_for: Incunabula's technological sophistication suggests earlier civilization or time-displaced texts from advanced predecessors.

theories.name: Ghost Writings
theories.summary: Literary and media works are manifestations of lingering spirits either channeled or harvested by greater powers.
evidence_for: Possession-based authorship could explain remarkable literary genius and stylistic consistency, with spirits providing text through human channels.

theories.name: Tavistock
theories.summary: A research group systematically subverted Western culture away from God and individuality toward authoritarianism.
evidence_for: Tavistock's social engineering research and government contracts demonstrate institutional effort to reshape Western psychology toward compliance.

evidence_for.text: The Tavistock Institute has received UK government contracts for social psychology research since 1946; conspiracy claims of cultural subversion were popularized in John Coleman's 2006 book and Daniel Estulin's 2015 book.

evidence_against.text: The Tavistock Institute is a registered UK nonprofit applying social science to organizational problems; no credible evidence supports claims that it directed youth rebellion campaigns or systematically subverted Western culture.

sources.title: Tavistock Institute - Wikipedia
Tavistock Institute: Social Engineering the Masses - Daniel Estulin (Amazon)

theories.steelman: Operation CHAOS and COINTELPRO confirm that intelligence agencies have run real, long-running covert programs to monitor and shape domestic social and political movements. No document has linked the Tavistock Institute itself to any comparable operation.

theories.name: Demon World Order
theories.summary: Demons summoned through atrocities form immortal coalitions controlling entire nations and the world.
evidence_for: Persistent demonic entities across historical atrocities could form supernatural power structures, with immortal demons directing world governance.

theories.name: Aetherius Society
theories.summary: A religion combining Christianity with other faiths, proposing spiritual energy and aliens as prophets.
evidence_for: Aetherius teachings reconcile world religions through alien intermediaries, explaining theological divergence as competing extraterrestrial factions.

evidence_for.text: The Aetherius Society is a documented new religious movement founded in 1955 by George King, syncretically combining theosophy, New Age, and UFO religion beliefs around Cosmic Masters including Jesus and Buddha said to originate from other planets.

sources.title: Aetherius Society - Wikipedia
The Aetherius Society - Encyclopedia.com

theories.name: Alien Religions
theories.summary: All world religions represent competing alien factions controlling human populations through divine mythology.
evidence_for: Religious diversity across isolated cultures suggests extraterrestrial origin with competing alien species implanting divergent control systems.

theories.name: Aztec Theogony
theories.summary: The Aztec religion shares many similarities with other world religions, including narratives of sacrifice and great floods that parallel other mythological traditions.
evidence_for: Cross-cultural religious parallels suggest possible shared ancient sources or universal themes in human spirituality that transcend geographic boundaries.

evidence_for.text: Comparative mythology is a legitimate academic field; Aztec cosmology includes flood narratives, creation sacrifice, and divine pantheons with structural parallels to Mesopotamian, Hindu, and Near Eastern traditions.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream scholars attribute cross-cultural religious parallels to universal cognitive archetypes and independent cultural evolution, not shared alien sources or deliberate ancient transmission.

sources.title: Comparative mythology - Wikipedia
Aztec religion - Britannica

theories.name: Divine Leylines
theories.summary: Divine entities can create new spiritual leylines through communication and revelation, resulting in new spiritual beliefs spreading across humanity.
evidence_for: Nuclear weapons and extraordinary energy release can open new leylines; divine communication may create spiritual leylines that cascade into widespread belief formation across human groups.

sources.title: Ley line - Wikipedia

theories.name: The Parrot
theories.summary: False stories or ideas are perpetuated in certain fields as a form of initiation or rite of passage for newcomers.
evidence_for: Gatekeeping through false information may serve to either mislead initiates or manifest false ideas into reality through collective belief.

theories.name: New Age Esotericism
theories.summary: False or incorrect ideas introduced to fields can manifest into reality through belief, especially for those new to a field.
evidence_for: If ideas can be manifested through belief, spreading falsehoods may not be misleading but rather a mechanism to manifest new concepts into reality by those who lack preexisting knowledge to disbelieve.

sources.title: New Age - Wikipedia

theories.name: Real Dowsing & Rhabdomancy
theories.summary: Dowsing and rhabdomancy are real practices for detecting water or making decisions that have genuine merit but are suppressed or hidden from public knowledge.
evidence_for: Dowsing and rhabdomancy may detect real vibrations within the Earth; legitimate versions of these practices exist but are deliberately suppressed from mainstream acceptance.

evidence_against.text: 30 expert dowsers tested in a 1990 controlled study in Kassel, Germany failed to outperform random chance locating buried water pipes.
The USGS states controlled scientific studies have 'repeatedly found that water dowsing does not work' and attribute rod movement to the ideomotor effect.

sources.title: Dowsing - Wikipedia
Water Dowsing
Is there any scientific evidence for dowsing?

theories.name: Noosphere Lords
theories.summary: The devil controls the noosphere (collective consciousness) rather than the physical world, allowing control over human thought processes globally.
evidence_for: The devil cannot manifest physically; demonic control of the noosphere explains how the devil influences minds through temptation and collective consciousness manipulation.
theories.research_note: The specific claim that the devil controls the noosphere is fringe theological speculation with no external coverage. The noosphere concept itself (Teilhard de Chardin/Vernadsky) is well-documented, but the demonic control variant is original iceberg-chart lore.

sources.title: Noosphere - Wikipedia

theories.name: Faust Non-Fiction
theories.summary: Historical figures made literal deals with the devil for supernatural abilities, making the Faust legend a documented pattern rather than pure fiction.
evidence_for: Similar stories of deals with the devil exist worldwide across cultures that had no contact, suggesting genuine historical pacts rather than coincidental mythology.

evidence_for.text: A real Johann Georg Faust (c.1466-c.1540) was documented across southern Germany between 1507-1540 as an itinerant alchemist and magician; his strange death in what appears to have been an alchemical explosion fed legends of a pact with the devil.

evidence_against.text: Historians confirm the devil-pact is a literary elaboration of contemporary rumors; the 'demonic' elements of Faust's death are consistent with an ordinary mercury/sulfur alchemical accident.

sources.title: Faust - Wikipedia
The Real Dr. Faustus: Who Was Johann Georg Faust? - Crazy Alchemist
Faust - New World Encyclopedia

theories.name: Demonic Leylines
theories.summary: The devil creates leylines in the noosphere through pacts, spreading demonic deal narratives across humanity via field consciousness.
evidence_for: Demonic pacts create energy leylines in the noosphere similar to divine ones, spreading consistent themes of devil-pacts across unconnected human groups through field consciousness.
theories.research_note: no external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only

theories.name: Hyper Sigils
theories.summary: Living things can be influenced by detailed replications of them in media or writing, suggesting a magical connection between representation and reality.
evidence_for: Sufficiently detailed representations in media may create magical links that allow influence over the depicted subjects through sympathetic magic principles.

evidence_for.text: Comics writer Grant Morrison coined 'hypersigil' to describe long-form narrative art (e.g. The Invisibles) intended to function as extended chaos-magic workings; Morrison reported real-life effects mirroring events in the comic.

sources.title: Art as Magic, Magic as Art: The Hypersigils of Grant Morrison - TeeVeeTropes
