Pop Magic! - Pastelegram

theories.name: Empty Internet To Empty Reality
theories.summary: The internet was created to profile and copy personalities, then replace those people with clones under government control, making most online users already replaced NPCs.
evidence_for: Internet profiling serves mass replacement of humans with clones; the 'Empty Internet' is empty because most posters are already government-controlled replacements or NPCs.
theories.research_note: no external coverage found for this specific framing; it extends Dead Internet Theory with a cloning element that has no distinct named presence in indexable sources — iceberg-chart lore only

theories.name: Amazon Rainforest Was Built
theories.summary: The Amazon Rainforest is not naturally occurring but was deliberately planted and cultivated by ancient people as a form of agriculture.
evidence_for: Ancient peoples engineered the Amazon through deliberate agriculture rather than it arising naturally, representing advanced prehistoric land management.

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research in Science Advances (2023) confirms Amazonian peoples intentionally created terra preta dark-earth soils for large-scale agriculture over millennia, fundamentally reshaping forest composition.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream archaeology frames Amazon domestication as incremental cultivation, not deliberate wholesale construction; vast areas of the forest show no pre-Columbian human modification.

sources.title: Pre-Columbian agriculture in the Amazon Basin - Wikipedia
Intentional creation of carbon-rich dark earth soils in the Amazon
Terra preta - Wikipedia

theories.name: Wavering Divinity
theories.summary: Humans in antedeluvian times possessed far greater divinity and supernatural power than modern humans, allowing them to construct natural phenomena including the Amazon Rainforest.
evidence_for: Ancient longevity and biblical magic suggest humans once had greater divine potency that has diminished over generations, explaining their construction of natural-seeming phenomena.

evidence_against.text: Archaeological and soil-science research confirms significant ancient human reshaping of the Amazon via terra preta and geoglyphs - but through practical agriculture and engineering, not supernatural divine potency.

sources.title: The Supposedly Pristine Amazon Rainforest Was Actually Shaped By Humans - Smithsonian Magazine
A new hypothesis for the origin of Amazonian Dark Earths - NCBI PMC

theories.name: Gatekeeper's Remorse
theories.summary: Those who know ancient truths and esoteric knowledge have experienced moral remorse and released texts revealing these suppressed truths to the public.
evidence_for: Secret keepers experience guilt for withholding esoteric knowledge and write texts revealing suppressed information as a form of atonement.

theories.name: Esoteric Atonement
theories.summary: Those who made demonic pacts for esoteric knowledge later experience remorse and seek atonement by publicly revealing the knowledge to devalue it.
evidence_for: Demons grant knowledge through pacts; recipients later feel remorse and publicly reveal the knowledge as atonement, thereby devaluing the original demonic bargain.

theories.name: Broder's Engine
theories.summary: A theoretical magnetic perpetual motion machine design capable of generating infinite energy through magnetic forces.
evidence_for: Magnetic perpetual motion designs theoretically generate infinite energy through self-sustaining magnetic interactions without external energy input.

theories.name: Pendulum People Perpetual Power Prevention
theories.summary: Advanced facilitators created our universe to harvest entropy; perpetual energy is suppressed to prevent us from ignoring our hidden purpose.
evidence_for: Our facilitators actively prevent perpetual energy technology; thermodynamic laws are artificial constraints designed to ensure we serve our original purpose of generating entropy.

evidence_against.text: Thermodynamic laws (conservation of energy and entropy increase) make perpetual motion physically impossible; free energy suppression theories lack any verified mechanism or credible insider testimony.

sources.title: Free energy suppression conspiracy theory - Wikipedia
Perpetual motion - Wikipedia

theories.name: Shanyna Isom
theories.summary: A woman with an unexplained and previously undiscovered medical condition causing extreme callousing throughout her body.
evidence_for: Her condition may result from secret military biological testing on civilians as part of undisclosed weaponry or biowarfare trials.

evidence_for.text: Shanyna Isom's condition is medically documented and unique: her hair follicles produce fingernail-like material at 12x the normal rate following a steroid allergic reaction in 2009; Johns Hopkins confirmed she was the only known case worldwide.

evidence_against.text: Doctors attributed the condition to an unusual allergic reaction to steroids combined with vitamin deficiencies; no evidence of military bioweapon testing was raised by medical investigators.

sources.title: Woman's Hair Turns to Black Fingernails, Baffling Her Doctors - ABC News
Shanyna Isom Grows Nails Instead Of Hair Due To Skin Condition - HuffPost

theories.name: Public Target Practice (Biological)
theories.summary: The government uses the public to test biological weapons, diseases, and weaponized medical conditions as military experiments.
evidence_for: Military testing extends beyond conventional weapons to biological phenomena and unexplained medical conditions in civilians as covert biowarfare trials.

evidence_for.text: MKUltra (1953-1964) is a documented CIA program that secretly dosed civilians and mental patients with LSD and other agents through at least 80 institutions without consent.
US government programs including Operation Sea-Spray conducted documented open-air biological agent tests on civilian populations in San Francisco.

sources.title: MKUltra - Wikipedia
Unethical human experimentation in the United States - Wikipedia
The CIA's Appalling Human Experiments With Mind Control

theories.steelman: This is precisely the documented pattern of Operation Sea-Spray, Project 112/SHAD, and the Dugway Proving Ground and Edgewood Arsenal tests, all involving the US government exposing unwitting civilians or service members to biological or chemical agents. No specific ongoing program matching this exact description has been confirmed, but the precedent for the general practice is extensive and repeated.

theories.name: Appendix Monkey Tail
theories.summary: The human appendix is a vestigial organ left over from evolutionary ancestry that is no longer biologically necessary.
evidence_for: The appendix is an evolutionary remnant adapted for earlier human biology; theories suggest it served purposes under ancient environmental conditions no longer present.

evidence_for.text: The appendix is widely recognized as a vestigial structure from ancestors who needed a larger cecum to digest plant fiber; it retains minor immune functions (as a reservoir for gut bacteria) but is not essential.

evidence_against.text: The 'monkey tail' label is inaccurate: vestigial tail structures are represented by the coccyx, not the appendix. The appendix is a remnant of a cecal digestive pouch, not a locomotion organ.

sources.title: Evolution of the Appendix: A Biological Remnant No More - Duke Health
Why do humans have an Appendix? - News-Medical.net

theories.name: Firmament Adapted Organs
theories.summary: Human organs are adapted for the firmament environment mentioned in Genesis; the removal of the water firmament made these organs vestigial.
evidence_for: Organs like the appendix were designed for a water-firmament environment that oxygenated differently; their vestigial nature reflects adaptation loss after the firmament's removal.

theories.name: Concrete Enemas
theories.summary: An unusual phenomenon of people reporting concrete enema injuries to hospitals that is theorized to be a secret society initiation rite.
evidence_for: Unusual clustering of concrete enema injuries suggests organized ritual practice rather than isolated incidents, indicating secret society rite-of-passage involvement.

evidence_against.text: The documented medical case was attributed to consensual sexual activity between adults; no clustering of such cases or secret society connection has been reported in medical literature.

sources.title: Friday Weird Science: The Concrete Enema
Concrete Enemas: An Unconventional and Dangerous Practice

theories.name: MK Ultra Supernatural Detection
theories.summary: MK Ultra experiments may have secretly tested supernatural detection procedures on unwilling subjects to identify and groom those with high spiritual attunement.
evidence_for: MK Ultra may have masked covert supernatural detection procedures; experiments identified and groomed individuals with high supernatural sensitivity using secret methods.

evidence_for.text: CIA MKUltra investigated telepathy and psychokinesis; the separate Project Stargate (1977-1995) at Fort Meade explicitly researched remote viewing as a military intelligence tool, confirming government interest in identifying psychically sensitive subjects.

evidence_against.text: A 1995 CIA review concluded Project Stargate failed to demonstrate the existence of paranormal phenomena and was terminated as operationally useless.

sources.title: Stargate Project - Wikipedia
The CIA Recruited Mind Readers to Spy on the Soviets in the 1970s - HISTORY

theories.steelman: MKUltra and its Operation Midnight Climax subproject confirm the CIA experimented on unwitting subjects with unconventional and pseudoscientific methods well outside mainstream science of the era. No declassified MKUltra document specifically describes testing for 'supernatural' ability, though the program's tolerance for unorthodox research directions is well documented.

theories.name: Dog Suicide Bridge
theories.summary: A bridge in Scotland where an unusual number of dogs have jumped to their deaths, theorized to be haunted by spirits.
evidence_for: Concentrated dog deaths at a specific location suggest hauntings by trapped spirits or electromagnetic anomalies that compel animal self-harm.

evidence_for.text: The phenomenon is documented and real: at least 300 dogs have jumped from Overtoun Bridge since the 1950s, with at least 50 deaths, creating a genuine and unexplained behavioral pattern.

evidence_against.text: A canine psychologist concluded that the potent scent of male mink urine beneath the bridge parapet is the most probable explanation, luring dogs to leap toward the source — a naturalistic rather than supernatural cause.

sources.title: Overtoun Bridge - Wikipedia
Overtoun Bridge: Solving The Mystery Of Scotland's Dog Suicide Hotspot - All That's Interesting
What's Really Going on at the 'Dog Suicide Bridge'? - HowStuffWorks

theories.name: Afterlife Duality
theories.summary: After death, souls ascend to the spiritual realm or become trapped in the noosphere as spirits depending on their allegiance to God or demons.
evidence_for: Rather than heaven/hell, afterlife may involve ascension to God's spiritual realm or entrapment in the noosphere as demonic spirits based on spiritual allegiance.
theories.research_note: fringe spiritual-cosmological noosphere variant; no external secular or academic coverage found; spiritual community lore only

theories.name: Pro War Buddhism

evidence_for: Buddhist militarization in oppressive contexts represents defensive response to systemic religious suppression and violence against their communities.

evidence_for.text: The 969 Movement and MaBaTha organization in Myanmar, led by monks including Ashin Wirathu, openly embraced Buddhist nationalist militarism, providing ideological cover for anti-Rohingya ethnic cleansing; documented across Myanmar, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.

In Myanmar, Buddhist Monks Preach Nationalism - Foreign Policy

theories.summary: Wars and conflicts target spiritual practices and religious enlightenment to psychologically weaken populations, making them easier to conquer long-term.
evidence_for: Historical conflicts aim to suppress spiritual practices that grant willpower and capabilities; destroying religious traditions weakens resistance for future conquest.

evidence_for.text: Documented conquests including Tudor suppression of Catholic Irish spiritual practices and Antiochus IV's banning of Jewish rites (167 BCE) show deliberate targeting of religion to break population resistance.

sources.title: Religious war - Wikipedia
Religious Wars and Religious Freedom: A Troubled History

theories.name: Akashic Records

Akashic Records - Occult World

theories.name: Noosphere Surveillance
theories.summary: The government or demonic entities monitor field consciousness, using surveillance to manipulate collective human thought and grant power to complicit elites.
evidence_for: Demons may monitor field consciousness on behalf of elites; surveillance of noosphere allows precise influence and elevation of pact-makers within human hierarchies.
theories.research_note: No external coverage found; the specific claim that governments or demons monitor collective field consciousness is original iceberg-chart lore derived from the noosphere concept. The legitimate noosphere concept does not include surveillance elements.

theories.name: Cloud Farming
theories.summary: Governments deposit chemicals into clouds to control precipitation, with some using harmful substances causing damage to populations.
evidence_for: Government cloud seeding with chemicals has occurred historically; extension to harmful substance dispersal is plausible given documented weather modification programs.

evidence_for.text: At least nine US states have active government-contracted cloud seeding programs, documented in a 2024 GAO report.

evidence_against.text: NOAA states no technology exists to create, modify, strengthen, or steer hurricanes; NOAA does not fund or oversee cloud seeding or weather modification activities.

sources.title: Cloud seeding - Wikipedia
Chemtrail conspiracy theory - Wikipedia
Fact check: Debunking weather modification claims - NOAA
Why it's so hard to bust the weather control conspiracy theory - MIT Technology Review

theories.steelman: Operation Sea-Spray and Project 112/SHAD both confirm the US government has, in the past, dispersed substances over populated areas without public knowledge or consent to study effects. Weather modification itself is openly practiced by several governments today; harmful covert additives specifically have not been documented.

theories.name: Vibrational Abstinence
theories.summary: Clouds possess unique properties due to lack of vibrational earth energy, allowing elites to harvest rare substances for exclusive use.
evidence_for: Diminished vibrational energy above Earth creates unique substances in clouds; elites harvest these rare properties for unknown technological or spiritual purposes.

theories.name: Limbic Brain
theories.summary: The limbic system takes control when humans are isolated from society, causing abhorrent behaviors due to its primitive nature.
evidence_for: Isolation activates limbic override of higher brain functions; prolonged disconnection from social cohesion releases primal, aggressive responses.

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research confirms social isolation activates dysfunction in the limbic system and ventral striatum, increasing anxiety-like and aggressive behaviors in isolated subjects.

evidence_against.text: Neuroscience shows isolation affects limbic function as part of an integrated network, not a singular takeover; effects vary significantly by individual, duration, and species.

sources.title: Social isolation impairs adult neurogenesis in the limbic system and alters behaviors
How social isolation transforms the brain

theories.name: Spiritual Auras
theories.summary: Isolated individuals lose protection from human auras and become vulnerable to demonic influence from alternate realities.
evidence_for: Isolation strips human-aura protection; disconnected individuals amplify susceptibility to demonic influence from coexisting realities.
theories.research_note: No external coverage found for the specific claim that isolation removes aura protection enabling demonic vulnerability. Aura beliefs are documented in New Age and Theosophical literature but this specific framing is iceberg-chart lore only.

theories.name: Emergency Broadcast Neuralyzers
theories.summary: Emergency broadcasts contain hypnotic or neuralytic components designed to control populations or erase mass memories.
evidence_for: Emergency broadcasts may encode hypnotic triggers; mass memory erasure could occur through frequency manipulation of broadcast signals.

Zombie Conspiracy Theories Spread Ahead of Emergency Test - Newsweek

evidence_for: Demonic telecommunications enable mass memory erasure; fake emergencies summon demons who then execute memory wipes through broadcast neuralizers as inverse-causality events.
theories.research_note: No external coverage found; the claim that emergency broadcast systems function as demon-powered neuralizers is original iceberg-chart lore with no findable sources in news, academic, or fringe media.

theories.name: Roman Metaphysics
theories.summary: Ancient Rome developed philosophical ideas about matter and perpetuity that resemble modern metaphysics and quantum concepts.
evidence_for: Ancient Roman philosophical texts describe perpetual matter and arbitrary forms, suggesting knowledge of quantum principles contradicting traditional history.

theories.name: Fated Reincarnations
theories.summary: Certain souls are reincarnated on predetermined schedules to advance human knowledge; overpopulation creates soulless NPCs when all necessary souls arrive.
evidence_for: If reincarnation operates on fate-based timelines with assigned roles, excess population beyond necessary soul placements produces soulless beings managing predetermined civilization development cycles.

evidence_against.text: The claim that population growth outruns available souls is an arithmetic myth; the number who died in the last century alone exceeds current living population.
The NPC-soul theory originated from a 4chan post combining bogus physics and speculative religion, spreading as a dehumanizing meme rather than a serious spiritual framework.

sources.title: How The 'NPC' Meme Tries To Dehumanize 'SJWs'
Reincarnation and world population - Do the numbers add up?

evidence_for: Earth's isolation and the moon's artificial hollow properties suggest a planetary prison system with lunar surveillance infrastructure.

evidence_against.text: Seismic data from lunar missions shows the Moon has a solid, differentiated interior with thin crust, extensive mantle, and dense core, contradicting the hollow artificial construct claim.

theories.name: Martian Eden

evidence_for: Evidence of water on Mars suggests it was habitable for Eden; humans were transported to Earth after Martian expulsion, with the moon serving as the ark.

evidence_for.text: Ufologist Brinsley Le Poer Trench proposed in his 1960 book The Sky People that the rivers of Genesis match the canal system long theorized to exist on Mars, making Mars the site of Eden.

Lost on Mars - The New Atlantis

theories.name: Inorganic Beings
theories.summary: Non-carbon-based or non-physical life exists outside human perception and comprehension in realms beyond our dimensional awareness.
evidence_for: Non-physical entities may exist beyond human sensory range; inorganic consciousness could operate in alternate dimensions of reality inaccessible to baseline perception.

Inorganic beings - Riverwalker Press

theories.name: Memetic & Spiritual Entities
theories.summary: Entities exist solely in the noosphere and spiritual realm, communicating through astral projection and field consciousness.
evidence_for: Entities inhabiting noosphere and spiritual realms communicate via astral projection and field consciousness; what we perceive as unexplainable phenomena are their native modes of existence.

evidence_against.text: The noosphere as developed by Teilhard de Chardin describes a philosophical layer of collective human thought, not literal autonomous entities; astral projection as a communication mechanism has no scientific evidence.

theories.name: Sleep Deprivation Lifestyle
theories.summary: Extended sleep deprivation causes the primal mind to take control, resulting in loss of self-regulation and higher cognition.
evidence_for: Sleep deprivation grants primal mind dominance; extended wakefulness triggers regressive behavioral and cognitive collapse through loss of higher-brain regulation.

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research confirms sleep deprivation degrades prefrontal cortex function, reducing inhibitory control, decision-making, emotional regulation, and cognitive flexibility - consistent with regression toward reactive, less reasoned behaviour.

evidence_against.text: While cognitive impairment from sleep deprivation is well-documented, the 'primal mind takeover' framing is not a scientific construct; no research identifies a discrete primal mind that assumes dominance during sleep loss.

sources.title: The Common Effects of Sleep Deprivation on Human Long-Term Memory and Cognitive Control - NCBI PMC
Effects of sleep deprivation on cognition - ScienceDirect

theories.name: Spiritual Abstainment
theories.summary: Sleep deprivation and pineal gland calcification deprive humans of spiritual communication, degrading functionality and consciousness.
evidence_for: Sleep deprivation cuts off spiritual realm access; calcified pineal glands prevent spiritual communication, creating subtle cognitive/functional degradation similar to sleep loss effects.

theories.name: Dinosaurs Helped Build The Pyramids
theories.summary: Ancient Egyptian writings describe massive beasts aiding pyramid construction that modern interpretation identifies as dinosaurs.
evidence_for: Egyptian texts depicting enormous labor-beasts align with dinosaur descriptions; these creatures provided construction labor, contradicting extinction chronologies.
theories.research_note: No dedicated external coverage found. The theory is scientifically impossible: non-avian dinosaurs went extinct approximately 66 million years ago, while the Egyptian pyramids were constructed roughly 4,500 years ago. No Wikipedia article, academic source, or news investigation addresses this specific claim; iceberg-chart lore only.

theories.name: Cryptid Labor

evidence_for: Cryptid creatures could serve specialized labor functions or provide invaluable biological knowledge; their suppression suggests deliberate concealment of their capabilities.

evidence_against.text: Sitchin's Anunnaki theories have been universally rejected by mainstream Sumerologists, Orientalists, and Assyriologists as pseudohistory based on deliberately mistranslated and out-of-context Sumerian texts.

Why Some Say One Of History's First Civilizations Was Actually Created By Ancient Aliens - All That's Interesting

evidence_for: Job's trials symbolize humanity's ultimate apocalyptic test; technological collapse will determine whether God reclaims humanity or abandons it to sin, with eternal consequences.

theories.name: Rapture Already Happened
theories.summary: The biblical rapture has already occurred and we live in an abandoned world left behind by God.
evidence_for: If rapture already happened and God departed, the world we inhabit is divine-abandoned, explaining widespread suffering and absence of heavenly intervention.

evidence_for.text: Full preterism (hyperpreterism) is a documented theological position holding that all end-times prophecy including the resurrection and return of Christ already occurred in AD 70; some preterists posit a physical rapture occurred in AD 66.

sources.title: Preterism - Wikipedia
Preterism: Has All Prophecy Been Fulfilled? - The Gospel Coalition

theories.name: Religious Hijacking
theories.summary: The government uses mind-controlled religious authority figures to implant false religious beliefs and control individual minds through drugged hallucinations.
evidence_for: Government agents pose as divine beings to drug-altered subjects; religion serves as mind-control framework, making drugged targets receptive to false religious revelations and elite commands.

evidence_for.text: CIA MKUltra (1953-1973) documented the use of LSD, hypnosis, and psychological manipulation on unwitting subjects, establishing a real precedent for covert government mind-alteration programs.

evidence_against.text: MKUltra documented mind-control experiments but no declassified evidence links these programs to systematic infiltration or control of religious authorities or congregations.

Mind Control and Accountability: Uncovering the Truth of the CIA's MKULTRA Project - House Oversight Committee

theories.name: Greider's Endgame
theories.summary: A secret society controlling the Federal Reserve is attempting to establish a New World Order through financial dominance.
evidence_for: Secret cabal controls central banking; orchestrated financial architecture enables new world order establishment through economic manipulation and sovereign debt bondage.

evidence_for.text: William Greider's 1988 book Secrets of the Temple critically analyzed Federal Reserve opacity and power, arguing the institution operates with minimal democratic accountability - providing fuel for later conspiracy theories though Greider himself dismissed conspiratorial readings.

evidence_against.text: Greider explicitly dismissed the conspiracy interpretation of the Fed in the opening of his book; the NWO banking conspiracy conflates legitimate criticism of central bank opacity with unfounded claims of secret cabal control.

sources.title: Secrets of the Temple: How the Federal Reserve Runs the Country - Simon & Schuster
New World Order conspiracy theory - Wikipedia

theories.name: Post Apocalyptic Stargate Travel
theories.summary: Elites are facilitating apocalyptic reset of humanity to make contact with aliens or access extradimensional alien hub worlds.
evidence_for: Elites orchestrate human reset cycles to contact aliens or return to alien hub-worlds through opened stargates, treating apocalypse as necessary initiation for ascension.

evidence_against.text: No evidence exists for stargate technology; the Iraqi stargate theory, the closest documented variant, has been debunked by archaeologists who found no physical evidence at Mesopotamian sites.

theories.name: Soy Spraying & Weaponized Soy
theories.summary: Soy is deliberately proliferated in modern society to suppress testosterone and weaken populations through estrogenic overload.
evidence_for: Soy's estrogenic properties enable population weakening through excessive consumption; deliberate agricultural promotion ensures hormonal suppression of target demographics.

Combining the “Soybtle Soycietal Soypression” theory with the “Fluoride Is Water Poison” theory, perhaps we can see that not only does soy make one’s soul vulnerable to the ambient energy of the universe, but it also opens one’s soul up to demons directly, stunting one’s divinity and causing the influence of demons to take hold. Combining this with the “Demon World Order” theory, we can see that perhaps demons are purposefully giving the population soy to grant themselves and other demons more power in a more subtle way than the “Atrocity Demons” theory would persist.

evidence_against.text: A 2021 meta-analysis of 41 clinical studies with 1,753 participants found neither soy nor soy isoflavones affect testosterone or estrogen levels in men.

theories.name: Bicameralism
theories.summary: Ancient human brains were bicameral—one part making decisions, the other controlling the body—with sentience developing only recently.
evidence_for: Bicameral brain structure explains ancient ability to perceive divine beings; humans lack current sensory capacity for multi-reality interaction due to evolutionary constraints.

evidence_for.text: Julian Jaynes's 1976 book The Origin of Consciousness in the Breakdown of the Bicameral Mind proposes ancient humans heard auditory hallucinations from the right hemisphere interpreted as god-commands; the theory draws on Homer's Iliad and other ancient texts as evidence.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream neuroscience and psychology have not accepted bicameralism; the theory relies on selective literary interpretation rather than neurological or archaeological evidence.

theories.name: Staring Anxiety
theories.summary: Extended screen exposure causes severe cognitive deterioration beyond conventional understanding, amounting to mental rot.
evidence_for: Screen interaction with digital technology interfaces with demons through electromagnetic signals, causing psychological and spiritual degradation beyond recognized health effects.

evidence_for.text: Academic research links excessive passive screen time to lowered self-esteem, increased mental health issues, slowed learning, and accelerated cognitive decline risk, though effect sizes are modest.

evidence_against.text: Research shows effect sizes are modest; active screen use is associated with better cognitive outcomes; no scientific basis exists for the proposed 'electromagnetic demon interface' mechanism.

evidence_for: Digital technology facilitates demonic communication; users interface with demons through electronics, suffering spiritual and psychological harm similar to black-magic consequences.

theories.name: Ancient Structure Purpose
theories.summary: Ancient megastructures served purposes modern science suppresses or refuses to acknowledge, related to lost ancient technology.
evidence_for: Ancient structures embody technological knowledge science denies; their true purposes—likely astronomical, energetic, or dimensional—are deliberately suppressed from academic study.

evidence_for.text: Many megalithic sites contain precise astronomical alignments (e.g., Stonehenge solstice alignment, Newgrange winter-solstice passage) indicating sophisticated astronomical knowledge; exact construction methods and some purposes remain debated among mainstream scholars.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream archaeology documents the construction methods (stone tools, timber levers, coordinated labor) and interprets megalithic structures as ceremonial, funerary, or astronomical observatories; no evidence of institutional suppression of alternative findings.

sources.title: Megalith - Wikipedia
Decoding the Giants: Unraveling the Mysteries of Ancient Megaliths - FactSpark

theories.name: Leylines True Purpose
theories.summary: Leylines facilitate dimensional travel and allow human survival through reality-shifting during extinction events.
evidence_for: Leylines weaken dimensional boundaries, enabling reality-shifting to alternate timelines during cataclysms; early humans discovered leylines naturally or through facilitation for extinction survival.

NPR reports that criminal syndicates in Myanmar borderlands traffic and enslave people to staff scam operations, with Chinese fugitives running semi-state criminal enterprises generating billions annually.

theories.name: Retrocausality

theories.name: Incorruptible Saints

evidence_for.text: The Catholic Church has documented over 300 cases of saints whose exhumed bodies showed unusual resistance to decomposition; some bodies remained flexible and emitted pleasant odours despite no embalming or special burial conditions.

evidence_against.text: Incorruptibility is no longer counted toward sainthood in Catholic canon law, acknowledging that cool crypts, airless vaults, and saponification can naturally preserve bodies; many 'incorrupt' saints' remains later deteriorated.

sources.title: Incorruptibility - Wikipedia

This may explain how the elites in society are all consistently so abhorrent but still have unity amongst each other, as once a body has been possessed by a demon, the other demons will work with it and bring it into power.

theories.name: Oikeiosis
theories.summary: A philosophy that revolves around humanity’s quality being judged by their behavior toward others, with the proliferation of hedonism and materialism causing humanity to wane in the modern age.
evidence_for: Similar to the “Alighieri’s Insights” theory, perhaps the inverse is also true, and behavior causes souls to be brought into heaven and their bodies to be possessed by angels. This may be another way to remove one’s soul as mentioned in the “Forced Soul Removal” theory and the “Spirit Removal'' theory.

theories.name: Forced Soul Removal
theories.summary: The concept of the soul of a human being susceptible to removal through things like traumatic events or even simple disregarding of the spiritual aspects of humanity, as things like materialism and hedonism will cause one to forfeit their soul to whatever powers will takes its place.
evidence_for: Continuing the “Demonic Interrogation” theory which states that certain drugs or practices may open up one’s mind to demons, perhaps these drugs may even go beyond facilitating mere influence from demons and might even allow a person’s body to become possessed by a demon entirely, resulting in the death of their soul but not their body, or perhaps even trapping their soul within themselves.

theories.name: Real Humans Died Millenia Ago
theories.summary: The theory of humanity in its current state being far removed from humanity of the past, as our current, far weaker state is incapable of the many great feats of those throughout history.
evidence_for: In the Bible, there are numerous prophecies that exist as a response to the sin of humanity, such as the birth and sacrifice of Jesus, and the events of the book of Revelation. Just as well, there is the common theme within the Bible of free will, and that all sin can be prevented by choice.

theories.name: Artifice Predance
theories.summary: A phenomena of humans discovering information or substances that seemed manufactured, but with all knowledge of this information seemingly lost throughout history.
evidence_for: In Genesis, it is stated that God spoke the world into creation. Perhaps this is meant to be taken literally, in that God is such a fundamental force within the universe that merely by speech alone, things are manifested into existence.

theories.name: Hiring A Hitman On Yourself
theories.summary: The concept of forgoing the consequences of suicide by having someone else you hired carry at your own murder.
evidence_for: Applying the “Soul Entrapment” theory to a Biblical context, perhaps just as Abel was killed by Cain, then, as some theories state, Cain was killed by Lamech, perhaps that chain has continued to the modern day.

theories.name: Roman Empire Still Rules The World
theories.summary: The theory that the Roman empire never fell, but is instead ruling the world from the shadows, remaining in control of most of the world’s largest governments.
evidence_for: Just like physical traits can be passed on through generations, perhaps spiritual traits can be passed on as well. This may explain why society seems to be getting progressively worse as time passes, as mentioned in the “Kali Yuga” theory, as the sinful nature of the soul is passed down onto the next generations, whereas whatever forms of divinity the soul had is being suppressed, due to it being a recessive trait.

theories.name: Fractalization
theories.summary: An observed fact by those during particularly intense drug trips where reality appears to be comprised of fundamental patterns, able to be observed consistently by those who experience drug trips.
evidence_for: In the Old Testament, there seemed to be substantial evidence of phenomena like divination and magic, though none exist in modern times. This may be the result of a form of schism that was created between the physical reality, the noosphere, and the spiritual realm.

theories.name: Paleolithic Deep State
theories.summary: The theory of most world governments being controlled by a group that has existed since ancient, prehistoric times, having evolved alongside humanity but remained secret despite their power.
evidence_for: In the Old Testament, we can see that the prophecy of the messiah is carried out through the lineage of those who are true followers of God.

theories.name: Depression Overriding
theories.summary: A spiritual concept of overriding one’s psychological state using force of will, manipulating one’s own psychological energy as a form of non physical alchemy.
evidence_for: Continuing the “Demons Carry Out Prophecies” theory, if these demons do not have free will, then perhaps as humans succumb to sin, they become ruled over by these demons, and thus relinquish their free will as well, becoming tools to carry out prophecies. This may explain certain issues of free will mentioned in the Bible, such as in the book of Exodus when the pharaoh was seemingly influenced by God to refuse the requests of Moses, or Judas’s prophesied betrayal of Jesus.

theories.name: Encorings
theories.summary: Patterns that seem to emerge throughout all instances of humanity around the globe, such as weaponry, funeral traditions, and religion.
evidence_for: Great resets of humanity may be facilitated not by chance or humanity themselves, but instead by higher powers such as God himself to prevent humanity from reaching a certain undesirable point. Similarly, God’s promise to Noah to no longer reset humanity in the Bible may be an indicator that our current state is our final chance to receive whatever desirable outcome is needed.

theories.name: Golden Rules Of Nature
theories.summary: The notion of human nature abiding by certain rules that must be maintained and followed so as to keep humanity in a proper state, and that these rules are being disregarded in the modern day, thus resulting in a global weakening of humanity.
evidence_for: Continuing the “True Free Will” theory, and combining it with the “Divined Heritage” theory, which states that ancient societies may write down their desired futures so that they can remain in public consciousness and thus come to pass in the future, perhaps we can see that in the communication of the prophecies of the Bible, the future of the prophecy was inevitable. The “Demons Carry Out Prophecies” theory states that demons do not have free will, and as the “True Free Will” theory states, when humans submit to these demons, they also relinquish their free will and thus contribute to these prophecies, but if we accept that the thoughts and desires of humans can influence the future, then perhaps this is the method by which these prophecies will come to pass, as it is the will of God for these prophecies to be carried out, and those who abide by the will of God will drive the universe toward these prophecies, even unknowingly.

theories.name: God’s Ego Death
theories.summary: The religious theory of God splitting himself apart to create the universe as we know it, and that evidence of God can be observed when we view the universe on a fundamental scale.
evidence_for: As the “The Reality Schism” theory states, God exists as the ruler of the spiritual world and the devil exists as the ruler of the noosphere, and that humanity exists as rulers of the physical world. This may explain theories such as “Divine Humanity” or other theories that humans have some form of godly energy within every person, such as “Residual Divinity”, as the previously unified God has split himself apart, no longer ruling all three realities, and instead forfeiting a third this rule to humans and a third to satan.

theories.name: God’s Last Wish
theories.summary: The notion that God split himself apart to create the universe, as mentioned in the “God’s Ego Death” theory, so as to create humanity as a vessel for free will, with free will being seen as worth breaking apart divinity for.
evidence_for: In the garden of Eden, God appears to be unaware of the sin the humans have committed while he is in the garden. This may suggest the idea that while God had ultimate power in all three realities, this omniscience was partially forfeit to create humanity, as mentioned in “God’s Ego Death”, and that human free will may exist beyond the omniscience of God, such as how free will is abstained from the influence of God in the “True Free Will” theory.

theories.name: There Is No Conspiracy
theories.summary: The idea that there is no grand conspiracy that hangs above all people, and that everyone on Earth is equally as weak and unknowledgeable as everyone else, with all actions of humanity being responses to this fear.
evidence_for: Combining the “Humanity Is A God” theory with the “The Human Experiment” theory, perhaps a battle for humanity between God in the spiritual world and the devil in the noosphere is taking place, as the demons that proliferate sin attempt to halt humanity’s progress and ascension so as to delay the inevitable prophecies of revelation.

theories.name: All Conspiracies Are True
theories.summary: The theory that all theories, particularly ones that deal with intense concepts of reality, religion, and science, can be traced back to a single root source that we do not know yet.
evidence_for: Akin to the “Lucifer’s War” theory, perhaps the true will of God is to proliferate truth and beauty in the world rather than sin. As God is the origin of everything, then the pursuit of these truths will only lead to God, and thus will proliferate beauty.

theories.name: The Final Understanding
theories.summary: The idea that all theories, while they attempt to explain an immense range of topics and phenomena, have the commonality of being created and understood by humans, and that each individual person has a purpose in these theories as their own comprehender and potential contributor.
evidence_for: Combining the “Lucifer’s War” theory with the “Humanity’s purpose” theory, we can conclude that humanity’s purpose is not subjugation or subservience, but understanding and enlightenment. Thusly, the pursuit of knowledge and understanding of the world around us, either seen or unseen, brings humanity another step toward their perfect ascension.

theories.name: Operation Sea-Spray
theories.summary: In September 1950 the U.S. Navy secretly sprayed the bacteria Serratia marcescens and Bacillus globigii over the San Francisco Bay Area from an offshore ship to study how vulnerable a city would be to a biological weapons attack. The test exposed hundreds of thousands of residents without their knowledge; it became public in 1977 when Senate hearings following the Church Committee investigations exposed a range of secret Army and Navy biodefense testing programs.

evidence_for.text: U.S. Army records show the bacteria were dispersed from a vessel offshore of San Francisco from September 20-27, 1950, exposing an estimated 800,000 residents.
The program's existence was confirmed and detailed in 1977 U.S. Senate subcommittee hearings on Army and Navy biological testing.

evidence_for: An Army witness at the 1977 hearings defended the program, stating the agents were 'believed at the time to be non-pathogenic.'

evidence_against.text: A causal link between the test and the cluster of Serratia infections at Stanford Hospital (including the death of Edward J. Nevin) was never proven in court; all patients had recent medical procedures that could independently explain infection.

sources.title: Operation Sea-Spray
In 1950, the U.S. Released a Bioweapon in San Francisco

theories.name: Guatemala Syphilis Experiments
aliases: US Public Health Service Guatemala experiments
theories.summary: Between 1946 and 1948, U.S. Public Health Service researchers led by Dr. John Cutler deliberately infected roughly 1,300 Guatemalan soldiers, prisoners, psychiatric patients, and sex workers with syphilis, gonorrhea, and chancroid without informed consent, in order to test penicillin's effectiveness. The experiments remained obscure until historian Susan Reverby discovered Cutler's unpublished archives in 2005; the findings were made public in October 2010, prompting a formal U.S. government apology.

evidence_for.text: Wellesley College historian Susan Reverby uncovered Dr. John Cutler's own unpublished records documenting the deliberate-infection study while researching the Tuskegee syphilis study archives.
On October 1, 2010, Secretary of State Hillary Clinton and HHS Secretary Kathleen Sebelius issued a joint statement calling the experiments 'reprehensible' and formally apologizing to Guatemala.

evidence_for: A U.S. presidential bioethics commission subsequently concluded the study involved 'gross violations of ethics.'

evidence_against.text: Multiple lawsuits by victims' families seeking compensation have been dismissed or remain unresolved, so the full scope of harm and appropriate redress remain disputed.

sources.title: Syphilis experiments in Guatemala
U.S. Apologizes For Syphilis Experiment In Guatemala

theories.name: Vanderbilt Radioactive Iron Pregnancy Study
aliases: Craft v. Vanderbilt University radiation study
theories.summary: From 1945 to 1947, Vanderbilt University researchers, working with the Tennessee Department of Health, gave roughly 829 pregnant women 'nutrition cocktails' containing radioactive iron without disclosing the radioactive tracer, ostensibly to study iron absorption in pregnancy. The study came to light in the early 1990s amid broader U.S. human radiation experiment disclosures, leading to a class-action lawsuit and a 1998 multi-million-dollar settlement.

evidence_for.text: Court records in the resulting class action document that Vanderbilt researchers administered radioactive iron-59 tracers to pregnant women from 1945-47 without informing them of the radioactive component.

Vanderbilt agreed to a multi-million-dollar settlement covering the women and their children after the university acknowledged it had lost many of the records identifying which women received the radioactive tracer.

evidence_for: The study is cited in the federal Advisory Committee on Human Radiation Experiments (ACHRE) report on Cold War-era radiation research.

evidence_against.text: Government and university reviews concluded the radiation doses administered were low and unlikely to have caused measurable harm, even though consent was inadequate.

sources.title: Human radiation experiments
Vanderbilt Settles Lawsuit Over Radiation Tests on Pregnant Women in the 1940s
Craft v. Vanderbilt University, 18 F. Supp. 2d 786 (M.D. Tenn. 1998)

theories.name: Fernald School Radioactive Oatmeal Experiments
theories.summary: Between 1946 and 1953, Harvard and MIT researchers, with funding tied to Quaker Oats, fed radioactive iron- and calcium-laced cereal and milk to boys at the Walter E. Fernald State School in Massachusetts, recruiting them into a fictitious 'Science Club' with incentives like Red Sox tickets, without informing them or their parents of the radioactive tracers. The experiments surfaced publicly in the early 1990s amid nationwide disclosures of Cold War radiation research, leading to a 1995 class-action lawsuit and a 1998 settlement.

evidence_for.text: MIT nutrition professor Robert S. Harris led studies in which boys at the Fernald School consumed radioactively-tagged iron and calcium in their food without informed parental consent.
MIT and Quaker Oats paid $1.85 million to settle the resulting class-action lawsuit, formally acknowledged in a January 1998 court-approved settlement.

evidence_for: The experiments were documented as part of the federal Advisory Committee on Human Radiation Experiments investigation launched after 1993 press disclosures.

evidence_against.text: The federal Advisory Committee on Human Radiation Experiments concluded the radiation doses were low and it was 'extremely unlikely' any child was physically harmed, though the lack of consent remained ethically indefensible.

sources.title: Fernald State School
Court approves Fernald settlement
A Spoonful of Sugar Helps the Radioactive Oatmeal Go Down

theories.name: Unit 731
aliases: Epidemic Prevention and Water Purification Department
theories.summary: Between 1933 and 1945, the Imperial Japanese Army's Unit 731, led by Lieutenant General Shiro Ishii, conducted lethal biological and chemical experiments -- including vivisection, deliberate infection with plague and other pathogens, and frostbite testing -- on thousands of Chinese, Russian, Korean, and other prisoners at a facility in Harbin, Manchuria. The unit's crimes were partially revealed at a 1949 Soviet war crimes trial, but the United States secretly granted Ishii and other leaders immunity from prosecution in exchange for their research data, a cover-up that became broadly documented through declassified U.S. records and investigative journalism from the early 1980s onward.

evidence_for.text: Twelve Unit 731 personnel were tried and convicted by the Soviet Union at the 1949 Khabarovsk War Crimes Trials for developing and using biological weapons.

Declassified U.S. National Archives records, including memoranda from Brigadier General Charles Willoughby, document that General MacArthur's headquarters secretly granted immunity to Ishii and other Unit 731 leaders in exchange for exclusive access to their human-experimentation data.

evidence_for: In 2002, the Tokyo District Court formally acknowledged Japan had conducted biological warfare in China during the war.

evidence_against.text: Some specific casualty figures (estimates range from about 3,000 to over 14,000 victims) and the precise extent of the American cover-up remain debated among historians due to destroyed records.

sources.title: Unit 731
100,000 Pages Declassified in Search for Japanese War Crimes Records

theories.name: Edgewood Arsenal Human Experiments
aliases: Army Chemical Corps Medical Research Volunteer Program
theories.summary: From the 1950s to 1975, the U.S. Army Chemical Corps administered more than 250 chemical agents -- including nerve agents, LSD, BZ, and mustard gas -- to roughly 7,000 soldiers and civilians at Edgewood Arsenal, Maryland, often without adequate informed consent or follow-up medical care. The program was shut down amid scandal in 1975 after Army and congressional scrutiny, and its scope was later confirmed in detail by a 1994 GAO investigation and a series of Institute of Medicine studies.

evidence_for.text: A 1994 U.S. Government Accountability Office report on Cold War-era human experimentation confirmed roughly 7,120 Army and Air Force personnel were used as test subjects at Edgewood and affiliated sites between 1952 and 1975.
The Army's Medical Research Volunteer Program at Edgewood Arsenal was abruptly terminated in 1975 amid a scandal over undisclosed testing on soldiers.

evidence_for: Federal courts ruled in 2013 and again on appeal in 2015 that the Army has an ongoing legal duty to notify and provide medical care to former test subjects.

evidence_against.text: The GAO itself concluded precise information on the full scope of the testing program may never be known because records were incomplete or destroyed.

sources.title: Edgewood Arsenal human experiments
Human Experimentation: An Overview on Cold War Era Programs (GAO/T-NSIAD-94-266)

theories.name: Project 112 / Project SHAD
theories.summary: From 1962 to 1973, the U.S. Department of Defense's Project 112, and its shipboard component Project SHAD, exposed thousands of American service members to chemical and biological agents -- including sarin and VX nerve agents -- in tests conducted at sea and at land sites, largely without the subjects' knowledge or consent. The Pentagon denied the program's existence for decades until a CBS News investigation broadcast in May 2000 forced the Department of Defense to confirm the tests and begin notifying and treating affected veterans.

evidence_for.text: A CBS Evening News investigation broadcast in May 2000, the result of a six-year probe by journalist Eric Longabardi, exposed more than 100 secret biological and chemical warfare tests conducted at sea on U.S. sailors.
Following the CBS report, the Department of Defense and Department of Veterans Affairs launched formal investigations and began notifying veterans of their exposure.

evidence_for: A 2007 Institute of Medicine study assessed long-term health effects on Project SHAD participants at the government's request.

evidence_against.text: Because many participants were never told what agents they were exposed to, and complete unit rosters were not maintained, the full scope of exposure and subsequent health effects remains difficult to establish with certainty.

sources.title: Project 112
DoD Tested Weapons On Thousands

theories.name: Bayer HIV-Tainted Factor VIII
theories.summary: In the early-to-mid 1980s, Bayer's Cutter Biological division and other manufacturers continued selling older, non-heat-treated clotting-factor concentrate known to risk carrying HIV to hemophiliacs in Asia and Latin America even after a safer heat-treated version was approved in the U.S. in February 1984, while marketing the safer product domestically. The dual-track strategy was exposed through internal company documents surfaced in litigation and a 2003 New York Times investigation, leading to a 1997 settlement of roughly $600 million with thousands of infected U.S. hemophiliacs.

evidence_for.text: Internal Cutter memos, marketing meeting minutes, and telexes to foreign distributors, obtained through litigation discovery, showed the company knowingly continued selling unheated, HIV-risk concentrate abroad in countries including Argentina, Hong Kong, and Japan after switching to heat-treated product in the U.S.

Bayer and three other blood-product manufacturers paid roughly $600 million in 1997 to settle lawsuits brought by more than 6,000 U.S. hemophiliacs infected with HIV through contaminated blood products.

evidence_for: A Cutter manager's internal 1983 memo acknowledged 'strong evidence to suggest that AIDS is passed on to other people through ... plasma products.'

evidence_against.text: The companies maintained the overseas sales were a response to genuine supply shortages of the newer product rather than a deliberate cost-driven decision to dump risky stock, a defense some courts weighed in dismissing certain claims.

sources.title: Contaminated haemophilia blood products
Bayer Sold HIV-Risky Meds

theories.name: Ethiopian Immigrant Birth-Control Injections
aliases: Where Did the Children Disappear To
theories.summary: Israeli health authorities and clinics administered the long-acting contraceptive Depo-Provera to Ethiopian Jewish immigrant women, many while they were still in transit camps in Ethiopia, often without adequate informed consent about its sterilizing effects, contributing to a roughly 50% decline in the community's birthrate over a decade. The practice was exposed by a December 2012 Israeli television documentary, and in February 2013 the Health Ministry acknowledged the problem and ordered clinics to stop prescribing the drug without ensuring patients understood its effects.

evidence_for.text: Israel's Health Ministry director general ordered gynecologists to stop renewing Depo-Provera prescriptions for Ethiopian women without confirming comprehension, an implicit admission that consent had been inadequate.
Investigations found that between 2005 and 2008, Ethiopian women -- fewer than 2% of Israel's population -- made up 57% of all Depo-Provera recipients at Israeli health maintenance organizations.

evidence_for: The practice was first brought to wide public attention by journalist Gal Gabai's December 2012 documentary 'Vanishing Act (Where Did the Children Disappear To).'

evidence_against.text: Israeli officials denied any deliberate policy of ethnically-targeted population control, characterizing the pattern as a result of individual clinic practices and cultural miscommunication rather than a coordinated program.

sources.title: Israel Admits Targeting Ethiopian Jews for Compulsory Contraception
Did Israeli Doctors Force Contraception on Ethiopian Immigrants?

theories.name: Dugway Proving Ground Chemical Tests
theories.summary: The U.S. Army's Dugway Proving Ground in Utah conducted decades of open-air chemical and biological weapons testing, including a March 1968 incident in which an F-4 Phantom's nerve-agent (VX) spray drifted into nearby Skull Valley and killed roughly 6,000 sheep. The Army resisted acknowledging responsibility for years; a classified 1970 report finding 'incontrovertible' evidence of VX contamination was not released to the public until 1998, and the incident helped prompt President Nixon's 1969 ban on open-air chemical weapons testing.

evidence_for.text: A 1970 U.S. Army report, kept classified until its 1998 public release, found VX nerve agent residue in snow, grass, and animal tissue samples collected from Skull Valley three weeks after the March 1968 sheep deaths, calling the evidence 'incontrovertible.'
Government scientists' chemical analysis found the VX-type compounds recovered from the site were identical to the nerve agent supplied to the Army for the test.

evidence_for: The incident is widely credited with contributing to President Nixon's November 1969 decision to renounce first use of chemical weapons and halt open-air testing.

evidence_against.text: The Army never formally admitted negligence or legal responsibility for the sheep deaths even after the report was made public.

sources.title: Dugway sheep incident
How the Death of 6,000 Sheep Spurred the American Debate on Chemical Weapons

theories.name: Lavon Affair (Operation Susannah)
theories.summary: In 1954, Israeli military intelligence (Unit 131) recruited Egyptian Jews to plant bombs at American, British, and Egyptian civilian targets in Cairo and Alexandria, intending to be blamed on Egyptian nationalists and derail British withdrawal from the Suez Canal Zone. Egyptian authorities arrested and publicly tried the operatives in 1954; Israel denied involvement for decades until 2005, when President Moshe Katsav publicly honored the surviving agents.

evidence_for.text: Egyptian authorities arrested the Israeli-recruited operatives in 1954 and put them on a public trial, exposing the bombing campaign; two were executed.
In March 2005, Israeli President Moshe Katsav awarded certificates of appreciation to the surviving Operation Susannah agents, formally ending Israel's decades-long denial of the operation.

evidence_against.text: Israel's internal 1960 Olshan-Dori Committee inquiry found Defense Minister Pinhas Lavon had likely not personally authorized the operation, so exact command responsibility remains disputed.

sources.title: Lavon Affair
Israel honors 9 Egyptian spies

theories.name: Katyn Massacre
theories.summary: In spring 1940, the Soviet NKVD executed nearly 22,000 Polish military officers, police, and intelligentsia at Katyn Forest and other sites, on direct written orders from Stalin and the Politburo. The USSR blamed Nazi Germany for the massacre for 50 years until April 1990, when Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev formally admitted Soviet responsibility and began releasing the incriminating archives.

evidence_for.text: In April 1990 Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev admitted the NKVD had executed the Poles and identified additional mass-grave sites at Mednoye and Piatykhatky.
Declassified Soviet archives, including Beria's March 1940 execution proposal signed by Stalin, were released by Boris Yeltsin to Poland, confirming the 21,857 documented victims.

evidence_against.text: Russian officials have periodically sought to relitigate or downplay the finding since the 1990 admission, though the historical record itself is not seriously disputed by historians.

sources.title: Katyn massacre
After 80 Years, The 'Katyn Lie' Lives On In Russia

theories.name: Operation Ajax (1953 Iranian Coup)
theories.summary: In August 1953, the CIA and British MI6 orchestrated the overthrow of Iran's democratically elected Prime Minister Mohammad Mosaddegh after he nationalized the Anglo-Iranian Oil Company, replacing him with the authoritarian rule of the Shah. The US government formally acknowledged the CIA's role for the first time in August 2013 with the release of declassified internal documents.

evidence_for.text: Declassified CIA internal documents released in August 2013 state the coup was conducted 'under CIA direction' as 'an act of U.S. foreign policy, conceived and approved at the highest levels of government.'
The CIA's own internal history and subsequent National Security Archive document releases detail the planning and execution of the TPAJAX covert operation.

evidence_against.text: Some operational details, including the full extent of British MI6 involvement, remain redacted in released files.

sources.title: 1953 Iranian coup d'état
CIA Confirms Role in 1953 Iran Coup

theories.name: Mukden Incident
theories.summary: On September 18, 1931, Japanese Kwantung Army officers detonated a small explosive charge on the South Manchuria Railway near Mukden and blamed Chinese saboteurs, using the incident as a pretext to invade and occupy Manchuria. The League of Nations' Lytton Commission concluded in its October 1932 report that Japan's justification was unfounded, and postwar investigations further confirmed Japanese officers had staged the blast themselves.

evidence_for.text: The League of Nations' Lytton Commission report (October 1932) rejected Japan's justification for the invasion and found Manchukuo to be a product of Japanese military aggression.
Postwar historical investigation confirmed Kwantung Army officers Seishirō Itagaki and Kanji Ishiwara planned and executed the railway bombing themselves.

evidence_against.text: None — documented historical fact; even Japan's own Yūshūkan war museum now acknowledges Kwantung Army responsibility for staging the incident.

sources.title: Mukden Incident
Situation in Manchuria: Report of the Lytton Commission of Inquiry

theories.name: Operation Himmler (Gleiwitz Incident)
theories.summary: On the night of August 31, 1939, SS operatives under Alfred Naujocks staged an attack on the Gleiwitz radio station, dressing in Polish uniforms and leaving murdered concentration-camp prisoners as fake casualties, fabricating a pretext for Nazi Germany's invasion of Poland the next day. The deception was exposed at the Nuremberg trials in 1945 through Naujocks' sworn affidavit.

evidence_for.text: Alfred Naujocks' November 1945 affidavit, entered as Nuremberg prosecution exhibit PS-2751, describes orders from Heydrich and Müller to stage the fake Polish attack on Gleiwitz.
Hitler cited the fabricated 'border incidents' in his September 1, 1939 Reichstag speech justifying the invasion of Poland.

evidence_against.text: None — documented historical fact, established via sworn Nuremberg testimony from the operation's own commander.

sources.title: Operation Himmler
Affidavit concerning the staging of the supposed Polish attack (Alfred Naujocks, Nuremberg IMT)

theories.name: Operation Fast and Furious
theories.summary: Between 2009 and 2011, ATF agents in Arizona deliberately allowed licensed gun dealers to sell firearms to suspected straw purchasers for Mexican drug cartels, hoping to trace the weapons to cartel leadership, but lost track of most of the roughly 2,000 guns involved. The scandal became public after Border Patrol agent Brian Terry was killed in December 2010 with weapons traced to the operation, prompting congressional investigations in 2011.

evidence_for.text: The DOJ Office of the Inspector General's September 2012 report, 'A Review of ATF's Operation Fast and Furious and Related Matters,' documented the operation's management failures and found 14 ATF/DOJ officials responsible.

2011 congressional hearings led by Senator Chuck Grassley produced whistleblower testimony from ATF agents describing the 'gunwalking' tactics and orders to 'stand down' rather than interdict weapons.

evidence_against.text: The Inspector General's report found no evidence that Attorney General Eric Holder personally authorized the gunwalking tactics.

sources.title: ATF gunwalking scandal
A Review of ATF's Operation Fast and Furious and Related Matters

theories.name: Nayirah Incubator Testimony
theories.summary: In October 1990, a 15-year-old identified only as 'Nayirah' tearfully testified to a US congressional caucus that she saw Iraqi soldiers rip Kuwaiti babies from hospital incubators and leave them to die; she was later revealed to be the daughter of Kuwait's ambassador to the US, and her testimony had been coached by the PR firm Hill & Knowlton as part of a Kuwaiti-government-funded campaign to build American support for war with Iraq. Journalists and human rights investigators exposed the fabrication in 1992.

evidence_for.text: Middle East Watch (Human Rights Watch) published its investigative report 'Kuwait's Stolen Incubators' in February 1992, finding no truth to the central incubator claims.

Journalist John MacArthur's 1992 reporting identified Nayirah as the daughter of Kuwait's ambassador to the US and detailed Hill & Knowlton's roughly $12 million PR campaign for Citizens for a Free Kuwait.

evidence_against.text: Iraqi forces did commit other documented abuses during the occupation of Kuwait, though not the specific incubator atrocity described in the testimony.

sources.title: Nayirah testimony
Kuwait's Stolen Incubators: The Widespread Repercussions of a Murky Incident

theories.name: 1954 Guatemalan Coup (PBSuccess)
theories.summary: In June 1954, the CIA's Operation PBSuccess armed, trained, and directed a small rebel force under Carlos Castillo Armas, combined with psychological warfare and bombing runs, to overthrow Guatemala's democratically elected President Jacobo Árbenz after his land reforms threatened the US-based United Fruit Company. The CIA's central role was confirmed through declassified documents released beginning in 1997.

evidence_for.text: Declassified CIA records released in 1997 (National Security Archive Electronic Briefing Book No. 4), including assassination target lists and internal histories, document the agency's direction of the coup.
CIA historian Nicholas Cullather's declassified internal study, 'Operation PBSUCCESS: The United States and Guatemala, 1952-1954,' details the agency's planning and execution of the operation.

evidence_against.text: The full extent of CIA assassination planning versus mere contingency listing of targets remains debated by historians.

sources.title: 1954 Guatemalan coup d'état
CIA and Assassinations: The Guatemala 1954 Documents

theories.name: Overthrow of the Hawaiian Kingdom
theories.summary: On January 17, 1893, a group of American and European businessmen in Honolulu, backed by US Minister John L. Stevens and armed US Marines and sailors from the USS Boston, overthrew Queen Liliʻuokalani's government and installed a Provisional Government, paving the way for US annexation. President Grover Cleveland's own 1893 investigation (the Blount Report) concluded the overthrow was illegal and unauthorized, and in 1993 Congress formally apologized via the Apology Resolution, acknowledging American agents' role.

evidence_for.text: President Cleveland's 1893 message to Congress, based on the Blount Report investigation, called the overthrow 'an act of war' committed without the consent of the Hawaiian people.

The 1993 US Apology Resolution (Public Law 103-150), signed by President Clinton, formally acknowledges 'the active participation of agents and citizens of the United States' in the overthrow of the Kingdom of Hawaii.

evidence_against.text: The 2009 US Supreme Court ruling in Hawaii v. Office of Hawaiian Affairs held that the Apology Resolution's language does not itself alter legal title to Hawaiian lands.

sources.title: Overthrow of the Hawaiian Kingdom
Apology Resolution (Public Law 103-150) excerpts

theories.name: Operation Menu (Secret Bombing of Cambodia)
theories.summary: From March 1969 to May 1970, the Nixon administration secretly ordered thousands of B-52 bombing raids on Cambodia while falsifying official reports to conceal the campaign from Congress and the public. The dual-reporting deception was exposed in 1973 after whistleblower Major Hal Knight alerted Senator William Proxmire, prompting Senate Armed Services Committee hearings in which Secretary of Defense James Schlesinger publicly confirmed the secret bombing.

evidence_for.text: July 1973 Senate Armed Services Committee hearings featured Major Hal Knight's testimony describing the falsified dual-reporting system used to hide roughly 3,875 bombing missions from Congress.
Defense Secretary James Schlesinger publicly acknowledged in 1973 that B-52s had secretly bombed Cambodia in 1969 and 1970.

evidence_against.text: None — documented historical fact, confirmed by Pentagon officials' own congressional testimony.

sources.title: Operation Menu
The Shadow War in Cambodia

theories.name: Cambridge Analytica Data Scandal
theories.summary: British political consulting firm Cambridge Analytica harvested personal data from up to 87 million Facebook users via a personality-quiz app, without informed consent, and used it to build psychological profiles for targeted political advertising for the 2016 Trump and Brexit Leave.EU campaigns. Whistleblower Christopher Wylie's March 2018 disclosures to The Guardian and The New York Times exposed the operation.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents the app's harvesting of up to 87 million Facebook profiles and use in the 2016 Trump and Cruz campaigns and the Brexit Leave.EU campaign.
The U.S. FTC imposed a record $5 billion penalty on Facebook in July 2019 for privacy violations tied to the scandal, alongside sweeping new privacy restrictions.

evidence_for: The UK Information Commissioner's Office fined Facebook £500,000 in October 2019, and Meta paid $725 million in a December 2022 class-action settlement.
evidence_against: None -- documented historical fact, confirmed by regulatory fines, court settlements, and whistleblower testimony corroborated by internal documents.

sources.title: Facebook–Cambridge Analytica data scandal
FTC Imposes $5 Billion Penalty and Sweeping New Privacy Restrictions on Facebook

theories.name: Internet Research Agency (Project Lakhta)
theories.summary: The Internet Research Agency, a St. Petersburg-based operation founded and financed by Yevgeny Prigozhin, ran coordinated fake social-media accounts and ad campaigns to influence U.S. politics and sow social division, including during the 2016 presidential election. A February 2018 U.S. federal grand jury indicted 13 Russian nationals and three entities, including the IRA, for the scheme.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents the IRA's founding by Prigozhin, its troll-farm operations, and the 2018 U.S. indictment of 13 individuals and three entities.
The Washington Post reported on the Justice Department's February 16, 2018 indictment of 13 Russians and three companies for interfering in the 2016 U.S. election.

evidence_for: Prigozhin himself publicly admitted in February 2023, "I invented it, I created it, I managed it for a long time."
evidence_against: The precise electoral impact of IRA activity on 2016 voting outcomes remains disputed among researchers.

sources.title: Internet Research Agency
Special counsel Mueller indicts 13 Russians for election interference

theories.name: GCHQ JTRIG
theories.summary: JTRIG, a covert unit within Britain's GCHQ, ran online "dirty tricks" operations including denial-of-service attacks, false-flag postings, honey traps, and coordinated manipulation of social media to discredit targets and shape online discourse. Its existence and tactics were revealed in 2014 through documents leaked by former NSA contractor Edward Snowden and published by Glenn Greenwald.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents JTRIG's cyberattacks, malware deployment, and "Online Covert Action" information-warfare tactics disclosed via Snowden documents.
The Intercept published the leaked GCHQ training document "The Art of Deception," detailing JTRIG methods to inject false material online and destroy targets' reputations.

evidence_against: GCHQ has not confirmed the full scope of operations or specific individual targets beyond what appears in the leaked documents.

sources.title: Joint Threat Research Intelligence Group
How Covert Agents Infiltrate the Internet to Manipulate, Deceive, and Destroy Reputations

theories.name: British Army 77th Brigade
theories.summary: The British Army's 77th Brigade, formed in January 2015 from the Security Assistance Group, conducts information operations and psychological-warfare campaigns, officially targeting foreign adversaries and disinformation. Reporting from 2019-2023, including a parliamentary admission by then-Defence Secretary Ben Wallace, confirmed the unit also monitored British citizens' social media posts, including during the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents the brigade's formation, its information-operations mission, and the 2019-2023 revelations of domestic social-media monitoring.

Big Brother Watch reported that Defence Secretary Ben Wallace admitted to Parliament that 77th Brigade "uses... material shared on social media platforms to assess UK disinformation trends," contradicting earlier Army denials.

evidence_against.text: The Army disputes characterizations of the monitoring as surveillance or action "against" British citizens, framing it as counter-disinformation work.

sources.title: 77th Brigade (United Kingdom)
Defence Secretary Admits 77th Brigade Tasked to "UK Disinformation"

theories.name: Pentagon Military Analyst Program
theories.summary: Starting in 2002, the Pentagon under Assistant Secretary of Defense Victoria Clarke recruited dozens of retired military officers appearing as independent TV analysts, giving them classified briefings, talking points, and access in exchange for promoting administration messaging on Iraq -- without disclosing many analysts' financial ties to defense contractors. New York Times reporter David Barstow exposed the program in an April 2008 investigation that won the 2009 Pulitzer Prize.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents the program's 2002 launch, its 147 events for 74 analysts, and Barstow's Pulitzer-winning exposure.

Democracy Now reported on Barstow's Pulitzer Prize-winning investigation, detailing analysts' undisclosed defense-contractor ties and Pentagon talking points used in at least 4,500 TV and radio appearances.

evidence_against.text: A Pentagon Inspector General report initially found no wrongdoing, though it was later withdrawn after being found to have significant factual problems.

sources.title: Pentagon military analyst program
Pentagon's Pundits: NY Times Reporter David Barstow Wins Pulitzer Prize for Exposing Military's Pro-War Propaganda Media Campaign

theories.name: ECHELON
theories.summary: ECHELON is a global signals-intelligence collection network operated jointly by the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand under the UKUSA Agreement, capable of intercepting telephone, fax, email, and satellite communications worldwide. A 2000-2001 European Parliament investigation concluded the system existed and was used to intercept private and commercial as well as military communications, a finding later corroborated by Edward Snowden's leaked NSA documents.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents ECHELON's Cold War origins, the Five Eyes structure, and its 2001 European Parliament investigation.

A European Union committee report to the European Parliament concluded that Echelon does indeed exist as a global interception network, per contemporary coverage of the 367-159 vote adopting the findings.

evidence_against.text: The precise current capabilities and interception volume of the system remain classified and disputed.

sources.title: ECHELON
Echelon Spying Network Exists, EU Committee Says

theories.name: Operation CHAOS
theories.summary: Operation CHAOS was a CIA domestic espionage program (1967-1974), established under President Johnson and expanded under Nixon, that compiled files on over 7,000 Americans and infiltrated antiwar, civil rights, and other protest groups to hunt for (ultimately unfound) foreign influence. Journalist Seymour Hersh exposed the program in a December 22, 1974 New York Times article, prompting the Rockefeller Commission and the Church Committee investigations.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia documents the program's 1967-1974 operation, its targeting of the antiwar and civil-rights movements, and Hersh's 1974 exposure.

The State Department's official historical record confirms Hersh's December 22, 1974 article revealed intelligence files on at least 10,000 American citizens and triggered Justice Department and congressional investigations.

evidence_against: None -- documented historical fact, confirmed by the Church Committee, Rockefeller Commission, and CIA's own "Family Jewels" records.

sources.title: Operation CHAOS
Editorial Note (CIA domestic operations disclosure), Foreign Relations of the United States, 1969-1976

theories.name: MOVE Bombing (1985 Philadelphia)
theories.summary: On May 13, 1985, Philadelphia police, on orders from Mayor Wilson Goode and Police Commissioner Gregore Sambor, dropped explosives from a helicopter onto a row house occupied by the MOVE organization; the resulting fire killed eleven people, including five children, and destroyed 61 homes. A city commission found the bombing of an occupied home unconscionable, and subsequent federal civil suits found the city liable for excessive force.

evidence_for.text: Wikipedia's detailed account documents the police helicopter bombing, the 11 deaths, the destruction of 61 homes, and the 1986 MOVE Commission report condemning the city's actions.
NPR's 30th-anniversary coverage recounts the bombing and the city's ongoing struggle to regain public trust after one of the country's most extreme incidents of police violence.

evidence_against.text: No city official faced criminal charges, though the city paid civil settlements ($1.5 million in 1996 and $12.83 million in 2005) to survivors and displaced residents.

sources.title: 1985 MOVE bombing
Philadelphia Marks 30th Anniversary Of MOVE Bombing

theories.name: CIA Heart Attack Gun
theories.summary: The CIA developed a pistol-like device, the "nondiscernible microbionoculator," that fired a frozen dart of saxitoxin shellfish toxin capable of killing a target while leaving only a small puncture wound that could pass for a heart attack. CIA Director William Colby publicly displayed the weapon before the Senate's Church Committee in September 1975, part of broader hearings into CIA assassination programs.

evidence_for.text: The Church Committee's public hearings and reports on "Alleged Assassination Plots Involving Foreign Leaders" documented the CIA's toxin-weapons program and led to Executive Order 11905 banning U.S.-sanctioned political assassination.
Military.com recounts Colby's 1975 Church Committee testimony presenting the dart gun, developed by Dr. Nathan Gordon at Fort Detrick to fire a frozen saxitoxin pellet from a modified M1911 pistol.

evidence_against: It is undocumented whether the device was ever actually used against a real target, as opposed to being developed and stockpiled.

sources.title: Church Committee
The CIA's 'Heart Attack Gun': A Cold War Weapon for Targeted Assassinations

theories.name: 1984 Rajneeshee Bioterror Attack
