Fact-checkers found no such formal oil-for-dollars agreement ever existed; the actual expiring pact was the 1974 US-Saudi Joint Commission on Economic Cooperation, an economic and military cooperation framework with no textual dollar-pricing requirement for oil.

evidence_for.text: A real US-Saudi economic pact, the 1974 Joint Commission on Economic Cooperation, did lapse on June 9, 2024, giving the claim a kernel of a real, dated event to attach to.

evidence_against.text: A GAO official confirmed the 1974 Joint Commission contained no formal agreement requiring oil to be traded in US dollars.
Multiple fact-checks found no documentary evidence of any formal 'petrodollar agreement' ever existing between the US and Saudi Arabia.
Saudi Arabia's practice of pricing oil in dollars has always been an informal market practice, not a binding contractual obligation that could 'expire.'

sources.title: Did a deal between Saudi Arabia and US to sell oil in dollars expire?
Did Saudi Arabia End its 'Petrodollar' Agreement With the US?
Petrodollar Panic: Separating Fact from Fiction

theories.name: SPR Drained to Rig Elections
theories.summary: Republican officials claim the Biden administration's release of roughly 180 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve between April and October 2022, which dropped the reserve to a roughly 40-year low, was primarily a deliberate political manipulation timed to suppress gas prices ahead of the 2022 midterm elections rather than a genuine emergency response, and that the reserve was left dangerously depleted afterward without adequate refilling.

evidence_for.text: The 2022 SPR releases were the largest in the reserve's history and coincided closely with the run-up to the November midterm elections.
Senator Tom Cotton and House committees formally accused the administration of draining the reserve for political gain and demanded answers.
The reserve fell to its lowest level since the 1980s and was not fully refilled in following years, including under the subsequent Trump administration.

evidence_against.text: The initial and largest tranche of releases followed Russia's invasion of Ukraine and an associated global oil price spike, a documented supply emergency independent of the midterms.

Energy analysts noted that falling prices in late 2022 were substantially attributable to demand destruction from high prices themselves, not the SPR releases, weakening the claim that releases were the deciding political lever.

sources.title: Biden Drains the Strategic Petroleum Reserve
Cotton charges Biden drained strategic oil stockpile for political gain
Does the President Control Gas Prices?
Trump hit Biden for tapping emergency oil stockpile but isn't refilling it

theories.name: US Sabotage of Nord Stream
theories.summary: Investigative journalist Seymour Hersh claimed in February 2023, citing a single anonymous source with purported direct knowledge of the operation, that US Navy divers planted C4 explosives on the Nord Stream pipelines during the June 2022 BALTOPS 22 NATO exercise, detonated via a Norwegian aircraft's sonar buoys in September 2022 on President Biden's direct order, to permanently cut Germany's dependence on Russian gas. Competing reporting, including from the New York Times and German investigators, instead points toward a pro-Ukrainian group as the more likely saboteurs; Ukraine's government denies involvement, and no state has been officially confirmed responsible.

evidence_for.text: Hersh's account cites explosives allegedly planted during the publicly documented BALTOPS 22 NATO exercise, with detonation timed to a real Norwegian P8 surveillance flight.
Biden and other officials made public statements before the war (e.g. 'there will no longer be a Nord Stream 2') that critics cite as motive evidence.

evidence_against.text: Hersh's article is based entirely on a single anonymous source and provides no documentary or physical evidence for its central claims.
A separate, differently-sourced New York Times investigation instead pointed to a pro-Ukrainian group as the likely perpetrators, a claim Ukraine's government denies.
No government has officially confirmed responsibility, and the two competing anonymously-sourced narratives (US-ordered vs. pro-Ukrainian group) remain unreconciled years later.

sources.title: How America Took Out The Nord Stream Pipeline
All at sea: Seymour Hersh and his Nord Stream sabotage story
Russia Calls for U.N. Investigation of Nord Stream Attack, as Hersh Accuses White House of False Flag
Pipes and Mirrors: the Nord Stream Explosions

theories.research_note: Checked against the atlas id list; no existing 'nord-stream' entry was found, so this fills that gap.

theories.name: TACO Trade Insider Trading
aliases: Great Time To Buy Tweet Scandal
theories.summary: Claims that Trump and/or people around him traded on advance knowledge of his own April 2025 tariff policy reversal — after unveiling sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs on April 2 that crashed markets, Trump's financial disclosures later showed 327 stock purchases on April 8, followed by his 'THIS IS A GREAT TIME TO BUY!!!' post at 9:37am on April 9, roughly four hours before he announced a 90-day tariff pause that sent the S&P 500 up 9.5% in a single day. Congressional Democrats requested an SEC investigation; no charges have been filed, and there is no public evidence the purchases were made with foreknowledge of the specific pause timing.

evidence_for.text: Financial disclosures made public in 2026 confirmed 327 stock purchases on April 8, 2025, including Apple, Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft, the day before the tariff-pause announcement.
Trump posted 'THIS IS A GREAT TIME TO BUY!!! DJT' at 9:37am, roughly four hours before announcing the 90-day tariff pause that drove a $4 trillion single-day market rebound.
Over a dozen House Democrats led by Rep. Maxine Waters formally requested an SEC, GAO, and Inspector General investigation into potential insider trading.

evidence_against.text: No evidence has been made public that the president or advisers traded specifically on foreknowledge of the pause's timing rather than a general market view.
Republican lawmakers, including Sen. John Cornyn, publicly dismissed the insider-trading characterization as unfounded.

sources.title: Trump Made 327 Stock Purchases the Day Before He Announced 'This Is a Great Time To Buy' and Paused Tariffs
Trump bought Apple, Nvidia and other tech giants before tariff reversal fueled rebound
Democrats question whether Trump and his allies engaged in insider trading on tariffs
Trump told investors to 'buy' on social media hours before his tariff pause rose stocks, raising questions about manipulation

theories.research_note: The factual timeline (purchases, tweet, pause, disclosure) is now documented via official financial disclosures released in mid-2026; the 'insider trading' legal conclusion remains unproven and no charges have been filed.

theories.name: Tariffs as Manufactured Wealth Transfer
theories.summary: Claims that the 2025 tariff shock-and-reversal cycle was a deliberately engineered volatility event designed to transfer wealth from ordinary investors, small businesses, and consumers to billionaires and Trump-aligned insiders positioned to buy the post-crash dip, citing the roughly $304 billion single-week wealth gain by the world's top billionaires following the tariff pause and the closeness of billionaire officials Treasury Secretary Bessent and Commerce Secretary Lutnick to the reversal decision.

evidence_for.text: The world's wealthiest people gained a collective $304 billion in the days after the tariff pause was announced, with Musk and Zuckerberg among the largest gainers.
Reporting indicates the reversal followed direct discussions with billionaire officials Bessent and Lutnick conveying Wall Street's alarm at the crash.
Commentators noted the episode coincided with deepening integration of tech oligarchs into administration policymaking, including the $500 billion Stargate initiative announced with Sam Altman.

evidence_against.text: Billionaire investors and executives were themselves vocal, public critics of the tariffs before the pause, arguing against a coordinated benefit narrative.
Ordinary index-fund and 401(k) holders also benefited proportionally from the market rebound, undercutting a narrow 'wealth transfer to insiders only' framing.

sources.title: 'They Are Laughing at Us All': Trump Tariff Whiplash Boosts Wealth of Mega-Rich by $304 Billion
Trump Tariffs: Wall Street Billionaires Criticize Policy as Market Declines
Trump's tariff pull-back and the class character of the capitalist state

theories.name: TikTok's Fake Divestiture / Surveillance Access Fight
theories.summary: Claims split in two directions: (1) that the January 2026 US divestiture deal for TikTok is largely cosmetic, leaving ByteDance and by extension the Chinese government with continued effective influence over the app's ranking algorithm and update pathway despite new US-majority ownership on paper; and (2) conversely, that the entire national-security framing was less about genuine Chinese-government data access and more a pretext for the US government itself (or connected firms like Oracle) to gain privileged access to and leverage over Americans' data and a dominant social platform.

evidence_for.text: ByteDance disclosed few technical details of the divestiture, and Congress received no briefing on whether the algorithm was verifiably free of Chinese influence before the deal closed.

Senator Ed Markey publicly criticized the lack of transparency, saying it 'reeks,' specifically questioning whether the algorithm was truly free of Chinese influence under the new ownership structure.
Analysis of the settlement's structure describes it as solving for 'jurisdictional optics' (US board, US hosting) while leaving underlying technical dependency on ByteDance's systems ambiguous.

evidence_against.text: The deal legally reduced ByteDance's ownership stake to under 20%, with the app operated by a newly incorporated US-controlled joint venture, TikTok USDS.

The original national-security rationale is grounded in a real, documented legal mechanism: China's 2017 National Intelligence Law compels domestic firms to cooperate with state intelligence work, and leaked internal TikTok discussions showed China-based staff had tracked specific US users' locations.

sources.title: Efforts to ban TikTok in the United States
US Power Play Over TikTok Did Nothing to Protect Americans
TikTok on the Clock: National Security, Forced Divestiture, and the Limits of the First Amendment
Saving TikTok While Protecting National Security

theories.name: Chip Export Controls: Leaky By Design
theories.summary: Claims that US semiconductor export controls aimed at restricting China's access to advanced AI chips are deliberately porous — either because chipmakers like Nvidia lobby to preserve profitable channels or because regulators tacitly tolerate circumvention — pointing to documented cases where Huawei used shell companies to get Taiwan's TSMC to fabricate roughly 2 million Ascend 910 AI chiplets despite Entity List restrictions, and to PRC fabs tied to Huawei that were flagged to regulators but never added to the restricted list.

evidence_for.text: The Commerce Department's Bureau of Industry and Security assessed in May 2025 that Huawei had developed its Ascend chips in violation of US export controls, using overseas foundries reliant on US technology.
Huawei reportedly used shell companies to have TSMC unknowingly manufacture around 2 million Ascend 910 chiplets.
An ITIF report found that export controls, rather than fully blocking Huawei, in some respects backfired and helped Huawei while hurting US firms' market position.

evidence_against.text: The Commerce Department has actively amended rules to close identified loopholes as they are discovered, indicating a pattern of enforcement-and-adaptation rather than deliberate permissiveness.
Analysts attribute circumvention primarily to the inherent difficulty of controlling millions of small, easily concealed chips across complex global supply chains, not to intentional design.

sources.title: Backfire: Export Controls Helped Huawei and Hurt U.S. Firms
U.S. issues worldwide crackdown on using Huawei Ascend chips, says it violates export controls
The Limits of Chip Export Controls in Meeting the China Challenge

theories.name: Chinese Spy Cranes at US Ports
theories.summary: Claims that Chinese-made ship-to-shore cranes, manufactured by Shanghai Zhenhua Heavy Industries (ZPMC), which supplies roughly 80% of cranes at US ports, contain hidden hardware allowing Beijing to surveil or potentially remotely disable US port operations during a crisis. A 2024 congressional investigation found more than a dozen unrequested cellular modems wired into crane control systems at several ports, connected to onboard Linux computers in ways investigators said could bypass firewalls; ZPMC denies any capability or intent to disrupt port operations and says its own internal probe found no hidden modems.

evidence_for.text: A House Homeland Security Committee investigation found more than 12 cellular modems in Chinese-made cranes used at US ports, some installed without ports having requested them.
Investigators described the modems as connecting to onboard Linux computers in ways that created 'an obscure method to collect information and bypass firewalls.'
ZPMC has repeatedly requested remote access to its ship-to-shore cranes operating at various US ports, a request lawmakers say raises disruption concerns beyond passive surveillance.

evidence_against.text: ZPMC's own internal investigation reported it found no evidence of the hidden modems and maintains it lacks the capability, intent, or motive to disrupt US port operations.

One port confirmed the modems were installed in 2017 for legitimate remote maintenance-monitoring functions and had already been removed by 2023 once flagged, which is consistent with a security lapse rather than an active, ongoing espionage operation.

sources.title: WTAS: Joint Investigation into CCP-Backed Company Supplying Cranes to U.S. Ports Reveals Shocking Findings
Congressional probe finds communications gear in Chinese cranes, raising spying concerns
Chinese crane firm denies posing security risk at US ports amid investigation
Why China's cargo cranes at U.S. ports pose espionage risk

theories.name: Fentanyl as Reverse Opium War
theories.summary: Claims that the Chinese Communist Party deliberately facilitates the flow of fentanyl precursor chemicals into the US as a form of intentional chemical warfare or historical revenge for the 19th-century Opium Wars. The framing was popularized by Secretary of State Marco Rubio in February 2025, who called it a 'reverse Opium War,' but when directly asked whether he had proof China was deliberately allowing the flow, Rubio acknowledged he did not. Researchers instead trace the pipeline mainly to profit-driven Chinese precursor chemical firms and Mexican trafficking networks, with, at most, lax Chinese state enforcement rather than a coordinated government weapons program.

evidence_for.text: Secretary of State Marco Rubio publicly suggested Beijing may be 'deliberately' flooding America with fentanyl as a 'reverse' Opium War.
The 'Reverse Opium War' framing predates Rubio's 2025 remarks, having circulated in commentary since at least 2023.

evidence_against.text: When asked directly if he 'knows' China deliberately allows fentanyl into the US, Rubio acknowledged he had no proof.

The Chinese Embassy in Washington called the claims 'groundless suspicions and smears,' and researchers found the fentanyl pipeline runs mainly through Mexican transnational criminal gangs sourcing precursors from small, profit-driven Chinese chemical firms rather than state-directed programs.
Analysis notes the US opioid crisis was already well underway before the fentanyl surge, undermining a narrative of a single orchestrated Chinese chemical-warfare campaign as its cause.

sources.title: Rubio accuses China of 'reverse' Opium War via fentanyl
The Reverse Opium War
What the Opium Wars can tell us about China, the U.S. and fentanyl

theories.research_note: Scored low per guardrails: the claim's proponent (Rubio) admitted lack of proof, and serious analysis attributes the pipeline to criminal/commercial actors rather than coordinated state policy; framed around the specific-actor (CCP policy) version rather than an ethnic-collective claim.

theories.name: Epstein Files Client-List Cover-Up
theories.summary: Proponents — spanning Epstein survivors' advocates, Democrats, and some MAGA-aligned figures — claim the DOJ and FBI are deliberately concealing a list of powerful clients who abused or paid for access to Epstein's victims, and that a July 2025 memo declaring no such list exists was a discretionary cover-up rather than a genuine null finding. The claim gained force because Attorney General Pam Bondi had said months earlier that a client list was 'sitting on my desk,' which the unsigned DOJ/FBI memo then contradicted. Congress passed the Epstein Files Transparency Act amid the backlash, and DOJ has released tranches of files while maintaining no chargeable evidence against third parties was found.

evidence_for.text: Bondi's February 2025 on-air claim that the Epstein client list was 'sitting on my desk to review' was directly contradicted five months later by an unsigned DOJ/FBI memo stating no such list exists.
The July 2025 memo stated investigators would release no further Epstein-related documents, a position DOJ reversed under sustained public and congressional pressure.
Congress passed the bipartisan Epstein Files Transparency Act in late 2025 compelling further disclosure, an unusual step if officials believed no further material existed.

CNN reported in February 2026 that FBI employees had compiled internal presentations cataloguing allegations against additional prominent names beyond Epstein and Maxwell that were not part of the public July 2025 memo.

evidence_against.text: The July 2025 memo affirmed the 2019 medical examiner's suicide finding and stated investigators found no evidence 'that could predicate an investigation against uncharged third parties' — an absence-of-evidence conclusion, not an admission of suppression.
Ghislaine Maxwell's 2021 conviction and subsequent DOJ interviews with her did not yield charges against additional individuals despite years of investigation and multiple grand juries.
Career DOJ and FBI officials across both the Biden and first Trump administrations reached similar conclusions about the case file, cutting against a single-administration cover-up theory.

theories.steelman: The documented public reversal — from a sitting Attorney General claiming a list was on her desk to an unsigned memo denying one exists — is a real, unexplained contradiction that would be a legitimate basis for scrutiny even absent proof of what is being protected.

sources.title: Epstein files
DOJ releases memo on sex offender Jeffrey Epstein files
DOJ releases huge set of Epstein files with many mentions of Trump
FBI employees compiled allegations against other men amid Epstein files pressure
Blumenthal letter to DOJ Inspector General on Epstein release

theories.research_note: Reflects an ongoing, still-unfolding document release as of mid-2026; scoring rewards the real documented flip-flop without treating it as proof of an active client-list suppression.

theories.name: Epstein as Intelligence-Asset Blackmail Operation
theories.summary: Some journalists, former officials, and online commentators claim Jeffrey Epstein was not an independent financier but an intelligence asset — most often alleged to be run by Israel's Mossad, sometimes by the CIA — who used blackmail material (recordings, photographs) gathered at his properties to compromise powerful visitors on behalf of a foreign or domestic intelligence service. The claim rests substantially on a single confidential FBI source and on statements by a former Israeli intelligence officer; both Israeli officials and the FBI document itself caution the claims are uncorroborated.

evidence_for.text: A declassified 2020 FBI memo records a confidential human source alleging Epstein was 'trained as a spy' by Ehud Barak and became a 'co-opted Mossad asset,' with properties allegedly wired for video to generate blackmail material.

Former Israeli intelligence officer Ari Ben-Menashe has stated publicly, including to journalists preparing the book 'Epstein: Dead Men Tell No Tales,' that Epstein and Maxwell ran a Mossad honeytrap operation.

U.S. Attorney Alexander Acosta reportedly told 2016 Trump transition officials he had been informed Epstein 'belonged to intelligence' and to leave the case alone, though he later disputed characterizations of that account.

evidence_against.text: The FBI memo itself explicitly labels the claims 'raw, unverified' from a single source, with no corroborating documentary or forensic evidence of espionage training or tasking presented.
Former Israeli Prime Minister Naftali Bennett called the Mossad claim 'categorically and totally false' and stated with '100% certainty' Epstein had no ties to Israeli intelligence.
No court proceeding, congressional inquiry, or declassified record has produced primary evidence — recruitment paperwork, tasking orders, handler communications — of an intelligence relationship.

sources.title: Alleged connections between Jeffrey Epstein and intelligence agencies
Former Israeli Leader Denies Epstein Worked for Mossad
Jeffrey Epstein intelligence asset theories gain momentum
Was Jeffrey Epstein also a Mossad spy?

theories.name: 2024 New Jersey Drone Wave Cover-Up
theories.summary: Beginning in November 2024, thousands of witnesses across New Jersey, New York and Pennsylvania reported nightly drone sightings near military bases, reservoirs and power infrastructure. Prominent figures — including a sitting member of Congress and guests on Joe Rogan's podcast — speculated the government was flying and concealing the drones' purpose, searching for lost nuclear material, or hiding a foreign incursion. Federal agencies (FBI, DHS, FAA, Pentagon) stated the sightings were overwhelmingly manned aircraft and authorized commercial or hobbyist drones misidentified at night, while separately acknowledging a pattern of unauthorized drone incursions over some defense installations.

evidence_for.text: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene publicly stated her belief that 'the government is in control of the drones,' and Pentagon officials separately confirmed a genuine, concurrent pattern of unauthorized drone overflights of sensitive military sites.
New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy stated the continued reporting of drone activity had prompted 'an outcropping of conspiracy theories' after being briefed, without fully ruling out unexplained sightings.
The federal government never issued a comprehensive public accounting identifying every reported sighting, leaving a documented information gap that outlasted the panic itself.

evidence_against.text: A New York Times visual analysis of dozens of viral drone videos found the large majority were conventional aircraft — planes and helicopters — misidentified due to night lighting and viewing angle.
The FBI, DHS, FAA and DOD issued a rare joint statement affirming no evidence of foreign involvement, malicious activity, or national security threat from the reported sightings.
No debris, intercepted drone, or physical evidence tying the sightings to a coordinated foreign or covert domestic program was ever produced.

sources.title: 2024 United States drone sightings
Mystery New Jersey Drones Spark Baseless Conspiracy Theories
What do we know about mystery drones flying over New Jersey?
As mystery surrounding drone sightings deepens, conspiracy theories take flight

theories.name: Trump Butler Rally Shooting: Staged or Allowed
theories.summary: After a gunman grazed Donald Trump's ear at a July 13, 2024 rally in Butler, Pennsylvania, two opposing conspiracy narratives emerged: some (initially mostly anti-Trump commentators, later some disaffected MAGA figures) claim the shooting was staged or faked to generate sympathy and boost his campaign, while others (including Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene) claim the opposite — that the Secret Service deliberately stood down or that shooter Thomas Crooks did not act alone, amounting to an allowed attempt covered up by federal agencies. Bipartisan congressional task forces, a Secret Service internal review, and an FBI investigation spanning both the Biden and Trump administrations all concluded Crooks acted alone and that the near-miss resulted from documented security lapses, not deliberate facilitation or staging.

evidence_for.text: Independent Secret Service and congressional task force reviews substantiated a real, serious security failure — an unsecured rooftop with direct line of sight to the stage — that went unexplained for months, fueling suspicion of deliberate negligence.
Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene has stated publicly that the government has not fully disclosed Crooks' digital communications or any associates, calling it a 'cover up.'
The Secret Service director resigned within weeks of the shooting specifically because internal review found the rooftop coverage failure was serious and preventable.

evidence_against.text: Bipartisan House and Senate task force investigations, spanning both the Biden and Trump administrations, concluded Crooks acted alone with no accomplices or evidence of a stand-down order.
Dozens of contemporaneous eyewitness photographs and videos, along with hospital and forensic records, are consistent with an actual gunshot wound rather than a staged event.

Multiple Secret Service agents faced discipline specifically because investigators found genuine, non-deliberate failures (inadequate rooftop assignment, communication breakdowns) rather than orchestrated facilitation.

sources.title: Attempted assassination of Donald Trump in Pennsylvania
Conspiracy theories arise around the near misses for Trump in Butler and Washington
MAGA Increasingly Believes Trump Assassination Attempt Was Fake
Fact-checking the wild conspiracy theories related to the attempted Trump assassination

theories.name: Grusch UAP Crash-Retrieval Program Testimony
theories.summary: Former intelligence officer David Grusch testified under oath to Congress in July 2023 that the U.S. government operates a decades-long, compartmentalized program that has retrieved crashed 'non-human' craft and biological material, and that this program was deliberately hidden from congressional oversight. His claims, based on interviews with roughly 40 alleged witnesses rather than firsthand observation, sparked further whistleblower activity and legislative pushes for disclosure. The Pentagon's All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) investigated and publicly reported finding no evidence corroborating a crash-retrieval or reverse-engineering program.

evidence_for.text: Grusch testified under oath before Congress — a setting carrying perjury risk — and a Department of Defense Inspector General review found his complaint met the threshold for a credible and urgent whistleblower concern warranting further investigation.

Grusch stated his information came from over 40 individuals with direct knowledge of classified programs, some of whom he said provided documentation and photography he described as compelling but did not make public.
His testimony prompted a bipartisan push in Congress for a UAP Disclosure Act and additional witnesses coming forward at follow-up 2024-2025 hearings with related, still-unverified accounts.

evidence_against.text: The Pentagon's AARO released a report stating it found no verifiable evidence of extraterrestrial craft, crash retrievals, or reverse-engineering programs after reviewing decades of government records and interviewing hundreds of individuals.
Grusch has acknowledged he never personally saw a craft, body, or biological material — his claims are secondhand, sourced entirely from other people's accounts.
No physical evidence, photograph, or document substantiating a 'non-human biologics' recovery has been produced publicly or, according to AARO, privately to investigators.

theories.steelman: A sworn congressional whistleblower complaint that a DoD Inspector General found credible enough to merit investigation is a materially stronger evidentiary starting point than most UFO claims, even though the underlying program has not been independently verified.

Whistleblower testifies U.S. salvaged 'non-human biologics' from UFO crash sites
UFO hearing key takeaways

theories.name: Maui Fires Directed-Energy Weapon / Land-Grab Theory
theories.summary: Following the August 2023 Lahaina, Maui wildfires that killed 102 people, social media users claimed the fires were deliberately ignited by satellite- or aircraft-mounted directed-energy weapons, often paired with a claim that the disaster was engineered or exploited to clear land for outside redevelopment and displace Native Hawaiian communities. State and federal investigators attributed the fire to downed power lines igniting drought-stressed invasive grasses in high winds, with no evidence of any energy weapon.

evidence_for.text: Some viral photographs showed selectively burned structures next to untouched vegetation, which proponents argued was inconsistent with an ordinary wildfire's burn pattern.
Hawaii's governor publicly pledged to block opportunistic land purchases in Lahaina after real, documented reports of investors and speculators contacting fire victims to buy their properties.

Hawaiian Electric acknowledged its power lines likely ignited the fire and faced billions of dollars in resulting litigation, confirming genuine utility-company culpability that fed broader distrust of official narratives.

evidence_against.text: Fact-checkers traced the core 'laser burn' photographs circulating online to unrelated images posted since at least January 2018, predating the fire entirely.
Fire investigators attributed the selective burn patterns to standard wildfire physics — building materials, defensible space, and wind-driven embers — not directed energy.
No physical, sensor, or satellite evidence of directed-energy weapon deployment has been produced by any investigator, journalist, or whistleblower.

sources.title: Conspiracy theories claim the Maui disaster was caused by direct energy weapons
Were 2023 Maui Fires Caused by a 'Direct Energy Weapon'?
High Winds, Drought Conditions Led to Maui Fires, No Evidence Intentionally Set

theories.name: East Palestine Derailment Cover-Up
theories.summary: After a Norfolk Southern train carrying vinyl chloride derailed and was controlled-burned in East Palestine, Ohio in February 2023, residents and online commentators alleged that federal and state officials, working with the railroad, understated the toxic exposure, suppressed cancer-cluster and wildlife die-off data, and steered the narrative to protect Norfolk Southern from liability. Investigations documented genuine communication failures and a contested controlled-burn decision, but produced no proof officials knowingly concealed a larger toxic release than reported.

evidence_for.text: The decision to conduct a controlled 'vent and burn' of vinyl chloride was made partly on Norfolk Southern contractors' advice and against some outside chemists' warnings that a burn was unnecessary and would generate dioxins, a documented conflict of interest.
It took nearly two weeks after the derailment for Ohio officials to advise residents on water safety, a documented communication gap residents and journalists characterized as an 'information void.'
A NewsNation investigation found some residents and experts felt officials wanted the East Palestine story to 'go away' before health effects were fully assessed.

evidence_against.text: EPA, CDC/ATSDR, and independent university air-monitoring teams conducted extensive testing and published contaminant data below acute-harm thresholds in most sampled areas, rather than suppressing it.
No cancer cluster tied to the derailment has been confirmed by state or federal health agencies, despite ongoing resident concern and monitoring.
Congressional hearings, an NTSB investigation, and a DOJ/EPA settlement with Norfolk Southern produced public findings and financial penalties rather than a suppressed record.

sources.title: East Palestine, Ohio, train derailment
Extremists Co-opt Norfolk Southern Train Derailment
How Ohio train derailment conspiracy theories filled the info void
East Palestine train crash investigators hunt for truth, as theories flourish

theories.name: Cloud Seeding Blamed for Dubai and Texas Floods
theories.summary: After record rainfall flooded Dubai in April 2024 and after the deadly July 2025 Texas Hill Country floods, online commentators and some state legislators claimed cloud seeding or a broader covert solar-geoengineering program caused or worsened the disasters, proving large-scale weather modification is already secretly underway. The claim helped drive a fast-moving 2024-2025 U.S. state legislative wave — Tennessee (2024) and Florida (2025) banned geoengineering and weather modification outright, and over twenty more states introduced similar bills — even though atmospheric scientists say cloud seeding cannot generate rainfall anywhere near the volume recorded in either event.

evidence_for.text: The UAE operates a real, publicly acknowledged cloud-seeding program, and UAE aircraft flew seeding missions in the days before the April 2024 Dubai deluge, a documented, non-secret fact proponents cite.

Weather-modification and cloud-seeding programs are real and legally regulated across dozens of U.S. states (irrigation districts, ski resorts), giving the broader 'weather modification exists' claim a true factual core distinct from the flood-causation claim.

Tennessee's SB 2691 (2024) and Florida's 2025 law criminalizing unauthorized atmospheric dispersal passed with substantial legislative support, showing the belief reached elected officials rather than remaining confined to social media.

evidence_against.text: Atmospheric scientists calculate cloud seeding can only marginally enhance rainfall from clouds already forming; it cannot manufacture the volume of rain recorded in Dubai (over a year's worth in a day) or the Texas Hill Country flash flood.

Meteorologists attributed the Dubai storm to an unusually intense, naturally occurring low-pressure system and the Texas floods to a stalled, tropical-moisture-fed storm cell, both independently documented by satellite and radar data.
No cloud-seeding aircraft or operation was active anywhere near the Texas flood zone at the time of the July 2025 disaster, according to weather-modification registries.

sources.title: What is cloud seeding? Here's what it can — and can't — do to the weather
Dubai floods and cloud seeding
Conspiracy theorists said 'cloud seeding' caused the Texas floods. It did not.
Why it's so hard to bust the weather control conspiracy theory
A Growing Number of US States Consider Bills to Ban Geoengineering

theories.name: 'Died Suddenly' Vaccine Excess-Death Movement
theories.summary: Beginning in late 2022, a movement centered on the viral film 'Died Suddenly' and adjacent social-media campaigns claims COVID-19 vaccines are causing a hidden epidemic of sudden deaths in young, healthy people and a wave of unusually aggressive 'turbo cancers,' and that public health agencies, medical examiners, and media are suppressing or miscoding the true death toll to protect vaccination campaigns. This is distinct from the earlier, near-universally rejected 'vaccine microchip' theory; here proponents point to genuine published excess-mortality statistics and argue the increases are being wrongly attributed to COVID-19 itself or other causes.

evidence_for.text: Multiple preprint and peer-reviewed analyses (including in Japan and parts of Europe) have documented periods of unexplained all-cause excess mortality following mass vaccination campaigns that some researchers argue is not fully accounted for by COVID-19 deaths alone.

The CDC's own surveillance systems documented a genuine, officially acknowledged rise in myocarditis among young men following mRNA vaccination, giving the broader movement a real mechanism to build from.
Some countries' vaccine injury compensation programs have paid claims related to post-vaccination cardiac events, providing official, if narrow, acknowledgment of harm.

evidence_against.text: 'Turbo cancer' is not a recognized medical diagnosis; oncologists and fact-checkers found no peer-reviewed evidence vaccines cause new, faster-growing cancer types, with cited cases remaining anecdotal rather than causal.
Large controlled studies found vaccinated populations had markedly lower all-cause mortality than unvaccinated populations during the same periods, the opposite of the movement's core claim.

Independent epidemiologists attribute most pandemic-era excess mortality to COVID-19 infection itself, delayed medical care, and drug overdoses rather than vaccination, based on death-certificate and hospitalization data.

sources.title: COVID-19 Vaccines Have Not Been Shown to Cause 'Turbo Cancer'
Do COVID-19 vaccines cause 'turbo cancer'?
Excess COVID-19-associated deaths among the unvaccinated population
Assessment of Risk for Sudden Cardiac Death After COVID-19 Vaccine — Oregon

theories.research_note: The movement blends a real, acknowledged rare side effect (myocarditis) and contested excess-mortality data with the unsubstantiated 'turbo cancer' and mass-death claims; sources above are mixed academic/fact-check to reflect that split.

theories.name: CrowdStrike Outage Sabotage Theory
theories.summary: After a faulty CrowdStrike software update crashed millions of Windows computers worldwide on July 19, 2024 — grounding flights and disabling hospitals and banks — online commentators claimed the outage was deliberate: state-sponsored sabotage, an inside job by a disgruntled employee, a staged event covering up a real cyberattack, or an intentionally planted government surveillance backdoor. CrowdStrike, Microsoft, and independent researchers who examined the faulty file all attributed it to an ordinary, if severe, software-validation failure.

evidence_for.text: The scale and simultaneity of the outage — roughly 8.5 million Windows devices crashing worldwide within hours — was unprecedented for an unintentional bug, which proponents cite as evidence accident alone seemed implausible.
CrowdStrike's own post-incident review acknowledged its testing process failed to catch a defective content update before global deployment, a real, serious internal control failure.
Congress held hearings in September 2024 grilling CrowdStrike executives, an unusual level of scrutiny some proponents cited as evidence something more than an accident was suspected.

evidence_against.text: CrowdStrike CEO George Kurtz testified under oath to Congress that the incident was conclusively not the result of a cyberattack, a statement carrying legal liability if false.
Independent security researchers who reverse-engineered the faulty driver file found a straightforward coding defect, not evidence of intentional malicious code or a backdoor.
No competitor, government agency, or insider has been identified, charged, or credibly implicated in deliberately causing the failure in the time since the incident.

sources.title: 2024 CrowdStrike-related IT outages
CrowdStrike outage resurfaces debunked Trump conspiracy theory
What is CrowdStrike and how is it linked to the global outage?
Congress grills CrowdStrike about multibillion-dollar July outage

theories.name: Biden Body Double / Deepfake Theory
theories.summary: Proponents claim President Joe Biden was, at various points from 2020 through his 2024 withdrawal from the race, secretly replaced in public appearances by a body double, an actor, or an AI-generated deepfake, to conceal severe cognitive or physical decline. The theory intensified around Biden's halting June 2024 debate performance and subsequent withdrawal, with commentators citing inconsistencies in ear shape, gait, or voice as 'proof,' though fact-checkers traced the clips to lighting, camera angles, and editing artifacts rather than substitution.

evidence_for.text: Biden's widely-viewed June 2024 debate performance was visibly halting and confused enough that Democratic allies and donors publicly called for him to withdraw, a real, documented event that gave the broader 'something is being hidden' narrative genuine grounding.

Reporting after Biden left office (including the book 'Original Sin') documented that senior aides managed his schedule and public appearances specifically to minimize exposure of his declining stamina, a real practice of image management.
Side-by-side photo comparisons circulated online showing apparent differences in ear lobes, moles, or hairline across appearances, presented by proponents as forensic evidence.

evidence_against.text: No forensic, biometric, or voice-print analysis by any credible outlet has found evidence of a body double or deepfake substitution; fact-checkers traced viral comparison clips to different lighting, lenses, and video compression.

Biden was accompanied at all times by Secret Service, press pools, and staff who would need to be complicit in an enormous, leak-proof conspiracy sustained across years and thousands of public appearances.

The proximate, publicly documented explanation for Biden's 2024 withdrawal — age-related decline reported contemporaneously by numerous journalists and aides — is fully consistent with the observed facts without requiring substitution.

sources.title: A coup, fake signatures and deepfakes are the latest conspiracy theories about 2024
Is Joe Biden Dead, Replaced by 10 Different Deepfake Body Doubles? An Investigation
Joe Biden's Eyes in Video Spark Wild Conspiracy Theories

theories.name: The Autopen Presidency
theories.summary: Republican lawmakers and the Trump administration allege that during his final year in office Joe Biden was so cognitively diminished that he did not personally authorize many pardons and executive actions issued in his name, and that unnamed senior staff instead used an autopen to sign directives — including a wave of preemptive pardons — without his knowledge or informed consent, effectively running the presidency themselves. A House Oversight Committee report (October 2025) endorsed this framing and called some Biden pardons 'void,' while Biden and his aides maintain every action was authorized and call the claim a politically motivated attack on a legal, longstanding practice.

evidence_for.text: Trump signed an executive order in June 2025 directing the Attorney General to formally investigate 'who ran the United States' during Biden's final year and his use of the autopen, initiating an official government inquiry rather than a purely online claim.

The House Oversight Committee's October 2025 report, based on staff interviews, concluded some autopen-signed executive actions were issued without adequate evidence Biden personally reviewed their contents, and called them 'void.'
Attorney General Pam Bondi opened a review of Biden pardons signed by autopen following the Oversight Committee's findings.

evidence_against.text: A 2005 Justice Department Office of Legal Counsel opinion holds that a president need not be physically present when a document is signed by autopen, meaning its use carries no inherent legal defect.

The top Democrat on the Oversight Committee stated witnesses testified Biden 'fully executed his duties as President,' including personally authorizing every executive order, pardon, and use of the autopen.

Biden's spokesperson said the investigation 'confirmed what has been clear from the start' — that he made the decisions of his presidency — and no recording or named firsthand witness has produced a specific pardon issued against Biden's actual wishes.

theories.steelman: Even setting aside the legal irrelevance of autopen use, contemporaneous reporting by multiple outlets describing a deliberate 'circle of protection' limiting unscripted access to Biden gives the underlying concern about diminished capacity a real evidentiary basis distinct from the autopen framing itself.

theories.name: AI Chatbots Trained to Push Propaganda
aliases: Grok Told to Reflect Musk's Views
theories.summary: Commentators across the political spectrum claim major AI chatbots are deliberately tuned by their parent companies to push a political viewpoint on users under cover of neutral assistance. Conservatives point to Google Gemini's 2024 image generator inserting diversity into historically inaccurate contexts as evidence of imposed left-leaning bias, while critics point to xAI's Grok being documented searching for 'what does Elon Musk think' before answering controversial questions, and briefly instructing itself in 2025 to treat 'white genocide' claims as fact after a leaked system-prompt change. Unlike the broader, harder-to-prove claim that all AI output is propaganda, these two specific cases involve company-acknowledged and reporter-documented instances of deliberate prompt engineering shaping chatbot political outputs.

evidence_for.text: TechCrunch documented Grok 4's chain-of-thought explicitly searching for 'Elon Musk's views' on topics like abortion and Israel-Palestine before formulating its answer, a reproducible, reported behavior.

xAI confirmed an 'unauthorized' system-prompt change caused Grok to reference 'white genocide' in South Africa as fact in unrelated conversations in May 2025, and xAI began publishing its system prompts publicly afterward as a remedial step.

Google acknowledged and apologized after its Gemini image generator systematically overrode historically-specific prompts (e.g., '1943 German soldier') with diverse-race outputs, confirming a real, deliberate fine-tuning choice.

evidence_against.text: Both documented incidents (Gemini's overcorrection, Grok's prompt leak) were acknowledged by the companies as unintended overcorrections or unauthorized changes rather than sanctioned campaigns to indoctrinate users.
No leaked internal directive, memo, or whistleblower account has surfaced instructing engineers to embed partisan messaging as an ongoing corporate policy at any major AI lab.
The two incidents cited point in opposite ideological directions — perceived-left at Google, perceived-right/Musk-aligned at xAI — undercutting a single unified 'AI industry propaganda' narrative.

'Crazy conspiracist' and 'unhinged comedian': Grok's AI persona prompts exposed

theories.name: Diddy 'List' Elite Blackmail Network Theory
theories.summary: After Sean 'Diddy' Combs was indicted on federal sex-trafficking and racketeering charges in 2024, online speculation — explicitly compared by journalists to Pizzagate and QAnon — claimed a secret 'list' of A-list celebrities and executives attended or were complicit in his 'freak-off' parties and are being protected from exposure by a Hollywood-and-media cover-up, echoing the Epstein 'client list' narrative. Named figures circulated widely on social media despite no court filing, indictment, or credible reporting linking them to wrongdoing; Combs's 2025 trial concerned specific conduct toward named accusers and ended in acquittal on the trafficking/racketeering counts with conviction only on lesser prostitution-transportation charges.

evidence_for.text: Combs was convicted in July 2025 on two federal counts of transportation to engage in prostitution, confirming some core criminal conduct alleged in the case, which proponents cite as proof the broader 'cover-up' framing has a real foundation.

Numerous celebrities were documented, through genuine photographs and past reporting, as having attended Combs's parties over decades, giving the 'guest list' speculation a real, if non-criminal, starting point.

Federal prosecutors stated the alleged criminal enterprise involved Combs's own employees as co-conspirators, confirming he did not act entirely alone, which proponents extrapolate into a wider network.

evidence_against.text: Combs was acquitted of the racketeering conspiracy and sex-trafficking charges that would have required proving a broader criminal enterprise involving other named individuals.
None of the celebrities named in viral social media posts have been charged, subpoenaed as suspects, or credibly linked to criminal conduct by prosecutors.

Multiple viral 'proof' items — including a fabricated song falsely attributed to Justin Bieber and claims that named celebrities fled the country — were explicitly debunked as fabricated or fictional content.

sources.title: Diddy, Jeffrey Epstein Cases and Conspiracy Theories Show Parallels
How Sean Diddy Combs Became a QAnon Conspiracy Theory

theories.name: Starlink Vote-Switching Theory (2024 Election)
theories.summary: After Kamala Harris's 2024 election loss, social media users — largely on the left, mirroring the structure of 2020's 'Stop the Steal' claims from the right — alleged Elon Musk used his Starlink satellite internet service to intercept or alter vote-tabulation data in swing states in Donald Trump's favor, with some versions adding that Musk coordinated with Vladimir Putin. Election security officials in multiple states and independent fact-checkers found no connection between Starlink and any vote-counting system.

evidence_for.text: Musk was a major, unusually visible financial and operational backer of the Trump campaign in 2024, including running get-out-the-vote operations in swing states, giving proponents a documented motive narrative.

Starlink terminals were documented in general use for communications support in some campaign and disaster-relief operations during the same period, a real fact proponents cite to suggest technical proximity, though without evidence of any tabulation link.

evidence_against.text: Election officials confirmed the vast majority of U.S. vote-counting machines and tabulators are never connected to the internet at all, making satellite-based tampering technically implausible for certified tabulation systems.

The only documented instance of Starlink being used in connection with election infrastructure nationally was in California, a state Trump lost decisively, undercutting the 'swing state rigging' claim.
State election officials in Georgia and other contested states publicly rebutted the claims and pointed to certified audits and hand recounts in select jurisdictions that matched machine tallies.

sources.title: Fact check: Was Elon Musk's Starlink used to rig the US election?
Musk's Starlink Was Not Connected to Vote Tabulation
Conspiracy theory spreading on social media about Starlink interfering with election results
Election officials in Georgia and other swing states knock down Starlink vote rigging conspiracy theories

theories.name: Noncitizen Voting Plot / SAVE Act Narrative
theories.summary: Donald Trump, Elon Musk, and allied Republican officials claimed ahead of and after the 2024 election that Democrats were deliberately recruiting and enabling large numbers of noncitizens to vote illegally in federal elections to steal the outcome, a claim used to justify the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register. Multiple state and federal reviews found documented noncitizen voting to be extraordinarily rare and non-coordinated, though not literally zero.

evidence_for.text: Post-election reviews identified isolated confirmed cases of noncitizen voting in several states, including 16 credible cases in Michigan out of 5.7 million votes cast, giving the claim a small kernel of real, documented fact.
The House of Representatives passed the SAVE Act in 2025 with Republican-majority support, showing the claim influenced actual federal legislation rather than remaining confined to online discourse.

Republican state officials sued the Biden-era DHS seeking noncitizen voter-roll data, and the resulting legal disputes over data access were real and documented, feeding the narrative that information was being withheld.

evidence_against.text: A comprehensive 2025 review found no evidence of any coordinated plot to register or turn out noncitizen voters, describing confirmed cases as isolated administrative or registration errors rather than intentional fraud.
The documented rate of suspected noncitizen voting nationally was calculated at roughly 0.0001% of ballots cast, far too small to affect any statewide result.
A Loyola Law School election-law expert and multiple state election directors stated no state uncovered any organized effort by any party to recruit noncitizen voters.

sources.title: The House has passed the Trump-backed SAVE Act. Here are 8 things to know

theories.name: Engineered Egg Scarcity / Bird Flu Culling Conspiracy
theories.summary: As avian-flu culling drove U.S. egg prices to record highs in 2024-2025, some commentators claimed the scarcity was substantially manufactured or exaggerated by large egg producers and regulators — either to gouge consumers through coordinated price-fixing, or to push the public toward alternatives such as lab-grown protein — rather than being a purely natural consequence of H5N1 outbreaks. The claim draws real strength from a prior, unrelated proven price-fixing case against major egg producers, though mainstream reporting attributes the 2024-2025 shortage overwhelmingly to actual mass bird die-offs and mandatory culling.

evidence_for.text: A federal jury in Illinois found major egg producers, including Cal-Maine Foods and Rose Acre Farms, liable in December 2023 for a prior conspiracy (1998-2008) to fix prices by deliberately constraining the national egg supply — a proven precedent for coordinated industry-wide scarcity engineering in this exact commodity.

Cal-Maine Foods reported profits rising more than sevenfold even as it stayed free of bird flu into December 2023, a documented disconnect between one major producer's profitability and the outbreak it later cited for high prices.

USDA culling protocols require destroying entire flocks at any sign of infection and then impose a lengthy, government-mandated repopulation process of nine months to over a year, a real regulatory bottleneck proponents argue is disproportionate.

evidence_against.text: USDA and industry data document that avian influenza killed well over 100 million egg-laying hens in the U.S. from 2022-2025, a real, verified mortality event sufficient on its own to explain the bulk of the price increase.

Economists and agricultural researchers attribute most of the 2024-2025 price spike to genuine supply shock from the outbreak and industry consolidation dynamics rather than a deliberately fabricated shortage.

No evidence has emerged that the 2024-2025 outbreak itself, as opposed to the separate and unrelated 2000s price-fixing case, involved deliberate underreporting or fabrication of bird deaths by producers or USDA.

theories.steelman: Because a federal jury already found the same industry conspired for a decade to restrict egg supply for profit, the argument that today's producers benefit from and may be slow to correct a supply shock is not pure speculation, even though it falls well short of proving the current outbreak is fabricated.

sources.title: Nestlé, Kraft defeat egg producers in price-fixing case
What's Driving High Egg Prices: Bird Flu or Corporate Greed?
Egg Prices: It's Not Just Bird Flu. It's Corporate Greed.

theories.name: Predictive Programming
theories.summary: The theory holds that films, television, and other mass media are used by elite planners to pre-condition the public to accept future societal changes or staged events, so that when they occur audiences respond with passive acceptance rather than resistance. Popularized by broadcaster Alan Watt in the early 2000s, it has since been applied retroactively to almost any film or show that seems to 'predict' a later real-world tragedy.
evidence_for: Some media genuinely foreshadows real events by coincidence or informed extrapolation (e.g., disaster films drawing on real epidemiological modeling), which proponents read as evidence of insider knowledge

Documented historical psy-ops (Operation Mockingbird, wartime propaganda films) show that governments have in fact used entertainment media to shape public opinion, lending surface plausibility to the broader claim
The sheer volume of media produced guarantees some works will appear to anticipate later events, and proponents catalogue these matches as a pattern worth investigating
evidence_against: No leaked document, whistleblower, or declassified program has ever described a coordinated 'predictive programming' initiative directed at conditioning the public for planned events
The theory is unfalsifiable and relies entirely on post-hoc pattern-matching (apophenia) between fictional works and unrelated later news stories
Media scholars note that fiction routinely explores plausible near-future scenarios for dramatic reasons, which produces coincidental hits without any coordinating conspiracy
The most-cited examples (e.g., cartoons 'predicting' events) rely on cherry-picked, often misremembered or digitally altered clips
theories.steelman: If media content were deliberately used to precondition public acceptance of policy outcomes, it would echo the documented Pentagon Military Analyst Program and the Nayirah testimony, both showing government and PR actors shaping ostensibly independent media narratives toward a desired public response. No evidence supports the broader claim that fictional media systematically encodes advance notice of future events.

theories.name: Targeted Individuals / Gang Stalking
theories.summary: Self-described 'Targeted Individuals' (TIs) believe they are subjected to a coordinated, long-term campaign of harassment, surveillance, and 'electronic torture' by a diffuse network of neighbors, police, and government agents acting in concert, with no single identifiable stalker. The community coalesced on internet forums in the early 2000s and has since built an extensive shared vocabulary of alleged tactics ('gang stalking,' 'voice-to-skull,' 'street theater').
evidence_for: Real historical precedents exist for coordinated, multi-agency harassment of civilians — COINTELPRO's FBI campaigns against activists genuinely used neighbors, informants, and staged 'street theater' to destabilize targets

Some documented individual stalking and workplace-mobbing cases share surface features with TI accounts (multiple people coordinating harassment), making the broader claim feel plausible to those affected

A 2020 clinical study found TIs report serious, measurable psychological and social harm regardless of the objective cause, suggesting the distress itself is real even where the attribution is contested

As mentioned in the “Hook Operators” theory, certain people can take control of others remotely through psychological means. Perhaps rather than certain innocuous people being employed by the government, they are instead being controlled by these.
evidence_against: Clinical research comparing TI reports to documented stalking cases found the pattern (diffuse, unfalsifiable, escalating in scope, no physical evidence) matches a recognized delusional syndrome; one study found all 128 'gang stalking' claimants examined met criteria for delusional disorder versus only 5 among individually-stalked victims
No investigation, leak, or whistleblower has ever produced operational documents for a mass, cross-agency civilian harassment program matching TI descriptions
The alleged tactics (radio-frequency 'voice to skull' harassment, neighborhood-wide coordination) are not supported by any known technology or logistics at the claimed scale
Online TI communities show clear contagion effects, with narratives and terminology spreading and standardizing across unrelated individuals via shared forums
theories.steelman: Government covert harassment of specific individuals is a proven pattern, documented in COINTELPRO's campaigns against the Black Panther Party and other domestic targets, which used surveillance, infiltration, and disruption against people the state deemed threats. No evidence supports the broader 'gang stalking' and 'electronic torture' claims of a diffuse civilian-and-police harassment network, which independent investigations have not corroborated.

theories.name: RFID Chip Implantation ('Mark of the Beast')
aliases: Mark of the Beast microchip theory
theories.summary: This theory holds that governments and corporations intend to make implantable RFID microchips mandatory for identification, payment, and tracking, fulfilling the Book of Revelation's prophecy of a 'mark' required to buy or sell. It grew out of 1990s fears about barcodes and national ID cards and intensified after companies like Applied Digital Solutions began marketing implantable VeriChips in the early 2000s.
evidence_for: Applied Digital Solutions did in fact develop and win FDA clearance for an implantable human RFID chip (VeriChip, cleared 2004) and marketed it for medical-record and payment access, showing the underlying technology and corporate intent are real

Some employers (e.g., a Wisconsin company in 2017) have offered or implemented voluntary employee RFID implants for building access and payment, demonstrating incremental real-world adoption of exactly the mechanism feared
Revelation 13:16-17 explicitly describes a hand/forehead mark required for commerce, giving the theory a textual anchor that predates and is independent of the technology itself
evidence_against: VeriChip and similar implants have always been opt-in and failed commercially — Applied Digital Solutions discontinued its human-implant business in 2010 for lack of demand, not because of any suppression

No government has proposed or enacted mandatory implantable identification tied to financial transactions; existing 'digital ID' and payment systems (chip cards, phones) remain external, not subdermal
The prophetic reading of Revelation 13 is a theological interpretation, not a verifiable historical or technological claim, and mainstream biblical scholarship disputes the literal 'RFID' reading
The passive RFID tags used in implants have short read ranges and no GPS capability, making them useless for the mass real-time tracking proponents describe

theories.name: Smartphones Are Secretly Listening for Ad Targeting
theories.summary: Widely held belief that smartphones covertly activate their microphones to listen to everyday conversations and feed keywords to advertising platforms like Facebook and Google, producing uncannily targeted ads. Roughly half of Americans report believing this and say they have received ads matching things they only spoke about aloud.
evidence_for: Cox Media Group circulated marketing materials describing an 'Active Listening' ad product that claimed to use device microphones to inform targeting, later pulled after backlash — showing at least one real ad-tech vendor pitched exactly this capability to clients
Facebook and other platforms hold patents describing methods for using ambient audio signals for content or ad delivery, indicating the underlying capability has been seriously explored internally
Millions of users report anecdotally specific, well-timed ad matches to spoken-but-not-typed/searched topics, a pattern consistent enough across unrelated users to demand explanation

App-permission audits have repeatedly found some apps requesting microphone access with vague justifications, and permission misuse for other data types has previously been confirmed (e.g., unauthorized background location tracking)

evidence_for.text: Cox Media Group's leaked pitch deck described an 'Active Listening' product using AI to capture real-time intent data from device microphones, and listed Facebook, Google, and Amazon as clients.

evidence_against: A 2018 Northeastern University study analyzed network traffic from over 17,000 Android apps and found none secretly transmitting audio without explicit user-triggered activation

Continuous audio streaming at scale would require roughly 130MB/day per user — an amount of covert bandwidth and server storage that would be both detectable by outside network researchers and prohibitively expensive across billions of devices
Facebook, Google, and Apple have all formally denied microphone-based ad targeting, and no forensic network analysis by outside security researchers has ever caught it happening

The 'coincidence' is well explained by the ordinary ad-tech ecosystem: cross-app tracking, location data, purchase histories, social-graph inference, and confirmation bias (people forget the many non-matching ads) account for the perceived accuracy

evidence_against.text: Meta, Amazon, and Google all denied involvement with CMG's Active Listening product; CMG subsequently said the product was discontinued. Researchers who investigated the claim found no systematic backdoor microphone access by major ad platforms.

sources.title: In Leak, Facebook Partner Brags About Listening to Your Phone's Microphone to Serve Ads - Futurism
Facebook ad partner admits eavesdropping on conversations via phone mics for ad targeting - TechSpot
Facebook ad partner Cox could listen to a user's conversations - AppleInsider

theories.steelman: If smartphones were covertly used to harvest behavioral signals for ad targeting, it would extend the documented pattern of Cambridge Analytica's covert psychological profiling built from platform data. Independent network-traffic audits have repeatedly failed to find evidence of always-on microphone eavesdropping specifically, so that particular mechanism remains unconfirmed even though covert data harvesting for targeting is proven elsewhere.

theories.name: Dead Internet Theory
theories.summary: The theory claims that since around 2016, most of the internet's traffic, content, and even social-media 'users' have been replaced by bots and AI-generated material, as part of a deliberate effort by governments and corporations to manipulate public perception and commerce. It originated in obscure imageboard posts (Wizardchan, 2019) and was popularized in a 2021 Agora Road's Macintosh Cafe forum post before spreading to mainstream tech coverage.
evidence_for: Independent bot-traffic reports (e.g., Imperva's annual 'Bad Bot Report') have repeatedly found automated traffic constitutes roughly half of all internet traffic in recent years, a real and measurable phenomenon

The rapid, verifiable rise of generative AI since 2022 means a large and growing share of new text, images, and even social posts genuinely is machine-generated, lending new teeth to a theory first floated before generative AI existed

Documented state and commercial astroturfing operations (troll farms, bot networks used in elections and marketing) confirm that inauthentic coordinated activity at scale is a real, if narrower, phenomenon
The internet's dominant platforms could employ vast bot networks to simulate human activity while conditioning the small real user population toward predetermined ideologies.

evidence_for.text: Independent bot-traffic reports (e.g., Imperva's annual Bad Bot Report) consistently find automated traffic constitutes roughly half of all internet traffic, providing a measurable basis for the theory's claims about non-human activity online.

evidence_against: The strong version of the theory — a centrally coordinated program to fake most human activity online — has no supporting leak, whistleblower, or document; the bot traffic that does exist is fragmented across many uncoordinated commercial and criminal actors, not a single conspiracy

Platforms publish (and outside auditors can partially verify) real human engagement metrics and ad-revenue-driven incentives to prove real audiences, and take enforcement action against bot networks, all of which cut against a hidden mass-replacement narrative

Much of the 'evidence' cited is subjective — a feeling that content feels formulaic or repetitive — which is also explained by algorithmic content optimization and platform homogenization rather than fakery
The theory's own originators on Wizardchan and Agora Road described it as speculative pattern-noticing rather than a documented finding

evidence_against.text: The theory's specific claim — that governments and corporations deliberately orchestrated a wholesale replacement of human users by coordinated AI since 2016 — has no corroborating evidence beyond anecdotal forum posts.

sources.title: Dead Internet theory - Wikipedia
The Dead Internet Theory, Explained - Forbes
What is the dead internet theory? - Live Science

theories.steelman: If a coordinated effort to flood the internet with bot-driven content to shape perception were occurring at the scale claimed, it would mirror the documented tactics of the Internet Research Agency's troll-and-bot campaigns and Cambridge Analytica's data-driven targeting. Bot traffic volume is independently measurable and substantial, but no evidence supports a single centrally directed program to 'replace' most human content as described.

theories.name: Bitcoin Was Created by a Government Agency
theories.summary: This theory holds that Bitcoin was not created by an independent pseudonymous programmer but was secretly designed and released by the NSA or CIA to eventually monitor, control, or replace global financial flows under the guise of decentralization. It draws on the fact that Bitcoin's SHA-256 hash function was designed by the NSA and that a 1996 NSA-affiliated paper discussed anonymous electronic cash.
evidence_for: The SHA-256 algorithm used in Bitcoin mining was indeed designed and published by the NSA in 2001, a genuine and documented technical link

A 1996 paper titled 'How to Make a Mint: The Cryptography of Anonymous Electronic Cash,' co-authored by NSA-linked researchers, predates Bitcoin by over a decade and discusses similar cryptographic e-cash concepts
Satoshi Nakamoto's true identity has never been conclusively established despite extensive investigative efforts, leaving a real evidentiary vacuum that invites speculation

Intelligence agencies have a documented history of covertly funding or steering supposedly independent technologies (e.g., Tor originated with U.S. Naval Research Laboratory funding), showing the general pattern is not unprecedented
evidence_against: SHA-256 is a public, widely audited cryptographic standard used across banking, government, and consumer software; using it implies nothing about who built any particular application, including Bitcoin

The 1996 'anonymous electronic cash' paper describes a centralized, bank-issued digital cash system fundamentally different from Bitcoin's decentralized, permissionless blockchain architecture — the technical lineage does not actually connect
No leaked document, whistleblower, or forensic analysis has ever tied Nakamoto's original code commits, writing style, or wallet activity to any intelligence agency
Bitcoin's early adoption was driven by cypherpunk and libertarian communities explicitly hostile to government monetary control, an odd outcome for an intelligence-agency product

evidence_against.text: The 1996 NSA paper describes centralized e-cash schemes and does not propose Bitcoin's decentralized blockchain architecture; no credible link between the NSA and Satoshi Nakamoto's identity has ever been established.

sources.title: No, the NSA Did Not Invent Bitcoin - Bitcoin News
NSA Paper Debunked As Bitcoin Origin Theory - ainvest
Did the NSA Invent Bitcoin? A 1996 Research Paper Reignites the Debate - Bitcoin News

theories.name: Gold Price / Gold Standard Suppression
theories.summary: This theory holds that central banks and major bullion banks have systematically suppressed the market price of gold — through gold leasing, derivatives, and coordinated sales — to conceal the weakness of fiat currencies since the US left the gold standard in 1971. It was formalized by the Gold Anti-Trust Action Committee (GATA), founded in 1999 by Bill Murphy and Chris Powell.
evidence_for: The London Gold Pool (1961-1968), in which Western central banks jointly intervened to hold gold at $35/oz, was a real, documented, and eventually failed coordinated gold-price-suppression scheme, establishing clear historical precedent

In 2020, JPMorgan paid a record $920 million to the DOJ, CFTC, and SEC after being found to have run an eight-year spoofing scheme manipulating gold and silver futures prices, with several individual traders criminally convicted — proof that precious-metals price manipulation by major banks is not merely theoretical

Central banks openly engage in large-scale gold leasing and swaps whose aggregate scale and purpose are not fully disclosed, which GATA argues creates a 'paper gold' overhang that suppresses the price signal
Multiple former regulators and bullion-market insiders cited by GATA have alleged coordinated below-market gold sales tied to defending currency credibility
evidence_against: The proven JPMorgan and other bank cases involved profit-driven trader-level spoofing schemes, not evidence of a coordinated central-bank conspiracy to suppress gold for monetary-system reasons

Mainstream economists attribute gold's price behavior to ordinary supply, demand, interest-rate, and currency-strength dynamics, and gold has in fact risen dramatically over the long run since 1971 (from $35 to well over $2,000/oz), undercutting claims of sustained successful suppression
GATA's specific claims about hidden central-bank gold reserves and leasing volumes have never been substantiated by an independent audit or leak
No systemic 'suppression' conspiracy has been confirmed by any regulator, despite the aggressive scrutiny that followed the JPMorgan prosecutions
theories.steelman: Major banks have been proven to collude in rigging benchmark prices for profit, as shown in the Libor and forex-rigging scandals, and gold/silver price-fixing panels faced comparable regulatory fines and lawsuits for trader collusion. That precedent supports the possibility of periodic gold-price manipulation for trading profit, though no comparable evidence supports the theory's broader claim of a coordinated central-bank campaign to defend fiat currency by suppressing gold prices.

theories.name: Death of Diana, Princess of Wales — Assassination Theory
theories.summary: This theory holds that Diana, Princess of Wales, was assassinated in the August 1997 Paris car crash — most prominently by British intelligence acting on behalf of the royal family — to prevent her marrying Dodi Fayed or exposing royal secrets, rather than dying in a genuine accident. It was chiefly promoted by Dodi's father, Mohamed Al-Fayed.
evidence_for: Diana herself reportedly told friends and wrote notes months before her death expressing fear that she would be killed in a staged car accident, a documented statement predating the crash

The driver, Henri Paul, had blood-alcohol levels far exceeding French legal limits despite witnesses describing him as sober shortly before departure, an unresolved discrepancy that fueled suspicion of a cover story

A mysterious bright flash reported by some witnesses and a still-unidentified white Fiat Uno that fled the scene were never definitively traced, leaving a genuine loose end in the physical reconstruction
The British royal family's historical secrecy and Diana's own adversarial relationship with the Palace gave the theory a plausible-sounding motive
evidence_against: Operation Paget, the multi-year Metropolitan Police investigation (2004-2006) that examined 175 separate conspiracy allegations across an 832-page report, concluded every one was unfounded and the deaths resulted from an accident
The 2008 British inquest jury returned a verdict of unlawful killing due to the 'gross negligence' of the driver (drunk, speeding) and pursuing paparazzi — not murder
Forensic testing on blood found at the scene showed no hCG hormone, ruling out the widely repeated claim that Diana was pregnant at the time
No credible evidence has ever linked Henri Paul to MI6, and toxicology and eyewitness accounts of his heavy drinking that night are well corroborated

theories.name: Elvis Presley Faked His Death
theories.summary: This theory holds that Elvis Presley did not die of heart failure in August 1977 but staged his death to escape the pressures of fame, possibly entering witness protection after aiding an FBI investigation. It spawned decades of purported sightings and a bestselling 1988 book by Gail Brewer-Giorgio.
evidence_for: Presley's full autopsy report was sealed by his family for 50 years (until 2027), which proponents read as concealment of anomalous findings rather than ordinary privacy protection

Some funeral attendees described the body in the casket as looking 'wax-like,' and Presley's middle name is spelled differently on his grave marker than on his birth certificate, both cited as tangible physical inconsistencies
Presley was known to have cooperated informally with federal drug-enforcement efforts in his final years, giving a kernel of truth to claims connecting him to FBI activity
Numerous self-reported sightings across decades created a large, self-reinforcing body of anecdotal 'evidence' that proponents treat cumulatively as significant
evidence_against: A formal autopsy was performed by multiple physicians immediately after death, and the official cause (cardiac arrhythmia linked to years of polypharmacy) is well documented in the released portions of the medical record
The grave-spelling discrepancy is fully explained: Presley used both 'Elvis Aaron' and 'Elvis Aron' interchangeably throughout his life, including on his own marriage license
A 1997 Gallup poll found only 4% of Americans believed Presley was alive, versus 93% certain he had died, reflecting the near-total absence of the theory even in the era it was most actively promoted
No verified sighting, DNA sample, or fingerprint match has ever substantiated any specific claim of Presley's survival in the 45+ years since his death

theories.name: Hitler Survived and Escaped to Argentina
theories.summary: This theory holds that Adolf Hitler did not die in his Berlin bunker in April 1945 but escaped, likely by U-boat, to Argentina, where he lived out his life under Peronist protection. It began as Soviet disinformation within weeks of the war's end and has persisted through purported FBI files and disputed forensic claims.
evidence_for: Stalin publicly floated the idea that Hitler had escaped as early as July 1945, and the surrender of German U-boats U-530 and U-977 in Argentina that same year gave the theory a superficially plausible escape route

The FBI and CIA did investigate numerous reported Hitler sightings in South America for years after the war, producing hundreds of pages of documents that proponents cite as evidence the agencies took the possibility seriously
Argentina was a well-documented postwar haven for numerous actual Nazi war criminals (Eichmann, Mengele), establishing that the escape infrastructure and network genuinely existed for others
Soviet forces, who first controlled the bunker site, gave inconsistent public statements about the remains for years, feeding suspicion about what had really been found
