theories.summary: Reports from DMT users describe consistent shared hallucinations of watching entities or beings, suggesting a collective perceptual phenomenon.
evidence_for: If ideas and memories transfer through Field Consciousness, drugs may increase attunement to this shared consciousness rather than generating hallucinations; users may be perceiving reality through collective human memory instead of their individual senses.

evidence_for.text: Rick Strassman's 1992 landmark research found that roughly half of DMT participants reported encounters with structured beings; subsequent surveys suggest 50–70% of breakthrough-dose users report entity contact.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream researchers including Robin Carhart-Harris conclude that DMT entities are consistent hallucinations arising from the drug's effects on specific brain regions, not contact with external beings.

sources.title: Dimethyltryptamine - Wikipedia
Do You Really Encounter Supernatural Entities When You Take DMT? - Beckley Foundation
Are DMT Entities Real? What Science Actually Says - ScienceInsights

theories.name: Missing Children Forests
theories.summary: A phenomenon reported in Los Angeles County where a disproportionate number of children have disappeared from forests without trace.
evidence_for: Children possess organs providing greater insight into reality; these same organs may enable communication with spiritual entities like Tulpas or forest-dwelling creatures that attract children to disappear into the woods.
theories.research_note: David Paulides' Missing 411 project documents unexplained wilderness disappearances across North American national parks and wilderness areas broadly; the specific LA County forests claim in the summary is not separately documented in found sources.

sources.title: David Paulides - Wikipedia
Official Missing 411 - David Paulides
How 1,600 People Went Missing from Our Public Lands Without a Trace - Outside Online

theories.name: Solipsism
theories.summary: The philosophical view that only one's own mind is certain to exist, and all external reality is merely a manifestation of personal consciousness.
evidence_for: Reality might exist only when humans perceive it; uninhabited Earth locations appear locked in perpetual homeostasis without progress or regression, suggesting places and events are unloaded when unobserved and only instantiated through human perception.

evidence_against.text: Historically no serious philosopher maintained solipsism as a genuine belief; it functions as a thought experiment probing the limits of epistemology, not a practical ontological position.

sources.title: Solipsism - Wikipedia
Solipsism - Britannica

theories.name: Thule
theories.summary: An legendary island referenced in ancient texts that appears never to have existed in observable reality.
evidence_for: Advanced civilizations with time travel technology might exist in the future and move freely along timelines; Thule could be home to one such civilization, existing outside normal spacetime rules, with legends of monsters being descriptions of experimental creatures from their continental science vessel.

evidence_against.text: Modern interpretations of Pytheas' account suggest Thule may have been Norway, Iceland, Shetland, or another northern location; classical scholars regard it as a real but misidentified place rather than a mystical or hidden one.

sources.title: Thule - Wikipedia
Thule - Greek Fictional Island - Mythology.net
The Mysterious Island of Thule in Greek Mythology - GreekReporter

theories.name: Die Glocke
theories.summary: An alleged Nazi superweapon or advanced technology research program from WWII, supposedly confiscated by the United States after the war.
evidence_for: Germany may have discovered something catastrophically important before or during WWII that they couldn't allow public knowledge of; they initiated WWII as a distraction while important people and information were extracted, ensuring governmental collapse would occur after the truth was secured.

evidence_against.text: No physical evidence, wartime documentation, or Allied intelligence report confirming the existence of Die Glocke has ever surfaced; the story originates with Igor Witkowski, who claimed to have seen documents held by an unnamed intelligence contact.
Mainstream historians and reviewers describe Die Glocke as a hoax or wild exaggeration; the device plays no role in any verified WWII German weapons program.

sources.title: Die Glocke (conspiracy theory) - Wikipedia
The Nazi Bell - Die Glocke: Secret Weapon or WWII Myth? - LegalClarity
Die Glocke: The Nazi Bell Hoax - Historic Mysteries

theories.steelman: If real, a confiscated German research program would fit the confirmed pattern of Operation Paperclip, in which the US secretly acquired and covered up the origins of Nazi-era scientific and military research. Unlike Paperclip, no declassified archival documents or physical evidence have corroborated Die Glocke's existence.

theories.name: Arnold's Real Height
theories.summary: Claims that actor Arnold Schwarzenegger's true height has been concealed, with conflicting evidence about his actual stature.
evidence_for: Just as mental processes can alter aspects of people's lives, physical attributes may similarly be malleable, especially through method acting where one's psychology focuses on embodying a different person.

evidence_for.text: Multiple journalists and measurements produced height estimates ranging from 5'6" to 6'3"; Men's Health concluded he was 5'10" rather than his claimed 6'2", and critic Roger Ebert reportedly disputed the official figure.

sources.title: The Ongoing Mystery of Arnold Schwarzenegger's Actual Height - Mental Floss
Arnold Height Truthers Think the Governator Is Telling a Very Tall Tale - MEL Magazine

theories.name: Natural Park Closures
theories.summary: U.S. national parks frequently close despite relatively low maintenance requirements; the true reasons for closures are theorized to be hidden.
evidence_for: In densely explored countries like the USA, large uninhabited areas become rare; if supernatural events occur in specific locations, national parks preserve those grounds for management while preventing permanent settlement, allowing cryptid colonies to exist and grow until removal is necessary.

evidence_against.text: Skeptical Inquirer investigation found that Missing411 disappearance cases are statistically consistent with expected missing-persons rates in wilderness areas; claims of unusual patterns or government obstruction were not substantiated.

sources.title: An Investigation of the Missing411 Conspiracy

theories.name: Lake Baikal
theories.summary: The world's deepest lake, with ancient depictions showing strange aquatic entities; declassified Russian documents describe a mysterious encounter resulting in researchers' deaths.
evidence_for: If extraterrestrials achieved space travel, they might hide on Earth in derelict spacecraft; their carbon signatures would be undetectable among Earth's abundant life, making subaquatic environments ideal hiding places.

evidence_against.text: Managers of the naval base in question state the 1982 diver encounter story is 'absolute nonsense'; the town of Severobaikalsky only formally became a town in 1984, two years after the alleged incident, raising further doubts.

sources.title: Inhuman Monsters of the Deep: The Swimmers of Lake Baikal - Historic Mysteries
Unexplained Objects: Soviet Submarine Witnesses Six Unknown Objects in Lake Baikal (1982) - UFO Report
Lake Baikal UFO Sightings: What Declassified Soviet Files Actually Tell - Hangar1Publishing

theories.name: Dancing Plague
theories.summary: In 1518 France, a crowd of people inexplicably began dancing in the streets and could not stop, resulting in numerous deaths.
evidence_for: If memetic viruses like Virus 23 exist and spread person-to-person, secret organizations would develop and weaponize them; they could engineer viruses targeting specific psychological profiles, allowing containment during development and testing phases.

evidence_for.text: City of Strasbourg chronicles from July-September 1518 document the outbreak; within a month, up to 400 people were dancing uncontrollably in the streets, some reportedly dying from exhaustion.

evidence_against.text: The leading scientific explanation is stress-induced mass hysteria triggered by famine and epidemic disease (smallpox, syphilis), not memetic weaponization or engineered pathogen.

sources.title: Dancing plague of 1518 - Wikipedia
Dancing plague of 1518 - Britannica
What Was the Dancing Plague of 1518? - HISTORY

theories.name: Panspermia
theories.summary: The hypothesis that microorganisms and life seeds exist throughout the universe, eventually developing into full species on suitable planets.
evidence_for: Advanced civilizations may have seeded microorganisms throughout the universe to find habitable planets; once life develops and sentience emerges over millions of years, the dominant species eventually invents space travel and inadvertently signals their existence back to the originators.

evidence_for.text: Pseudo-panspermia — that organic molecules originated in space and were delivered to Earth — is well-supported; microbes have survived simulated space-radiation exposure in laboratory tests supporting the plausibility of lithopanspermia.

sources.title: Panspermia - Wikipedia
Directed panspermia - Wikipedia
Panspermia - EBSCO Research Starters

theories.name: Webdriver Torso
theories.summary: A mysterious YouTube channel posting thousands of videos of colored shapes and sounds, claimed to test algorithmic optimization.
evidence_for: If computers run on exploitable algorithms, human minds may similarly respond to specific algorithmic inputs; seemingly random stimuli could alter cognition significantly, potentially creating sleeper agents or delivering the Despair Code.

evidence_against.text: YouTube officially confirmed in 2014 that Webdriver Torso is Google's internal automated testing account, uploading comparison clips to verify upload quality consistency.

sources.title: Webdriver Torso - Wikipedia
Google and the accidental mystery of Webdriver Torso - Engadget

theories.name: Toxoplasmosis
theories.summary: A parasitic disease more widespread than commonly believed that can alter behavior and psychology, potentially causing mass psychological effects.
evidence_for: Like wild animals forming symbiotic relationships, humans may have merged en masse with neurological or memetic entities granting benefits like self-awareness in exchange for unknown services; if entity numbers are insufficient, some humans lack awareness entirely.

evidence_for.text: T. gondii is correlated in human studies with increased impulsivity, risk-taking behavior, rage disorder, and links to schizophrenia, operating partly via dopamine pathway alteration.

evidence_against.text: The behavioral shifts serve a specific evolutionary purpose (making rodents fearless of cats to complete the parasite's lifecycle) rather than any mass-psychological manipulation agenda.

sources.title: Toxoplasmosis - Wikipedia
Toxoplasma Made Me Do It: How Parasites Influence Behavior - ASM
How a cat parasite can change your personality - Medical News Today
Influence of latent Toxoplasma infection on human personality, physiology and morphology - PubMed (Flegr 2013)

theories.name: Introspection Rundown
theories.summary: A Scientology practice intended to heal body and soul through intense self-examination, with secret procedures that have allegedly resulted in deaths.
evidence_for: If a body becomes too misaligned with its internal sense of self, it may be deemed beyond treatment; continuing such an existence would only torture the soul, preventing enlightenment or ascension, making termination the compassionate option.

evidence_for.text: Lisa McPherson died in 1995 after 17 days under the Introspection Rundown at Scientology's Flag Land Base; autopsy found severe dehydration and a pulmonary embolism consistent with prolonged enforced bed rest and isolation.

sources.title: Introspection Rundown - Wikipedia
Death of Lisa McPherson - Wikipedia
Scientology cult Introspection Rundown in the wrongful death of Lisa McPherson - WikiLeaks

theories.name: USA is an Experiment
theories.summary: The concept that the American nation was designed as an experiment to test whether democracy and freedom could functionally succeed.
evidence_for: The American experiment may test freedom, but U.S. governmental actions consistently violated constitutional principles to preserve the nation; if higher powers oversaw this test, they would have terminated it as failed after these violations—yet America persists, suggesting its hidden purpose transcends testing freedom.

evidence_for.text: Benjamin Franklin's famous 'A republic, if you can keep it' and Lincoln's July 4, 1861 address both explicitly framed American democracy as an experiment of uncertain outcome, indicating the founders themselves used this language.

sources.title: Why Franklin, Washington and Lincoln considered American democracy an experiment - Ohio Capital Journal
Founding Fathers Quotes on Government, Democracy, and Placing Power in the People

theories.name: Online Video Game Monitoring
theories.summary: A theory that governments secretly monitor online multiplayer games to track cultural and societal trends.
evidence_for: Rather than governments making populations fearful through false-flag attacks, game companies may intentionally include violence to desensitize players, reducing societal fear and government control susceptibility—an act working against governmental manipulation.

evidence_for.text: Classified NSA documents leaked by Edward Snowden and reported by ProPublica, The Guardian, and The New York Times in December 2013 confirm CIA and NSA agents operated inside World of Warcraft, Second Life, and Xbox Live to monitor for terrorism-related communications.
A 2008 NSA memo described online gaming as a 'target-rich communications network'; so many intelligence agents infiltrated games that they were reportedly surveilling each other.

sources.title: Spy Games: NSA and CIA Tried to Recruit World of Warcraft and Second Life Players
World of Spycraft: NSA and CIA Spied in Online Games
NSA, CIA target online games, reports say

theories.name: John "Babacombe" Lee
theories.summary: A condemned prisoner whose execution by hanging inexplicably failed three times despite the gallows functioning properly in test runs.
evidence_for: If individuals carry predetermined destinies they must fulfill, reality protects those with incomplete purposes through impossible means; many may be chosen to fulfill similar fates as backups, and if all perish except one, reality itself warps to ensure that person's survival and destiny completion.

evidence_for.text: On 23 February 1885, three separate attempts to hang Lee at Exeter Prison failed because the trapdoor would not open, despite working perfectly in tests without Lee present - a documented historical fact corroborated by multiple prison witnesses.

sources.title: John Babbacombe Lee - Wikipedia
The Weird Story of 'The Man They Couldn't Hang' - Smithsonian Magazine
John 'Babbacombe' Lee - Babbacombe & St Marychurch History Society
The Man They Could Not Hang - Ian Waugh

theories.name: RPG Insanity Training
theories.summary: An alleged conspiracy where the yakuza funded Japanese video game companies to promote anarchic and unstable behavior in players.
evidence_for: Video games may serve as recruitment preludes for crime syndicates by normalizing death and violence, making such concepts familiar and acceptable to potential recruits, rather than simply degrading mental health generally.

theories.name: Oz Factor
theories.summary: A phenomenon describing moments when people feel temporarily disconnected from reality, possibly entering parallel universes or between-world spaces.
evidence_for: In a simulated reality, dissociative events may occur when entering unloaded simulation areas; users could temporarily become undetectable by the simulation or deviate from intended pathways, causing areas to fail to preload, creating strange reality distortions.

evidence_for.text: UFO researcher Jenny Randles coined 'Oz Factor' in 1983 to describe the dissociative state - silence, time distortion, altered perception - commonly reported by close-encounter witnesses; it has become a recognized descriptive term in ufology.

evidence_against.text: Derealization and dissociation are well-documented psychological phenomena; the Oz Factor is more parsimoniously explained as a stress/perceptual response than as evidence for parallel universes or altered physical reality.

sources.title: Oz Factor - Encyclopedia.com
Jenny Randles - Wikipedia
The Oz Factor - HowStuffWorks

theories.name: Shaver's Writings
theories.summary: A theory by Richard Shaver positing that aliens colonized Earth eons ago, establishing underground civilizations whose descendants persist and are responsible for missing persons.
evidence_for: If subterranean aliens feed on missing humans, burial rituals may have originated as appeasement practices—offering corpses to feed these entities rather than living people, representing a form of worship or negotiated coexistence.

sources.title: Richard Sharpe Shaver - Wikipedia
Ray Palmer and the Shaver Mystery - The Pulp

theories.name: Porn Control
theories.summary: A theory that governments deliberately promote pornography and sexualization to keep populations docile and prevent spiritual ascension.
evidence_for: Normalization of eroticism and arousal through ubiquitous sexualization creates addiction to base desires; since ascension requires eliminating such desires, widespread sexual content makes enlightenment nearly impossible to achieve.
theories.research_note: No dedicated source coverage found for a government-run pornography proliferation conspiracy. The theory circulates in online forums but has no Wikipedia article, news investigation, or academic treatment connecting it to documented government programs.

theories.name: Temporary Autonomous Zone
theories.summary: Establishing autonomous zones outside governmental control to circumvent censorship and information suppression.
evidence_for: If governments discovered psychological operations like MK-Ultra and can instill subtle mental changes remotely, deployment devices would be concentrated in dense population centers, explaining why large cities have drastically different viewpoints than rural areas.

evidence_for.text: The TAZ concept has been practically implemented in autonomous zones, rave communities, and protest encampments worldwide since Hakim Bey's 1991 book; the notion of temporary spaces evading state control is documented across multiple political movements.

sources.title: Temporary Autonomous Zone - Wikipedia
T.A.Z.: The Temporary Autonomous Zone - The Anarchist Library

theories.name: Henotheism
theories.summary: A religious framework combining polytheistic and monotheistic elements, where multiple gods are acknowledged but only one is worshipped.
evidence_for: If enlightenment and ascension are achievable, widespread ancient practices may have elevated humans to godhood; Greece and Rome's mythological entities could be ascended humans still existing today, having transcended normal human limitations.

theories.name: Quantum Jumping
theories.summary: A hypothetical phenomenon where individuals can shift consciousness to alternate versions of themselves in parallel timelines through stress or enlightenment.
evidence_for: During near-death experiences, as a survival defense mechanism, consciousness might swap with another timeline's version of oneself better equipped to handle the situation; the life-flashing-before-eyes sensation represents memory integration from this alternate self.

evidence_against.text: Quantum Jumping is a commercial self-help visualization product by Burt Goldman with no support from physics or peer-reviewed psychology. Mindvalley itself notes users need not believe in literal alternate realities for the exercises to work.

sources.title: Quantum Jumping by Burt Goldman - Mindvalley
Quantum Jumping: Burt Goldman's Go-To Guide - MindValley Blog

theories.name: Fulcanelli
theories.summary: A legendary French alchemist reputed to possess extraordinary abilities including elemental transmutation.
evidence_for: If advanced subterranean civilizations exist with superior technology, some may occasionally travel to the surface to share or display their knowledge; loosened travel restrictions in recent centuries could explain the explosion of surface technological advancement.

evidence_for.text: The OSS (precursor to the CIA) reportedly searched for Fulcanelli after WWII, suggesting some institutional interest in his alleged alchemical knowledge; his two published books remain significant texts in esoteric literature.

evidence_against.text: Most researchers conclude Fulcanelli was likely Jean-Julien Champagne or a collective pseudonym; no verified physical transmutation was demonstrated and 'Projection Powder' transmutation claims are unsubstantiated.

sources.title: Fulcanelli - Wikipedia

theories.name: French Viper Releases
theories.summary: An allegation that the French government deliberately released dangerous viper species throughout the country.
evidence_for: Governments may release dangerous species, chemicals, or environmental changes into wilderness areas to make off-grid living impossible, increasing societal dependence on civilization and making autonomous existence infeasible.

theories.name: Grey Goo
theories.summary: A catastrophic scenario where self-replicating nanomachines consume all matter while exponentially multiplying.
evidence_for: Dyson spheres harvest stellar energy at macroscopic scale; similar structures built at subatomic levels could harvest individual atomic energy, and massive production could convert large matter quantities into pure energy, accelerating matter-energy conversion processes.

evidence_for.text: The scenario was coined by nanotechnology pioneer Eric Drexler in Engines of Creation (1986) and is considered a genuine theoretical possibility under known physics.

evidence_against.text: Drexler himself later stated that self-replicating nanomachines are not necessary for molecular manufacturing and that nanotechnology-based fabrication can be inherently safe; uncontrolled replicators are considered highly improbable.

sources.title: Gray goo - Wikipedia
Grey goo - Britannica
Nanotechnology Pioneer Slays Grey Goo Myths - ScienceDaily

theories.name: Mach's Principle
theories.summary: A physics contradiction regarding the theoretical conflict between centrifugal force and gravitational force.
evidence_for: Hollow Earth may be difficult to access because cave systems constantly shift; rather than solid rock, Earth operates in rotating layers with outer crust and inner layers spinning at different rates, periodically opening and closing cave pathways.

theories.name: Pokaini
theories.summary: A Latvian forest region containing massive stacked stone formations that appear to shift position over time.
evidence_for: Governments may conduct timeline-shift experiments, altering past events to change future outcomes; testing grounds require locations and objects capable of surviving extended timescales without substantial change while remaining inconspicuous—like forests with stone formations.

theories.name: Deep Ecology Fascism
theories.summary: An ideology asserting that nature possesses sentience and moral equivalence with humans, requiring legal protection from environmental harm.
evidence_for: If reincarnation exists, once humanity is eradicated, collective human souls inhabit new species, granting them human-level consciousness; humans may not be the first intelligent species on Earth, and future life may inherit our spiritual legacy.

sources.title: Deep ecology - Wikipedia
Deep Ecology - Britannica

theories.name: Deathworm
theories.summary: A legendary Mongolian creature described as a massive worm moving beneath sand and consuming lost travelers.
evidence_for: Ageless entities lacking mortality may develop vastly different instincts; a deathworm immune to aging and dominating its environment has no reproduction necessity for species survival, resulting in extreme rarity—possibly only one individual in existence.

evidence_against.text: In 1983, a specimen of Tartar sand boa (Eryx tataricus) was shown to local witnesses who confirmed it matched what they described; no verifiable specimen or photograph of the actual creature has ever been recovered.

sources.title: Mongolian death worm - Wikipedia
Inside the Decades Long Hunt for the Mongolian Death Worm - Atlas Obscura

theories.name: KKK funded by governments
theories.summary: A theory that the Ku Klux Klan and other white supremacist groups were perpetuated and funded by governments to suppress minority populations.
evidence_for: If governments desire population control, directing larger groups to target minorities through numerical superiority accomplishes reduction; humans can be divided into infinite categories, allowing this process to repeat indefinitely across multiple demographic axes.

evidence_for.text: More than 25% of active KKK members were FBI agents or informants, and FBI paid informant Gary Thomas Rowe participated in KKK violence including the attack on Freedom Riders, with FBI knowledge.

evidence_against.text: COINTELPRO-WHITE HATE (1964-1971) was explicitly designed to disrupt and destroy the KKK, not sustain it; Klan membership fell from ~14,000 in 1964 to ~4,300 in 1971 as a direct result of the program.

sources.title: COINTELPRO - Wikipedia
FBI leadership claimed Bureau was 'almost powerless' against KKK, despite making up one-fifth of its membership - MuckRock
The U.S. Government's Secret War on the KKK - Newsweek
To Ensure Domestic Tranquility: The FBI, COINTELPRO-WHITE HATE - Cambridge Core

theories.steelman: This fits the documented pattern of the FBI infiltrating and directing violent groups under COINTELPRO, including informants embedded in the Klan itself and campaigns that facilitated violence against the Black Panthers. It does not establish direct government funding of the Klan as an institution across its full history.

theories.name: Titanic Didn't Sink
theories.summary: A claim that the Titanic was secretly swapped with the RMS Olympia before its voyage for insurance fraud purposes.
evidence_for: Massive structures like the Titanic fail unusually frequently; an alien or advanced force may prevent humanity from constructing excessively large entities like Dyson spheres, constraining human civilization to Earth by sabotaging super-structure projects.

evidence_against.text: Artifacts recovered from the wreck bear yard construction number 401 (Titanic's build number), not 400 (Olympic's), directly disproving the ship-swap claim.
The insurance payout of £1 million was less than Titanic's £1.5 million construction cost, making the alleged fraud a net financial loss for White Star Line.

sources.title: Titanic conspiracy theories - Wikipedia
The Craziest Titanic Conspiracy Theories - HISTORY
The sinking of the Titanic wasn't a crafty insurance con - The Skeptic

theories.name: Smithsonian Suppression
theories.summary: An allegation that the Smithsonian Institution has destroyed or hidden human remains contradicting modern evolutionary theory.
evidence_for: Many organizations vehemently oppose concepts like giants or deities to preserve the narrative of human superiority; acknowledging advanced predecessors would undermine faith in human organizations and governments, threatening their authority and legitimacy.

evidence_against.text: The originating 2014 claim came from the satirical website World News Daily Report; no Supreme Court ruling on Smithsonian giant skeletons exists, and the alleged AIAA organization making the claims cannot be independently verified.

sources.title: Did the Smithsonian Destroy Thousands of Giant Human Skeletons? - Snopes
Giant human skeletons - Wikipedia
